title: Although they are amazing, I'm now on my 4th pair in 4 years.
body: 1 star because although they are amazing, I'm now on my 4th pair in 4 years.I bought my airpod 2nd gen directly from Apple (not on Amazon). My first pair had the microphones go out, but I didn't realize it until just after the 12 month warranty expired. I just thought Siri sucked and couldn't figure out what I was asking. Had I realized it was a microphone issue in time, I would have been under warranty. However, when I finally realized the microphones fully quit working, they wouldn't replace them (days past warranty). Soon after this the speakers on one side started going out. I had to adjust the iphone sound settings to have less sound go to one ear so they balanced out and then up the volume. At this point I decided I'd get a new pair because I use them all the time and need sound and microphones working.So, I bought a second pair. Fast forward several months and the microphones quit working on this pair. This time I call for warranty asap. They checked things over with me and replaced them. To apple's credit, it was single day shipping and quick turnaround.So, onto my third pair. About 3-4 months in with my third pair, the microphones quit working. WTH. I am so careful with these things and have no clue how or why this could possibly be happening. I was so busy working at the time I waited to contact support (thought the new pair would have typical 1 year functional warranty expectations). Well I was wrong there. Spent a long time with Apple support and learned that the replacements are only covered for the remainder of your original pairs warranty (or 90 days if your previous pair were under that time remaining). So, they only guarantee the replacements will function properly for the time remaining on your previous product warranty? I get that they don't want people seeking a brand new product every year through minor warranty claims, but what if the replacement product legit fails in a short period of time?After being escalated to senior support staff at Apple and some more discussions, they ultimately agreed to make an exception and provide me a warranty replacement pair. Sent them in and they confirmed the issue and replaced quickly. Again, quick turnaround with warranty shipping.So, onto my fourth pair. Everything works great, but it's only been a few days.I love the airpods. They sound great. I prefer earbuds that don't "plug" my ear and these fit my ears well. Only real issues have been those mentioned above. Another issue has been battery life. It's great when they are newer. Normally, they notify you when they are running low several times before powering off. As they get older, I'll get that low battery sound and then they immediately shut down.The airpods have amazing reviews and ratings by thousands, so I'm shocked I can't find anyone else having issues with microphones like I am. They are great when they work, but I've had tough luck getting them to work correctly for more than a year.UPDATE on 4th pair... less than 3 months into the 4th pair and people were occasionally having trouble hearing me in conversations. I isolated the left vs right mic in the settings and figured out the the right airpod microphone is going out (distant soft sound and cutting in and out). Didn't notice earlier because the left still works fine and it must autoselect that mic most of the time. Called apple and looks like I'm still in the 90 day warranty on replaced items (thankfully I caught it in time), so it looks like I may be able to get the right one replaced. Will have to again send the entire case and both airpods into their service center first to have them checked.I put these in my ears. I put them back in the case. I really don't understand how each pair could keep having this issue.

title: Very Disappointed
body: AirPods have always been a complete disaster. I’ve had three pairs of these over the years and they are all share the same trait: ABSOLUTE CRAP. I rarely use them anymore and I will never buy them again or recommend them. They are a complete rip off for the price you are paying. Apple only made the thing for pure profit and nothing else. Do not be fooled by the ads on tv for airpods, they are full of a bunch of bs and make them seem ages better than what they actually are. The build quality is ok. The sound quality however is terrible. The mids are ok but there is one thing that pisses me off, airpods HAVE NO TOP END. None. 0. They sound terrible. They sound like regular old earbuds in the sound quality department. Definitely not $130 “headphones.” If you could even call them headphones. The third problem is the way they fit in your ears. They fit like crap. They never feel like they are truly in the right spot and they constantly move around and shift position. And the sound gets worse every time they move. So you have to fiddle around with them to get them in the right spot to delver the best possible sound. The fourth problem is the way they preform around noise. Even since the first time I got airpods, I only liked using them in a near silent room. If you try to use them where there is a bunch of noise or if you are outside and it’s noisy, you have to jack the volume way up. Making the sound even worse. The more noise you have, the more of the top end disappears. For example, if you are outside and you are on your morning run (which is what I was looking forward to using these for when I first got them) you might as well forget it because all you will hear is the upper mids and treble. And trying to adjust any audio settings to fix that problem makes them sound even worse. The fifth problem is the battery life. The battery life is mediocre. And that is a generous statement. Under normal use (for me anyway), The case needs charging about once a week which isn’t bad. But that actual AirPods are not good. I normally have to charge these about twice a day sometimes even three times. The batteries in them are not meant to last. The more you use these, the less they hold their charge. It’s ridiculous. When I first got them, I could go a whole day without having to charge them and now I Cant go a full day without having to charge them at least twice. It seems to me that Apple designed the battery in these to absolutely kill itself right before they release a new version of airpods. So that way everyone will happily fork over hundreds of dollars to get the next pair of overpriced ShitPods. Yes, you may look cool wearing them. But here is a fact, no one hates airpods more that people who own airpods. If I pay $130, I better be getting some very decent headphones. But no. These are crap. Back in 2012, I bought an iPhone 4s and it came with a pair of standard Apple earbuds. I still own those earbuds. What makes me frustrated is that those earbuds from 2012 sound ages better than the CrapPods. I’d AT LEAST expect the sound quality to be the same if not better than some $15 earbuds from 2012. The bottom line is: do not buy these if you are looking for some actually decent headphones. It will save you a lot of time and money. There are so many better alternatives out there for the money. Don’t get ripped off. Don’t waste your money. Don’t buy airpods. Get something else.

title: Very high effort fake airpods
body: Real airpods are 5 stars, but Amazon is filled with fake airpods, and the ones I received weren't obvious. I saw a review here of someone who had trouble with their new airpods, brought it in to Apple for fixing, and the Apple repair team concluded it was a high quality knockoff. Turns out I'm a victim as well.It's like someone gutted a real pair of airpods, replaced the internals with cheap knockoffs and put it back together. EVERYTHING with my "airpods" was identical and seemed brand new. Plastic wrap was around the oem box (pretty easy to fake nowadays, but still impressive nontheless). Everything was immaculate just like a new box of airpods, OEM feeling cable, etc. The feel, touch, glue, placement of holes and patterns of the airpods seemed identical to my older airpods.1. The biggest clue was audio quality. The audio quality was considerably worse than regular airpods. It was noticeable right off the bat, especially when talking with other people. It was especially obvious since I still have my old gen 2 airpods to compare with.2. Bluetooth was very unstable. Leaving more then 6 feet away from my bluetooth adapter made it go haywire and produced a lot of static. It would also randomly go offsync with the left and right earbuds when listening with both of them on.3. Battery life. They lasted about 2-3 hours before the battery died on them. Not much else to say about this, just way lower battery life than new airpods should have.Interesting thing to note, the Apple limited warranty should NOT be used as a metric to see if it's legit. The first thing I checked was to see if these fake airpods had warranty, which it said I had 1 year starting from the day I paired it with my iphone 9/22/22. I thought this assured me that I had a legit pair of airpods. Once I concluded the new airpods were fake, I did some testing and all I did was put my real old airpods with the new fake airpod charging case, and now my old airpods purchased in 2019 said I have 1 year of warranty starting on 9/22/22.CONCLUSION: BUYER BEWARE, HIGH EFFORT AIRPODS SEEM TO PLAGUE AMAZON. If you want to save ~$25 bucks and roll the dice get these on Amazon. If you don't want to take the risk of dealing with this headache, just buy it directly off Apple.

title: Terrible product support with unrealistic expectations of customers
body: My wife purchased these for me as a gift in August 2020 and they worked great up until about a month ago. At that time, the right ear bud microphone began to fail and would no longer detect sound unless I was yelling. I contacted customer support at Apple and worked through all of the potential resolutions such as updating to the newest iOS (14.6) and confirming that the AirPods were running the most recent firmware. When customer support was not able to resolve the issue they suggested that I send the AirPods in for repair. I received a shipping box and label for FedEx and sent them offer for repair. The AirPods arrived back at my home about 3 days later with a note from the technicians stating that there was nothing wrong with them. I then tested them again on my phone and my wife's phone, and with both phones the right microphone would only detect sound at a loud volume (i.e. yelling) and yet still garbled. Again I contacted customer support at Apple. This time the agent suggested that the next step would be to take the AirPods to the nearest Apple Store so that I could demonstrate the issue for a technician there. Unfortunately the nearest Apple Store is 2.5 hours from my home, though this did not seem to be an inconvenience in the eyes of the agent. At this point, I stated that it did not make sense to drive 5 hours round-trip to repair a $130 device, and I questioned why I could not send them back for repair while identifying the exact issue. The agent agreed to send another shipping box and to include on the repair "ticket" that the issue was with the right microphone. So... (can you guess where this is going?) I received another box, sent the AirPods a 2nd time for repair, and waited. Again, they returned them to me and stated that nothing was wrong with them. And again, the right microphone still does not function correctly. At this point I am left wondering why the "World's Most Valuable Company" would find it acceptable to ask a customer to take 5 hours of his/her time to fix Apple's product, instead of simply sending a replacement pair and keeping the customer happy. Apple's product support has ruined what could be a great product.

title: Worst Apple Product There Is
body: I really want to like them. They're supposed to be effortlessly convenient to pair and use for all your Apple devices while sounding and looking good. But they're not....they're none of that. Let me explain:Sound - it's ok, really. No seal so you hear all the ambient noise around you which is good and bad depending on the situation. Not much for bass extension, treble is flat but clear. Adequate all things considered. If you've used the old design earpods, these are nearly the same sound profile. On a plus, the microphones are very good.Features - very little to say here. They're Bluetooth (5.0) so they pair quickly and have long range, when they work (more on that later).Battery Life - very good, a couple hours at high-ish volume on the pods and half a dozen charges per case charge. More than I need for most situations.Functionality - 100% atrocious and I've had 3 sets of these (shame on me). The case and charging system is so faulty it leads to numerous other problems with pairing and connecting both AirPods at the same time. The contact points in the case and on the pods where they charge easily loses connection, dirties, and later corrodes. Using them for fitness around any sweat or moisture will speed up their inevitable failure. Once this happens, one pod will refuse to charge or stay connected when put back in the case, draining it's battery or trying to reconnect to different devices when in the case. Even if they didn't suffer constant bugs, the pairing with multiple devices is equally buggy, frequently losing connection or trying to connect to other devices in the house for no apparent reason.As far as I can tell, AirPods Pro share the same charging design, so I will listen to my own advice and stay far away from these until the design is fixed. I would advise you do the same.

title: Needs improvement - average sound, very poor bluetooth (worst of any device)
body: Update to original reviewBattery life: is the worst of all devices I have. These are one year old - batter life dropped to 30 minutes.Bluetooth connectivity: you will not find a device that is as bad as these are. I have never struggled with Bluetooth on any device (we originally purchased 3 sets for xmas gifts - everyone hates with a passion the incredibly POOR bluetooth connectivity - you will disconnect, reconnect, disconnect, reconnect - constantly until both the headset and mic work! this is with MAC BOOK PROS (also never buying another one)). You can expect to connect - wait an hour before you ever finds it. Connect - no sound. Connect - microphone does not work. it's a miracle when any part of these massively overpriced headphones work. it's unreal how impossible it is just to get them to work. (again - THREE pairs in the house - everyone hates them.I would strongly say - buy ANYTHING but these. From their horrible tech to their very mediocre sound - what a waste of money. I can say one thing - this is the last poorly designed, poorly implemented tech that I will ever buy from Apple again. Such complete garbage.To be fair - buy them. when you see it's a nightmare to connect them and get both the mic and sound working - come back and comment. Apple has this ego trip where everyone in the world must align to them. if they don't - they refuse to work. That might sound good in the heads of the engineers at Apple - but us consumers simply say - never buy those horrible apple products again. Instead - we buy devices where the many fine companies in the world try to earn your business vs Apple's - you need to please us policy.Amazon has a wonderful CEO - we are big fans. if he happens to read this review - PLEASE! Create a pair of ear pods so we can buy them!

title: Bad product and bad customer service
body: I’ve been having connectivity issues with my AirPods for about 4 months now and I contacted them 2 months ago, I worked with apple support from the beginning of me realizing it was an issue— all the way from restarting network settings to sending my AirPods in. They obviously fixed the issue when I sent them in, as the issue went away once the AirPods were returned to me. To my surprise they stopped working within a week or two so I contacted them… again. This time to no avail, “Raiza” said it was my phone and asked for me to try connecting AirPods to other devices, as they “never found faulty issues when the technician worked with my AirPods to try to fix them” (why was this never even communicated with me anyways, til I contacted them? Communication issues?) Mind you, I don’t have other devices to try AirPods with, but I do have a JBL speaker I always connect to my phone with, and I never have connectivity issues with it, so I know for a fact the issue isn’t my phone! Apple support really has trust issues, and don’t take their customer’s concerns seriously or professional because it wouldn’t be the case that she kept insisting it was my phones network settings and was also told her boss would tell me the same thing. She then proceeded to end session as I was typing. I’m astounded at apple customer service. Maybe it’s a sign apple needs to spend more time either training or paying their employers more. Or charge their customers more for the warranty? Next time, I’ll remember to keep getting my devices through T-Mobile, they are much more helpful when it comes to warranty and have never had issues replacing my damaged apple devices throughout the 7 years with them; plus—no delays and no rhetorical questions! Thank you for stealing my money, Apple.

title: The most overrated earbuds on Earth
body: Decided to see what the hype was all about and bought Airpods. While they are admittedly aesthetically pleasing and pair nicely with Apple products, I just don't see what the hype is about. They're incredibly uncomfortable and fall out of my ears whenever I walk or laugh (and yes, I have tried orienting them differently in my ears to no avail). If they're not falling out, they feel incredibly loose and like they are about to fall out. I looked online to see if other people were experiencing this and how to fix it, and found some articles about how airpod covers are available to allow the earbuds to stay in place. For the price I'm paying for these earbuds, why do I need to buy another $13-20 product to make the $135 product actually possible to wear? Absolutely ridiculous. Why should anyone have to exert additional effort to actually make the product functional? After messing around with the Airpods, I finally gave up and put on my ($10 ) earbuds that have the same sound quality and infinite more comfort than Airpods. I really do believe that Apple is the only company on this planet that can get away with its product being designed so poorly that methods to make their product wearable have to be created. I also noticed that switching the Airpods from my Macbook Air to my iPhone XR was a hassle. I would have to tap the option to forget my Airpods on my iPhone and then reconnect them after using them with my Macbook Air. Finally, I know people who swear by these earbuds. They work well with other Apple products and look good on the wearer, but I don't think that these reasons justify the price tag or all the other cons I've listed above.

title: We have been forever changed for the worst.
body: I purchased the second generation iPods a few weeks ago. They would not stay in my ears, it didn’t matter if I was sitting completely still, walking slowly down the stairs or down the block, every time I moved my head the slightest bit they came out. So, I read every article I could find to fix this problem. Tried almost all of them. Even the ones that Apple recommended not to try. Nothing helped. Today I had a great experience with them. I went on a long slow walk and they, of course, fell out immediately. I kept putting them back in. Then, when the left pod fell out it tumbled down into a sewer drain. I continued my walk without any music and really enjoyed myself. I realized that as a society, we have forgotten the difference between inside and outside. From people talking on their phones while pushing a baby carriage in the middle of a crowded parking lot, talking or reading texts while crossing the street, to my favorite, talking loudly in an elevator, we all seem to want/expect the same experiences both inside and outside. Those of us who have/had young children, remember talking to them about using their inside voice and outside voice. There is a difference and a reason we said that!! It’s very simple. Inside and outside are DIFFERENT!! There was a time when you could tell someone was an idiot, they stopped at the bottom of an escalator and started having a conversation. Now we have completely lost our minds. By the way, I truly appreciate that new technology has changed our lives for the better. But, It is our responsibility to respect others, realize boundaries and not be morons.

title: Falls out of ears easily
body: I got these for free directly from Apple and that's the only reason I have them. I knew I'd never buy these -- just from the looks of the all hard plastic design I knew they would be a poor fit, especially being that there is no silicone tip to stay gripped in the ears and to provide a good seal for the best sound possible. What I feared is exactly what I experienced when I got to use these.It integrates nicely with other apple devices, and is also able to connect to non-apple devices as well. Sound is good with initial ear-seating, but it quickly loses seal and starts to fall out -- this is when I'm just sitting still. What's the point of having great technology if it cannot be used? Also, what's with the lightning port instead of the standard USB when Apple has so much concern over plastic waste? Side note : The whole trend of eliminating headphone jacks and forcing customers to purchase wireless headphones and/or accessories to use existing headphones already owned by customers fully contradicts Apple's advertised concern for waste reduction. All it does is it increases Apple's profit with our money and takes away our choices. Also now you have copycats like Samsung and others doing the same -- end of side note.I guess since it has Apple's name, too many people blindly rate 5 stars no matter what. I'll give them 5/5 stars for their marketing strategies. The price is just plain funny but I guess that is the new normal. The airpod pros are the practical choice if one was deciding on only airpods, but at that price it's a bit too much considering other available options.

title: Amazing Sound Quality but Some Cons
body: Update: I've had these for six months and the case has stopped charging. They are essentially useless now. I would recommend purchasing through Apple so you can get another pair if yours malfunctions. Since I purchased on here and the return window is closed, I cannot get another pair, which means $130 down the drain.This was definitely one of my best Amazon purchases. The sound quality on these is amazing even during phone calls. The charging case holds an impressive amount of charge so I only need to recharge it once every 2-3 days, which isn't very much considering I use these 8-10 hours a day.at work. That being said, there are some cons to these. The battery life of the AirPods themselves isn't the best-- you'll get 1-2 hours max. Now, five minutes in the charging case will get these back to full charge, so take that with a grain of salt. The biggest con for me has been the Bluetooth connectivity. I have an IPhone 11 so I would expect these to have no issues with connecting. However, every few times I use these, one or both of the AirPods does not connect when I pull it out of the case. It usually takes about five minutes to figure out the problem and re-connect them. It's definitely a problem when I'm trying to pick up a call on my AirPods or listen to something quickly. For that reason, I'd give these 4/5 stars. They're still worth the purchase but I'd recommend adding AppleCare so you can get a replacement if you have the same issues. (P.S. If you have an Apple watch, these do not connect independently to the Apple Watch, you will need to have your IPhone on you.)

title: My Airpods were very expensive
body: After having these bad little MFs for less than one week, I suffered an unfortunate bout of being relieved of my very possession of said precious subjects. I was working with them in my pocket & they must've fallen out & when some bottomfeeder happened upon them, his day was made & mine was ruined. If there's a God in heaven, someone very near & dear to whoever discovered their unanticipated treasure today is suffering through the final hours of their soon-to-be-over life as I type out this review & then at least whatever pleasure they may have experienced ultimately at my expense via my lost electronics accessories will have thereby been deemed short-lived. As much as I shudder to think my actions would ever bring pain or sadness to the heart or life of another, the only greater fear I hold within the privacy of my own consciousness is that of ever learning that my absent mindedness had a role in someone's effortless victory or circumstancial good fortune all while netting zero benefit directly to myself. To neglect one's self in such a way would be akin to betraying my own mother as she loves me dearly. In my eternal pledge to honor her without limitation or condition, protecting my self-interest is in effect a conscious act in treasuring her. Just for the sake of clarity, when it comes to my commitment to my mother as things pertain to honor & such, there is no task too great nor feat too challenging provided that the conditions are convenient & the terms favorably reasonable. My mother did hit me one time...we forgive, but we don't forget...let's not be foolish here...

title: Left Airpod Didn't Work
body: This is one of the purchases where everything that could go wrong did go wrong...The first time I purchased the Airpods with the additional AppleCare, I waited anxiously for them to arrive. I happened to be at home on the expected day of delivery so I was surprised when I saw the delivery status 'Customer Refused Delivery.' Nope. That definitely didn't happen. I called into Amazon's customer service, who told me, "Disregard that status message. They are Airpods - they probably got stolen. Things can go missing between the warehouse and delivery." Uh, ok. They refunded both the Airpods and the AppleCare, as (THIS IS IMPORTANT) the AppleCare, though sold separately, is connected to the device via the serial number when purchased.Cut to my second attempt at purchasing the Airpods and the AppleCare... I received the Airpods, and like so many others who have purchased them and left reviews, the left Airpod was defective. I returned the Airpods and received a refund with no issue. However, when I inquired with customer service about refunding the AppleCare (because as I mentioned, when purchased, it is associated with the Airpods via serial number), I was told that a one time exception had already been made for me and they couldn't refund it again. So, because of what was an issue on Amazon's end initially, I'm now screwed out $29 for the AppleCare purchase when I was sent defective Airpods.If you are willing to buy these Airpods after so many reviewers, including myself, provide feedback stating this product is defective, don't waste your money on the AppleCare.

title: Don’t get fulled by the price.
body: I had first generation AirPods and they lasted almost two years. I was planning to buy new pair but I wasn’t in the rush because they still worked, sound was decent and battery would last me 2-3 hours before I had to put them on the charger. Somehow I misplaced them so instead of looking for them, I decided to buy a pair on Amazon. They were cheaper than Apple store so I thought I was getting good deal, NOT! First of all, sound is really bad, there is static in the background and I’m hearing an echo of some unknown music (not what I’m listening). Last drop was battery life. First time it happened I thought that my daughter might have used my AirPods and drained my battery. This time I fully charged them, charging station and AirPods were 100% charged. I placed them in my purse where they will be safe, and not borrowed by teenager. They were sitting in my purse for 4-5 days, not being used at all so they should be fully charged. Well the light was green for a few seconds, then it changed to orange. Pods were not connecting to my phone even though I tried to connect them by holding button in the back of charging station. I even tried to forget device on my phone, then reconnect, but at that point orange light on the charging station disappeared, and that happens only if they are completely drained, dead. Today they will be going back to Amazon. I don’t want another pair, I want full refund, and I’ll be buying a new pair from good old Apple store.

title: Authentic Product, but Wildly Overrated
body: If I hadn't authenticated the serial number, I'd seriously think I was sold a counterfeit product. Since this is an authentic, new product, I'm just left to believe the product is mediocre at best.I regularly use my air pods on both iOS and Android devices. The air pods connect relatively quickly and easily to both, but especially with other Apple devices. (For some reason, the max volume is significantly lower on my Android device.) The Bluetooth range is not great with reception starting to break up if you move more than 4-5' away from the phone. The sound is pretty clear, but less than 10 weeks after purchase, individual ear buds will cut in and out or get static-y even while sitting stationary next to my phone. Like other reviewers, my individual ear buds don't seem to charge evenly with one often dying well before the second. I've also been disappointed with the battery performance. The ear buds re-charge quickly when inserted into the case and I'd say I can probably get 5 hours of run time between charges of the *case*, but I struggle to regularly get more than 2 hours of run time per full charge of the ear buds (and oftentimes not even that). Finally, I have no idea how people wear these all the time as I found the shape of the air pods to be tremendously uncomfortable. (Though I am aware accessories exist to mitigate this issue)I truly don't understand the effuse praise heaped upon air pods. I am deeply underwhelmed.

title: They don't work!
body: I received the AirPods today and they paired to my phone as expected. I used them for about 30 minutes and then charged them (they still had a charge of about 60%). I opened the case next to my iPhone and the phone showed them connected and fully charged. I then took out the AirPods and placed them in my ears and started an audiobook. The sound came through the speakers in my phone and the AirPods weren't working. I opened settings on my phone and did everything I could find online to pair them after unpairing as the phone required me to do because it couldn't connect. I went to "get product support" on the order page and spoke with customer service who walked me through the same steps I already took to try and pair the AirPods. I could not get them to pair. My wife's AirPods paired fine with my phone so I ruled out a problem with my phone. Amazon customer service connected me with Apple customer service and they are replacing the AirPods, however, I have a $215 credit authorization on my credit card pending their reception of the defective AirPods which must not be damaged. I don't think this is the norm for Apple products but sometimes you get a bad product. I think Amazon should have sent me a new Pair with case and taken the defective ones as any local store would have done. Amazon shouldn't have referred me to Apple for replacement. The lesson I learned is that you don't ever click on "get product support" but you do click on "return and replace".

title: Both sets died in 1.5 years
body: Let me start by saying our family buys lots of Apple products. Add that with the fact that having wireless earbuds are so convenient and Apple appears to makes a slick product. The airpods fit fine for me and my spouse. The case is also very small, but the earbuds could have smaller stems. The sound quality is good, but not excellent. However, both sets we purchased had one earbud die within 1.5 years. I talked with a friend and they said the same thing happened to them. Combine that with some negatives such as the high price, average battery life, the fact that these aren’t noise canceling, the annoyance that having a family sharing apple account combines all earbuds into everyones devices causing a lot of accidental switching of airplay, and you have a very expensive recurring cost per year per person. I thought about how a warranty might help, but you still have to pay for warranty plus the replacement parts and can only replace 2 parts. Buying a new pair would have ended up being the same cost for us or even cheaper with the airpods on sale. Maybe the gen 3’s will be more reliable but I’m not paying almost $200 to find out. My advice for most people, buy the off brands that come with an 18 month warranty and more features like double the battery life and controls. They will run as low as $30 and will probably be good enough. You can reward yourself with a music subscription using the money you’ll save.

title: Been looking forward to these for years. They only work with phone calls
body: I mean honestly, I expected them to be exactly like the ones that came with all the previous generations of iPod and Apple iPhone only with no wires… I feel like there’s a slight variation in how powerful they are meaning less powerful than the ones with the wires but more importantly yeah mine just kind of stopped working. Have them for less than two weeks they make the Connect and disconnect noises however, the only thing that actually works perfectly is when I use them for telephone calls. And when I’m on telephone calls, in the event I would like to listen to media or music or watch YouTube while I’m on a phone call, they will work perfectly the music media or YouTube will turn down and I can hear the person perfectly. The second person hangs up the AirPods then go back to not working at all that’s right I said it folks other than the connect disconnect the sound they do not work at all. Of course, other than the aforementioned phone calls which they work as they are designed to. It’s extremely frustrating I have actually had the opportunity to get a second pair sent to me from Amazon and they work just the same. I can’t find anything online about what to do my iPhone is brand new so I don’t know why my phones doing this but no one can give me a good answer and frankly I don’t want to anymore because I just wasted a bunch of money on and they don’t work for anything.

title: First and second sets UNACCEPTABLE.
body: After a week the AirPods kept disconnecting from my iPhone 7. I had to have the device leave Bluetooth and completely restart. Before that, the left side would play music but it would not respond to the double tap nor to the motion to automatically start the AirPods/turn Them off when taken out of the ear. I will return and re-order, give it another try since I really like them aside from this issue.Update receiving my replacement AirPods: I want to start by saying I am fairly text savvy and know how to work with my iPhone having used other Bluetooth devices with it. This new set of AirPods still has issues with consistency: it can take forever for the AirPods to link up with my phone. The tap modes such as pause and fast forward work infrequently. It is hard to believe that there is a single digit percentage of problems like these on all of the reviews that I see, because this is my SECOND pair and I am still having issues. I can get by without having to use the double tap modes, I can just use my phone, but the delayed wireless hook up to the phone is just unacceptable. Since I have tried other brands of wireless these seem to have the best sound and are the most comfortable. But if you don’t mind using a different wireless buds that stick directly in your ear, then skip apple and get those.So disappointed but keeping them anyway.

title: BEWARE! PLEASE READ MORE. Don’t buy Apple products through Amazon
body: It’s a pain to get Apple service if you bought these through Amazon and bumped into problems like I did.So I purchased these on Prime Day (10/13). As soon as 10/17 the left pod started having problems. It didn’t hold charge even was left overnight. Called Amazon support, was told to contact Apple directly.Long story short, Apple first suggested to send me replacement for left pod and put a hold on my card of $73.83 for non-return fee (in case I didn’t return the defected AirPod) — reasonable. Then I received the new left pod, Apple didn’t include the return label and just told me to discard the defected pod — which meant I just bought a brand new left pod and WTF!!!Apple explained that since I didn’t buy these AirPods directly from them, the limited warranty didn’t apply to my purchase. Woaaaa!!!!Amazon didn’t make AirPods right folks?After a week of frustration calling Apple to follow up (while using the defected pod, I didn’t wanna break the seal of the new pod unless I knew exactly how this issue gonna land), Apple agreed to reserve the charge on my credit card for the stupid replacement.Amazon Associate was nice enough to follow up w me about this case. But the contract whatsoever between Amazon and Apple was a mess and their customers were the one who suffered.

title: Not sure what's happening here but...these are not that great.
body: So this is my second pair of Airpods and I'm actually pretty disappointed in the overall quality. I don't see anything about them being refurbished but they certainly do not perform like my old Airpods I got directly from Apple. At first I thought I didn't think much of it because I've only had them for one month but these Airpods static and click off REGULARLY but the phone is still registering them as on and the videos/podcasts/music keeps playing but I can't hear anything. I have to take them off and put them in the case and take them back out for them to reconnect and I can hear just fine. Worse when it happens on phone calls and FTs because they're still connected with no sound. They do this several times a day. Not only that, sometimes when I take them out of the case and they are supposed to "automatically connect," sometimes they do, sometimes they don't. Sometimes only one does and the only thing that fixes it is me taking both of them in and out of the case several times. Sometimes they're connected straight of of the case with no sound. Unfortunately my return window closed about 11 days ago which infuriates me because I would like a replacement. Again, I have just barely had these for a month. As a realtor and a mom, I NEED to have free hands. So disappointing and frustrating.

title: Don't waste your money on these, and don't buy from this seller
body: What an absolute waste of money.I got these airbuds because of their shape--it's about the only shape that fits in my ear and stays. I was also excited about the functionality of the earbuds. However, the earbuds were never fully functional. I was never able to get the earbuds to switch through songs, for example. They did pause and play whatever I was listening to, but they most often did this when I would accidentally touch the earbuds. Or my hair grazed the earbuds. So, really they were not very functional at all anyway. But I could use them to listen to music, and I'm really not super picky, so I just kept using them.About two months in, one of the earbuds fell onto a hardwood floor and broke open. I was able to kind of stick it back together, but it never worked after that. Within days, the magnetic metal bottom of both earbuds fell off..At this point I contacted the seller and told them the issues. They said they would send a new pair. I waited weeks. Still waiting, months after my purchase, for any kind of communication. I had to escalate my complaints through amazon, but the best they could do was refund half of the price.DON'T BUY THESE.And if you really want to, at least don't buy these from this seller.

title: Amazon DO BETTER!! WTF
body: I have bought 2 pairs of these same airpods in the past. I've never had an issue. I saw they were only $89 on Prime Day this year so I figured I snag a pair for my son. When i ordered they were back ordered and wouldn't arrive for 3 weeks. No big deal. I wasn't in any rush. Well, I received them a few days ago and went to use them and connect them to my sons phone and the airpods wouldn't turn on when you press the button. Which I thought was weird because when they are NEW they will automatically turn on. They may not be 100% charged but they'll turn on. These did NOT. So I plugged them in and instantly they tried to connect to my phone and said something along the lines of if these are not your airpods do not connect and they were already titled 'AIRPODS DE KEVIN'. Both airpods and the case were at 1%. So I begin to inspect them. They were dirty and full of lint. I didn't even dare put them in my ears and see if they worked. What the hell AMAZON! No where in the description for your Prime Day Deal did it state they were used. Also with the fact that they were USED they didn't even have warranty. Wow! Down right wrong and absolutely disgusting! I am returning and won't be buying anything electronic from you anymore! I am disappointed and 1000% pissed off!

title: Were great, until they suddenly stopped working
body: Had the airpods for 4 months and they were amazing. Sound quality was great, felt very comfortable, the whole nine yards. I loved these earbuds. After 4 months, without warning it just stopped working. It connects to my Samsung phone, but it doesn't play any sound. Ok weird, I'll just put it back in the case and take it out again (disconnect then reconnect). Still the same issue. Try to unpair the earbuds and pair them back and still nothing. Put the earbuds in the case and held the button to reset it until it gets that amber light and still nothing. I then thought ok maybe it's because I'm not using an iPhone, so I get my old IPhone 6 and try to connect it to that and see if it fixes it and what do ya know nothing. I kept resetting it so many times and I finally got one earbud to play sound. Right earbud plays sound and left one doesn't, ok cool progress. I tried resetting it a couple more times and now the left one plays sound but the right one doesn't. Continued resetting it and now the right earbud plays sound but the left one doesn't. It sucks, but at least I can still use one earbud. I'll continue to try and see if I can get both ear buds to work and if I do I'll update this review, but until then It's a 1 star for me :/

title: Sound is Great. They Really Hurt & Fall Out Often!
body: Disappointed. I wanted to love these AirPods. The sound is perfect. Zero complaints with the sound quality. However, these really hurt. They should not cause you pain. When I say hurt, it is truly painful! This is not a mild discomfort. I tried to ignore it the first day, but after a few tries it is miserable. I tried barely placing them in my ear and adjusting it, it relieved some of the pain pressure but it still hurt. They keep falling out too!! So if you adjust it to barely go into your ear, hopefully you are not moving around and working out because these will fall out repeatedly disrupting your workout. I am so surprised at how badly these AirPods have turned out to be. They are expensive! If I had known, I never would have spent this much money. I saw others using them and just thought they would be great. THEY CAUSE PAIN AND FALL OUT REPEATEDLY. You would think that someone could design something better. I need to find something better! No sound is worth all of this suffering. My ears and head hurt hours after I remove these. Yes, it causes headaches. It’s so sad, because they have terrific sound and they look cool too. This is a BIG disappointment. My advice: Do NOT waste your money.

title: One airpod works and only one will connect at a time
body: I bought these as a graduation present for when I graduated college and I won't be buying airpods again. Initially when I bought them they worked fine for 5 months, outside of the return window of course. When I started having issues, such as only one working at a time or both just plain not playing audio, I took them to the apple store to see what's up. They took the airpods and returned them to me three days later claiming that the left airpod wasn't connecting. The moment they came in the mail I tried them on and played a song. And what a surprise, only the right airpod was playing and with no change in my problem. So I took them back, where apple told me that that both airpods connect and that they can't do anything unless they fail to connect. Wonderful news being essentially nothing could be done, especially when they already have my money for a jank/faulty product. They directed me to discuss with amazon about the issue. It falls outside of the return window so whatever, and none of the troubleshooting options provided worked. Don't buy, because if you run into issues similar to mine you're SOL. I will be investing my money in other headphones, not apple brand. All in all, these suck.

title: Range Sucks
body: Let me start this review by saying that I'm using these pods with a Blackberry Priv that has an Android OS, so take that FWIW. Maybe they aren't compatible, or optimally compatible, or whatever since they're an Apple device. I don't know, but the point of having them is to be able to listen to my phone without wires, and they don't allow that very well.The range is limited, like less than a few feet, and depending on where I'm using them it's less than that. For example, I can be at the gym on an incline bench, set my phone on the floor next to me, and the podcast that I'm listening to cuts out. Maybe there's interference from the gym's piped in music - they work well enough when they're about 10 inches away from me in the rack on the elliptical machine.This afternoon I was washing my car in my garage. When I was washing the tires I left the phone on the hood of the car, and when I went to wash the rear tires, my podcast cut out. I ended up turning them off them and using the speaker in the phone.The only time these pods really work is when I'm driving and I have my phone on my lap. YMMV depending on how, when, and where you use them.

title: Planned Obsolescence. Evil Apple
body: I never liked the idea of Apple getting rid of the headphone jack but never knew why.Now I know. It wasn't to make space for more tech in the phone. It was to boost the sales of Bluetooth headphones. And it worked. Apple made a lot of money.I got mine and I really liked them. I also have 100 dollar wired earphones. I've had them both for about 3 years. The airpods no longer have a 4 hour battery. It has a 1 one hour battery per charge. I called apple to ask about getting a replacement. They said it was 70 bucks per airpod. I said, I might as well buy a new pair.That's when it hit me. Apple is making money with the increase in sales for Bluetooth headphones but ALSO insuring that people will come back to buy more when the battery no longer holds a charge. They know this. I don't know about you, but if I pay over 100 dollars for some headphones. I want it to last more than 3 years. My wired earphones work like the day I bought them.This is diabolically evil of APPLE.I AM TELLING YOU THIS AS A WARNING. IF YOU BUY BLUETOOTH HEADOHONES, BE PREPARED TO BUY A NEW SET IN 2-3 YEARS BECAUSE THE BATTERY WILL INEVITABLY DIE.

title: One irritation after another
body: This product has been nothing but trouble. First, the right earpiece is louder than the left, and there is no way to adjust this. At least I don't THINK there is: It came without any directions at all. A great deal of time on the internet found no solution. There are no actual buttons, it is "touch" control, which means any time you adjust its position in your ear it turns itself off, or the volume changes, or it pauses whatever your listening to. Lately, the left earpiece turns itself off spontaneously, without being touched, after a few minutes. If you can manage to communicate that you want it turn back on, it will turn back on for - sometimes - up to an entire minute. If the two earpieces become unsynced, the only way to get them both back is to stop what you're doing, place them back in the case, and remove them again. And no, they are fully charged. It's not just that the device is fractious. It is designed poorly, since it is not obvious how to control it through various touch patterns and again, there were NO DIRECTIONS included. (!!) Maybe I got a bad set, but my advice is to look elsewhere for a more utile design.

title: Fall out of my ears constantly, other problems
body: 1 StarI don't think these are genuine Apple Air Pods. People I know who have them don't have any of the problems I've had. See below.• Frequently can’t perform functions asked through Siri, just says “working on it” then drops. Ironically none of my non-apple earpieces have any trouble, ever, only Apple EarPods fail, often so I think they are knock offs.• unless you put them back in their case when you’re done using them they will continue to answer your phone, sometimes mid-call when you aren’t wearing them. You can go to settings to turn them off, but every single other earpiece or headphone I've ever owned disconnects when you take them off.• they are worthless if you are doing anything other than sitting still in one place. They fall out of my ears constantly and if I don’t have music playing at the time I don’t always notice and have to search for them. The newer version may be better, this version is a ridiculous, inexcusable design flaw.•Since you can only recharge them or pair them with a devise when they’re in their case if you misplace the case you’re out of luck and buying a new one is not cheap.

title: Not recommended for Android phones
body: These airpods are light, comfortable and generally produced and received well for calls. Overall though, I would not recommend for Android phones (despite what other reviews tell you).I'd searched ahead of time and plenty of Android-phone users seemed to have good experiences despite this being an Apple product (which obviously works better with other Apple products). I wanted to try them because of the lightweight comfortable fit that many people had raved about, long battery life, as well as the tap functions. I didn't realize how annoying the bugs for Android would be though, even after forcing a firmware update the main issues consistently recur:1. Slipping out of sync (between left and right, sometimes more and more out of sync, if I don't pause and replay, or unsync and re-pair)2. Audio cut-outs - usually not if you have uninterrupted line-of-sight, but if my phone is in my pocket or bag, then it was guaranteed to cut out.Between backorder delay, and delay to return due to trying to troubleshoot the issues with re-pairing and firmware updating, I'm unable to do a return now :(

title: Cheap design with premium features (in other words fancy garbage)
body: At first these headphones were great. Then the new wore off. I found that they were getting quieter and didn’t block out ANY ambient noise which for me was a huge turn off because I use music as an escape from the screaming of my family and shop environment. Secondly about 5 months in the left side speaker blew. Now for most people they wouldn’t be bothered but for me I’m kinda mad because i spent 150 dollars of my good money for this garbage that doesn’t sound good, and broke way before it’s duty was over. The fitment was also a big turn off, they would constantly fall out of my ears and were uncomfortable to wear. Then the bane of their existence, the case would load up with so much metal filings that it wouldn’t be able to shut and charge the dang things. I’d have to clean it out with alcohol once a week just so they would charge. The only thing I liked about these was, how they looked, how they read texts to you, how they paired with your phone seamlessly and the sound they made when they hit the bottom of the garbage can. Avoid at all costs!!! They’re a cheap headphone with premium features that’s why they’re so expensive.

title: FAKE...NOT REAL
body: I purchased airpods (Gen2) for full price in April 2020 with the additional AppleCare protection plan. I am writing this in March 2021. Around Christmas time, these stopped working. Because of the holiday season and having other earbuds I didn't bother going into an AppleStore to get them looked at.The Apple employee told me that the serial number on the case is not the same as the serial number he is getting from the earbuds. That the case also does not feel like it is the same apple quality. The lid is lighter than what you find in the Apple Stores.I looked at my Amazon invoice from my order history and found the serial number on that and plugged it into checkcoverage.apple.com. It was not listed. I plugged in the number on the bottom of my case, (it was different), this one came up and everything was expired.I FEEL HORRIBLY CONNED. Looking at the amazon page it doesn't say you are buying from Apple. But it sure looks like it. They worked fine for the time I used them but if they are NOT Apple and the warranty is a SCAM, then why pay $125 something dollars for them. WE ARE GETTING RIPPED OFF BY FAKES.

title: The case charges but the earbuds do not.
body: I was please when these arrived the same day I ordered them, and I opened the beautiful box and elegant packaging and plugged the case into the same charging adapter I have been using for my previous set for a couple of years. I had lost one of those buds, otherwise I'd still be using them. I charged these for several hours, and when I opened top of the case, there was a flashing white light. I'd never seen this before. So I plugged them into my iMac, and checked a few hours later and still nothing. So I plugged them into the the adapter I plug my iphone into, and checked back a few hours later. Still a flashing white light. So I tried pairing them with my iphone, which recognizes them, but shows them only 3% charged. I would think they'd come from the factory with more of a charge than that.Next I took my old airpod, which needed charging, and put it in the case, and the light turned orange, it was charging. So I'm returning these and asking for a replacement, hoping for better luck next time. I've never had a problem with an Apple product before, so I'm assuming this is just an abberation.

title: Do yourself a favor just buy them locally
body: Got these to replace a first gen AirPod and they came in quickly but that’s where the pleasant experience ended. Trying to connect was a nightmare and when they finally got connected they would not stay connected for more than 10 seconds at a time. After talking to Amazon support and Apple support for hours it was finally determined that this had been sitting on a shelf for a long time because the firmware was out of date and because they wouldn’t stay connected to the phone they could not be updated. Fine I guess I need to exchange them on Amazon but apparently you can’t do that, you have to return them and wait for a credit and then buy them again. Oh did I mention when returning them you can’t just drop them off at Kohls like other things.. no that would be too easy, you have to have ups pick them up and an adult has to be home at the time of pickup so I am actually having to take a day off of work to send these back. I usually buy everything on Amazon but this experience will definitely have me thinking twice next time.

title: Apple won't help if u have a problem.
body: let me start with there are 5 people in my house. Which all have apple phone, I pads and air pods. I got my daughter air pods for Christmas 2019. Since the left ear bud stopped working and the case has metal fragments in it. Cleary a defective case. I called customer support and was given 2 options. Take it to a apple store or best buy. All which are closed in PA due to Covid 19. Or pay a refundable deposit of 59.00 for the ear bud and 69.00 for the case. I don't feel like I should have to make that large of a deposit since they were 99.00 new. I asked for a manager. Who also said that is all I can offer you at this point. This burned mybutt, since I have spent a lot of money on Apple products over the years. May I say I my self have switched to Samsung. At this point I'm tempted to switch my entire family too. You can be loyal like a dog. But you will be beat with the news paper for sure. They won't dare go out of there way to help you. I'm so over the BS with the customer service dept

title: It is impossible to fathom that Apple can't figure out bluetooth for Windows PC
body: Airpods from Apple work seamlessly, perfectly fine with an Iphone. However, they keep disconnecting, at times even during the calls on Zoom or Teams. Even connecting it to Bluetooth is so troublesome that I have to restart the system to get it to work. I am not the only person who has faced this issue; I have checked with four friends of mine, who also have faced the same problem.Either Windows or Apple is deliberately screwing up the system to not allow this thing to work seamlessly with Windows. Overall, it is impossible to fathom that Apple can't figure out bluetooth for Windows PC.There is no doubt that it took decades for other firms, and yet they couldn't figure out the shape of the ears and design a comfortable handsfree for human ear. The airpod design even from the wired stage, is the first and even now as well, the only one which fits comfortably in the human ear without causing a feling of pluggedness.It is a pity that I had to return this product.

title: These are all defective
body: Ever wonder why apple AirPods are 50% off on amazon and not in the apple store? Thought it was a good deal because I sure did.Turns out apple empties their defective inventory on amazon at discounts. In about a month and barely any usage they stopped working. I never dropped or scratched them. Never let the battery ever hit zero. One day I go to use em and one AirPod isn’t working. Has no better while the other did. And I use both together. The case had battery. Then I put both airpods in the case and plug to charge. Now they have fully stopped working.The AirPods are not fake btw. When you connect, Apple Music offers you 6 months free pricing the legitimacy. They are simply defective. I’m gonna take them to the App Store, invoke the warranty and get a new pair that isn’t defective. If that doesn’t work I’ll chase Amazon for a refund.For reference, I’ve had cheap $30 Bluetooth airpod rip offs from China that have been working for 2+ years with no problems.

title: Genuine Apple product but manufacture defect on arrival
body: Got it last night, the package is original but the pod for right ear has no sound output, left ear works fine. If I remove the pod from right ear, left ear will be muted too, so it seems not fully dead, just won't output any sound. Also, it took forever to pair with iphone 14 pro max too. I switched from Android but now I really regret making the change.Update Dec.7 today I went to Apple store for warranty service as it's defect on arrival. It took another 30 minutes for Apple to confirm there is no sound output from right pod, then the lady and her manager said they can't swap with a new set, they can only offer a replacement for right pod because it was purchased from Amazon instead of Apple store, even though they confirm it is genuine apple product with manufacture defect. So I got an individual pod with not matching firmware version and hopefully it's not a refurbished one. Guess the Apple store on Amazon is not the same as in those in malls, Amazon shoppers be aware!

title: Beware!
body: [[VIDEOID:e0f90fd00ca6c3598b1776b97620b7e]] I ordered these "Original Apple AirPods" for my girlfriend as a birthday present. I was super excited for her, but to my surprise, as soon as the package arrived I saw there was something wrong. Immediately looking at the box (see pictures and video) containing the "AirPods" I could tell this was not an original apple product (no apple logo, or serial number). Once I opened the colored box I could confirm that I was right on my assumption.I already contacted Amazon but I live in the Dominican Republic and I know I can't return packages to the US, so I'm stuck with some cheap knock offs, I will never use, which I paid more than 130+ Dollars.All of this is very annoying... I did NOT buy it through a third party seller, specifically because I wanted to avoid this situation. I'm very upset I got "tricked" into buying what I thought was an original product (paid in full as an original) sponsored by Amazon.

title: No idea if these are fakes or what - they are so much worse than my last AirPods
body: I used to have AirPods just like this, but recently lost one of the earbuds so decided to buy a new pair on Amazon. These are TERRIBLE and I am so disappointed.First they are extremely glitchy - constantly facing the issue where only one pod connects and not the other. They will also disconnect mid song, or the song I'm listening to will be choppy. My last AirPods (exact same generation) never had this issue.They also have way shorter range than my last AirPods. If my phone is even in the next room, they start breaking up and disconnect. Meanwhile my last AirPods used to hold up just fine if I went downstairs while my phone was in the other room.I have no idea what happened or what changed, but this set of AirPods is terrible and I will be returning them. Super disappointing and because I had such a great experience with my last AirPods it makes me wonder - has the Apple product gotten worse or did Amazon send me a fake?

title: STOPPED WORKING AFTER 3 MONTHS
body: I love the product and used it regularly... it worked perfectly, however after 3 months I noticed one day that it kept connecting and disconnecting. When it finally worked, music played in only the right airpod. I troubleshooted the issue and reset the airpods, forgot the device and even resetted my phone. It wasn’t an issue with my phone but for some reason the device wasn’t even showing up in Bluetooth devices. I called apple support and they told me to do the same thing I did, in fact there was nothing they could do for me to fix it. It doesn’t connect to any other iphone and would not appear in non apple phones. Do not buy this product without knowing there could be risks. If you happen to have a faulty airpod, even with a warranty, you wont be able to use it ever again. I dont even know what to do with mine. There no physical damage so I dont understand the problem. Please be aware that if your airpods have internal errors, you. Are. Screwed.

title: Yeah...no..😒
body: Extremely disappointed in this purchase. I ordered my first pair last year and they were perfect. Brand new, never used, fresh packaging. I lost both head phones , silly me, and decided to reorder to ensure I got the same product. Boy was I wrong. When I first opened the package I knew something was wrong. The box was dented and not covered in the standard Apple wrap. I open the AirPods and the first thing I noticed was the charging cord had a red stain on it and the right ear bud had ear wax on them. At this point I was curious as to if they were refurbished or fake. I clean them, flip open the case to begin connecting them and they say “Tash’s AirPods”...my names Leonah...still curious I connect them and was greater with the connection sound which followed by horrific static and the sound completely dropping from the right AirPod.Needless to say I will be returning these ASAP and going to the Apple store to buy a fresh pair.

title: Broke after a few months 👎🏻👎🏻
body: When I initially got the headphones, I was fascinated by how awesome they sounded. I would put them on and could basically hear in HD and the noise cancellation works, even with no music on.Buuut, that only lasted a few months. I bought them Sept. 2020 and up until about a month ago (2/21), they worked perfectly. Now, the sound only comes out from the little side speakers and not the main ones (it happened to both headphones), so the sound volume was reduced DRAMATICALLY.Now they just sound like cheap wireless headphones (maybe worse), and the only way to be able to hear it a bit better is by twisting it so the side speaker is facing my eardrum, but that makes it loose, so it falls out easily.Safe to say, I’m most likely not going to be buying Apple products from Amazon and might just stick to buying them directly from the Apple Store, because it seems like I got a defective pair and I’m not alone.

title: Order filled with third party refurbished parts
body: Within 3 months of purchasing this product, I noticed the headphones did not consistently charge in the case. It was very finicky and would lead the the airpods being dead half the time I would reach for them. Ultimately, I took them to the Apple Store, who then sent them off to be fixed/replaced. However, the techs at Apple noticed the serial numbers did not match between the case and the headphones and thus could not be fixed, indicating they were used parts that someone had repackaged as new and sold to me pretending they were a new set. Because they were broken and mismatched and apparently bought via a third party and not from Apple via Amazon as I had thought, I was not able to get the product fixed by Apple themselves and instead I have broken AirPods.Oh and the warranty associated with the serial number on the case expired 4 months earlier than when I purchased them, so I was out of luck there as well.

title: Failed amazon return
body: Got these as a gift for my mom. Presented as a gift and they completely wouldn’t hold a charge, gave us a flashing green light and wouldn’t connect. My mom lives in Hawaii and we’ve requested a return. Amazon’s system for returns has usually been good but for anyone living outside of the mainland it feels like a scam. They won’t allow you to ship it, they have UPS pick it up with a premade label and keep returning it because they can’t ship out lithium batteries by ground (obviously). They refuse to extend the return date a few days until I’m back in California to ship ground. They keep giving me the same line that they can send another ups person to pick it up for ground….. refuse to let me pay more for air. Like how did they ship it here if they can’t ship batteries?? Broken product, awful service. I should’ve ordered directly from the apple store. What a scam. Completely disappointed and frustrated.

title: Unreliable. Not worth the money!
body: Stopped working after only 9 months!!! I bought these less than 1 year ago and up until 2 months ago they worked just fine. The sound quality for listening to music is very unimpressive but I bought them to use for work while on virtual conference calls. 2 months ago the left earbud stopped picking up my voice on calls or recognizing any other voice commands. I tried several trouble shooting efforts to fix/reset but nothing worked. I could still hear music in it but could not use it on calls. So I started taking calls with just the right earbud. Two weeks ago this earbud too only picked up my voice intermittently. Finally today it stopped working too!!! I now have an expensive pair of earbuds that cant be used on phone calls or recognize any voice commands, which is why I bought them in the first place. I am very disappointed in the crappy quality. Apple lost a loyal customer on this one.

title: unreliable charging
body: This is my second pair (I had the first generation Airpods), and they have the same problem: unreliable charging. I'll put them in the case, and the orange charging light will come on; I take them out some time later, and one is completely discharged. I clean them, and still have problems. Also, sometime they don't register in my ear(s): I need to open and shut the case and insert/remove them to/from my ears multiple times. I followed Apple's cleaning instructions. Time for yet another visit to the Apple store.Update: Continues to happen, went to the Apple store, and this time they cleaned them and they worked, but they said I should buy a new charging case. This is after being only two years old. If they clean them they work fine. The problem started shortly after I got them, and the previous model had the same problem; and they tell me to buy a new charging case.

title: Watch out for your ears, if you like things quiet
body: These actually stay in better than I expected, and they work OK the most situations, but when I’m fortunate enough to be sitting in a quiet room, listening to something quietly in the background, while I am concentrating on something else, and then I get a phone call, the ringer volume blasts my ears, sometimes causing literal pain. I cannot drop the ringer volume as much as it would need to be for my ear comfort when I’m listening with the AirPods because then I will not hear the ringer when it is coming through the phone speaker. There is no way to separately adjust the volume of the ringer via phone speaker (when I want it loud enough to wake me up when I am on call overnight) versus the AirPods (where I want it to be controlled by the overall volume just like everything else that I’m listening to through the AirPods, so I don’t get blasted every time someone calls me).

title: Headphones inside were replaced with FAKE airpods
body: I am so upset with this purchase. I bought them thinking it was a great deal so something that was in fair condition. When I received the headphones I didn’t open them right away as I was busy planning my daughters birthday. When I finally opened them the airpods wouldn’t connect to my phone for some reason. After extensive research as to why they were f charging or connecting I found out that the AirPods inside the case were switched out and the AirPods were fake which is why I wasn’t holding any charge or connecting to my phone. I was so upset because I lost my money given that the window for return passed and was given an fauly product. Be careful when ordering these headphones and check them as soon as you buy them so you are able to return if needed. So upsetting to received a fake product. Amazon should really check for authenticity upon product returns like this.

title: Too many issues for the price
body: Overall, sound is fine. They’re just earbuds so I don’t expect audiophile levels of sound quality or any noise cancellation. But for an apple product, these things have far too many bugs for their price.These things are terrible outdoors or in very large spaces. They will cutout and disconnect, because I’m assuming the signal is too weak and doesn’t have anything to bounce off of. That makes them basically useless for the outdoors or in wide open spaces, like large atriums or gyms. I can really only expect to use these in the home or office, which meant I had to buy another pair of headphones that worked outdoors. It’s a bit ridiculous.Speaking of home and office use, the mic does not work well at all, and is unusable for phone calls, video/zoom calls, etc. So again, I have to switch to a headset or my other pair of headphones for that, since people cannot hear me if I attempt to use these.On top of that, they also have issues with ear detection and randomly disconnecting from my iPhone. I’ll be wearing them, listening to whatever, and suddenly they just drop the connection, as if I took them out of my ear. The only way to get it working again is to place it back into the case and take them out again and place them back in your ear. It’s very annoying and happens almost every time I use them.I’ve used two pairs since 2018, and both have had these issues. I’ve only used them with other apple products, multiple iPhones and an iPad, so I know it’s not a device compatibility or Bluetooth issue.My oldest pair has almost no battery life left, and is only useable for less than an hour at a time, so don’t expect these things to last more than a few years either.In conclusion, these have always felt like a step backward from a classic pair of wired earbuds. Sure, they’re wireless, but it just feels so much less usable in so many different ways, while being 10 times the cost. Apple needs to seriously step up their wireless earbuds game if they insist on moving away from wired connections and forcing us to use the garbage pods.

title: Don't Work with Android Phone
body: I like these AirPods, but they have some serious downsides in my mind, that are quite frustrating. What I like: the sound quality, when they are working properly, is very good. They fit well in my ears and feel secure, and the battery charge is pretty impressive (I only charge the case occasionally, and it keeps the earbuds charged for as long as I need them). The audio quality for phone calls is good, and it's nice to be able to talk on the phone hands-free. There is virtually no noise cancellation, but I didn't buy them for that purpose. I wanted comfortable, easy to use, high-quality wireless earbuds.What I don't like: I have an Android phone and have had consistent issues with the bluetooth connection. In fact, I returned my first pair of AirPods and exchanged them for a new pair because of this. Sometimes (especially when I'm walking outside) the bluetooth connection will cut out, then reconnect. It usually does this several times. When the signal reconnects the audio sounds echo-y, like one earbud is lagging slightly behind the other. It disrupts the sound quality and is really annoying. This only happens when I'm out walking, and usually only in certain places, so I wonder if it's an issue with signal interference. I don't have this problem when connected to my MacBook or my phone when I'm listening to music indoors or while I'm driving. These are advertised as working with Android phones, so I'm very disappointed with the issues I'm having. My replacement pair has the exact same problems.If these issues continue to be disruptive I will have to return these as well. It's very frustrating to have my music cut out and the poor audio quality. I'm hoping that the audio issues are signal interference from specific locations and that they'll work in other locations, because I really do like having the wireless earbuds for music and calls. I'm hopeful, and would appreciate any fixes others have tried, but I'll have to return them if this continues to be an issue.

title: Did not last more than 6 months.  Constantly fall out of my ears.
body: Mixed feelings on this item. I have a pair that I have had for about a year and a half that still work well. I only use them a few times a week, but other than the fact that they constantly fall out of my ears, I like them. If I wasn't able to find the aftermarket silicone ear hooks that I need to use to keep them from falling out all the time, I would have returned them and give them a 1 star rating. To be fair, I have small ears. So, I would consider another option if you have small ears. That said, this review is for a second pair I bought as a gift. Unfortunately, after 6 months the right pod would not charge/work at all. The left one would work, but only for about 20 min. If fully charged and left in the charging case, both the case and the working pod would completely drain, which implies the batteries are not good in the case or the pod(s). The person I bought these for didn't tell me about the problems until several months after she noticed it. So, I had her give them to me and contacted Apple. Support was really good and they tried to help me get them to work over the phone. Unfortunately, no luck. So, I asked what my options were to get them replaced or fixed. They said they were out of warrantee and it would cost over $200 +$12 S&H to get them repaired. I paid under $150 for them, so naturally all I could say was thanks for the help... I guess I'll just throw these in the trash. There are a lot of options out there that are a lot less pricy. So, I will replace the gift, since I don't like to give a gift that is defective. However, I may look at the Samsung or a couple of others that are out there. Again, the ones I own still work fine. This pair was obviously just a "lemon", but since Apple wasn't willing to do anything, I have a bad taste in my mouth. Pretty disappointed with Apple. Such good service, but won't stand behind their products. Don't even get me started about the easy-break glass on their phones and pads.

title: Not worth the price with such major weaknesses
body: Yes they are nice, but they're not worth the price. They perform no different than the wired version that comes with the iPhone and man they are a PAIN to connect to a computer. I can't get it to work and I don't understand how more people aren't mentioning it, just google it and you'll see how big the issue is. I digress, on to the product review.I use them mainly for sleeping and for the gym. They're okay at the gym, they stay in which is good but without any noise cancelling or noise isolation, the sound is either too loud at the loud portions or way too quiet to hear so I'm constantly adjusting the volume. The earbuds are nice for sleeping though. They are low profile so I can lay on my side without them pressing into my ears.I don't find myself having to worry about charge ever, but I'm not using them for extended periods of time.Sound quality is A tier. Everything sounds great but once again, without sound isolation the airpods won't get S or SS tier because I'm constantly having to adjust them. I haven't had this issue with beats or bose bluetooth earbuds.Overall, I find the lack of noise isolation to be the deciding factor. With such a premium price tag, I am surprised to find myself forgetting I have the earbuds in and able to carrying on normal conversations without taking them out (Maybe these are best suited for a workplace). This makes my gym experience a pain in the butt, but preferable to semi-wireless earbuds that slowly pull on one side and I have to keep adjusting to stay in my ears.The product is good unless you want to pair to a PC, but the price is ridiculous. They're nice enough that I will keep them, but had I known all of this earlier, I'd have gotten the airpod pros or whatever the equal competition is (they'd likely pair to a computer better).

title: they are ok
