body: the airpods were super good when i first got them, i got them when the airpod “2.0” came out and i decided to save money and buy these because i didn’t need the wireless charging feature. when i got them they worked flawless, if only they had stayed that way. they started to cut in and out as if i was walking too far away from them when i was still right next to them. i searched far and wide to figure out what was wrong with them but no one seemed to have this problem. i reset them over 100 times pissed off because i had just spent 160 dollars on earbuds that had the durability of paper. keep in mind i didn’t drop them a single time when this had happened. i ignored it and just used one earbud. this became a big problem when i wanted to block out noise when riding the bus etc... the problem with the airpods is that they don’t block out any noise whatsoever so u find urself turing it up to block out the noise but once it gets quiet you realize ur making ur ears want to die from the loud noise, and once i wanted to use two i noticed it got quiet on one earbud and loud in the other. i had heard rumors about them becoming quiet due to wax build up but i made sure my ears stayed clean 24/7. but it happened to me anyways. i searched up how to fix it and i used all the things they told me to do, but nothing worked. so i did the only thing i knew would actually work, i poked out the mesh inside of the airpod where the music comes out. ta da worked like magic, to this day they haven’t become quiet, but they still have the stupid cutting in and out problem. anyways if u buy these and they cut in and out go return them because you have a year warranty and i didn’t notice that so don’t mess up like i did and poke out the mesh in the earbud and void ur warranty.

title: Who needs gee whiz
body: When I read the other reviews, I see a lot of credence given to the “gee whiz” factor of the new chip, double tap, “Hey Siri”, etc. But let’s be honest, what is important? Sound quality, microphone quality, noise cancellation, and ease of use (pairing, connecting, etc.)Sound quality, I give them a VERY generous 2 out of 5. There is NO bass, little treble, so I’m left with a tinny sound with no warmth. It’s terrible, and I’ve had better sound from $10 ear buds. On the other hand, on phone calls and Skype calls via PC, the sound is loud and clear, just staccato.Microphone quality/noise cancellation: Leaving messages for myself, if there is any noise cancellation I can’t hear it. I hear ALL the background noise just as well as I hear myself. Other people seem to hear me loudly, but not necessarily clearly.Oh gee, I can pair by holding the open case close to my device. Big whoop. If my PC is currently using my desktop speakers, I have to go to the Bluetooth controls and connect the already paired AirPods. That’s ridiculous. All my other Bluetooth headsets are automatically included in the audio output selections once they are paired. Why should I have to go to Bluetooth settings to “connect” AirPods when they’ve already paired? This is TERRIBLE.All in all, had I known what I was getting, i would have purchased something else. As it is, I don’t want to put another $150 to $300 into another set of Bluetooth buds. But I’m listening to music as I write this, and it’s terrible. Maybe others like the sound, but when a bass guitar hitting a low C or low G comes out sounding like a drum, it’s not good.

title: ***Be careful if returning to Amazon***
body: I did not like how these fit. They fit awkwardly and I constantly had to check and refit them to ensure they stay in place. There's virtually no noise cancellation to speak of and the sound quality is average. These would sell for at most $30 if it doesn't have the Apple brand slap to them. The only plus sides are how quickly they connect to Apple products and battery seem to be decent.I ended up returning mine and had a horrible experience with Amazon that made me rethink about buying high value items on Amazon. I utilized the Amazon pickup service, UPS picked it up and delivered the package to Amazon. A week went by it still shows that the package has not been picked up. I contacted Amazon and Customer Service confirmed the package has been received and a refund will be issued in 48 hours. A month went by still no refund. I reached out to Amazon again, this time they said the returns team said the wrong item was returned, they discarded the package and will not be issuing a refund. I personally handed the package to UPS so I know the right item is enclosed. There apparently is no way for customers to dispute or request an investigation, we just had to take their words for it. I spent thousands of dollars on Amazon. If I want to "pull a fast one" I would've done it on something much more expensive...Anyway, the moral of the story is to think twice about buying anything expensive with Amazon if there's a chance you might return them. There is no recourse for customers if someone on the returns team decides you've returned the wrong item.

title: Not my favorite. Buggy.
body: I have problems with the Audio becoming desynced and some annoying issues with old firmware. There was also a rather nasty bug with the model I received. It nearly made me deaf - if you don't know to update the thing (which is a problem for Android...) then you might suffer some hearing damage. I used them for days with the bug and was on the cusp of returning them. The bug is that the audio defaults to maximum volume - with any device (iOS, Android, etc) - until the software is updated. This volume setting reverts to maximum everytime the device enters a power saving state. They sleep aggressive (like maybe 5 seconds of silence). So, if you're streaming music and it takes more than 5 seconds to fetch the next song -- you're getting blasted at full volume force from a device directly inserted into your ear. Very painful. Again, if you just update them they're fine. I was able to reproduce the problem on multiple devices and it wasnt until I updated my airpods that the problem went away. Now the only problem I have is the occasional desync of audio. It's easily fixed and a lot of other people have the same issue sadly. I have noticed that holding the phone differently affects the rate at which desync occurs, so probably just something with signaling. Leaving the phone flat and uncovered/held remedies my issue. When they work, they're really nice. But overall the initial frustrations really took it's toll on my ability to like this product. Hopefully there's better QA on the next generation.

title: Just give me my proper headphone jack back.
body: If you still have a headphone jack in your phone, don't buy these. After buying several dysfunctional dongles with headphone jacks (both official apple brand and off brand) and off brand wireless earbuds worth over $100 combined I gave in and bought these. In general these are better than most wireless earbuds, and its better to just buy these despite the price. They are about as comfortable to wear, and they have about the same level sound quality as my $10 gummy earbuds (Gummy earbuds have good quality and last a while so I'm not saying the quality is bad, just that these are insanely overpriced). They have some convenient functions but they are not worth $150 if you can avoid it. I enjoy being able to listen to music and podcasts privately so for the sake of continuing that source of pleasure I was forced to buy these. They don't always instantly connect to your phone which is slightly inconvenient but an easy fix, and the tap to pause function only sometimes works, so I could either tap it twice and it instantly pause perfectly like its supposed to or it could fail to pause after a dozen attempts. My next phone purchase might just be going backwards to the last iphone with a headphone jack just so I can listen to my music hassle free. That or finally abandon apple after all these years and find a good brand with a headphone jack.

title: Something off about these…
body: I had the first gen AirPods for a long time but lost one of the ear buds. Bought them from an apple store.Purchased these online and they have little strange issues that my previous ones never had. they don’t “react” in the same way as my old ones. You can put them in your ear and that “I’ve sensed you’ve put me in your ear” sound doesn’t activate it all. I have to take it out and put it back in multiple times until it senses it. Typically I have to get out the case and open and close it numerous times. Put it back in the case. Take it out. Just play with it until it decides it’s good and ready to work. I never had this issue with my old ones. As soon I put them in, they work as they should. These ones- are garbage.Secondly it doesn’t pick up when I open or close the case. Typically when you do this, the battery percentage info will appear on your phone. Maybe you have to do it a couple extra times but it will work quickly. These, RARELY work. I can open and close to case over 20 times before the battery will appear on my phone. If it even appears at all. Usually I just give up and plug it in.Really disappointed in these. Not sure why the quality is not the same as my old ones… that I’ve had for a LOOONG time. Something is wrong with these. Are they authentic?

title: Keeps Connecting
body: I've always wanted AirPods but found the cost too expensive. I was really happy when I found these AirPods for cheaper and I found the double tap feature really convenient when I'm on the go. Unfortunately, I've experienced multiple issues with them.I often find the notification that my AirPods are connected when they're in the case and the case is closed. I thought it was because I was using an older phone, but I just got the new iPhone 13 and the issues persist. It's pretty annoying to have the notification because while it's at the top of my screen, I can't swipe down to access the notification bar, and it takes a while to go away. There was a time when I was playing music through the speaker and the music stopped because my phone said my AirPods had connected.I've also had issues with the double tap feature. There are times when my music pauses by itself and though my hair sometimes brushes against, I just don't think it can mimic a double tap. There are also times when the headphones won't respond to a double tap.Lastly, they die so quickly! I don't know if this is normal for all AirPods but it feels like I'm constantly having to charge them, whereas my other Bluetooth headphones can run for days on a single charge. I'm pretty bummed that these didn't work out.

title: Disappointed
body: My niece has had hers for a while and loved them. So I thought I would give them a try.The first issue I have with them is the battery does not last longer than 3 hours. (That might be my bad, maybe they are not designed to.) If I am going to be on a call longer than 3 hours, I do like that i can use one earbud and the other can stay charging in the case. I do lose any noise canceling that it does that way. In addition sometimes the one earbud will not have audio. I have to put in and take it out of the case a few times to get it to work, so as long as I try switching the earbud before the one dies I can talk longer than 3 hours.The next issue I have is they do not always connect to my iPhone 11 and/or my Samsung Note 10+. I actually have better luck with the Samsung than the iPhone. Once I get the Samsung to connect it will almost always do so, the audio might cut in and out but they connect. The iPhone on the other hand will say it is connected but there will be no audio. It is frustrating, maybe it is the two phones that cause the issues, I am not sure. I bought them because so many loved them, but I would not recommend them at unless you really do not talk long on your phone and only had one phone. Then maybe you would have less issues than I have had.

title: Doesn't fit for everyone and gets lost easily
body: When i bought this product i was sure i found the best possible Bluetooth headset.Now after having it for few months already (or rather say not having it for few month) i can voice my opinion clearly.These peered well with my phone, and You can hear well in it too. But here comes the downside of this:A-It doesn't fit well in my ear. Gets my ear itchy with the feeling of my ear needs to breath :). I need to take it off in middle of a call for a few minutes because of comfort.How can a one shaped earbud fit universally into everyone's ear??????? It doesn't have the soft pieces in the end which makes it flexible fit in the ear customized to your ear shape.As a side note, I purchased clips to hold these around my ear. They helped somewhat.B-It is so small which looks cool on the ear but gets lost so fast. Only if you have the apple phone or device with it you can have the app which locates it in case it gets lost.This is the other downside of it.C-It doesn't cancel out noise well. When washing dishes I would rather use a cheap round the ear headset because with these the other person on the other side of the line would hear the water running and I would come in very not clear.

title: Premium price , poor product. No volume control buttons.
body: So disappointed in these second generation Apple Airpods that I bought as a gift, the recipient could not use them (bad fit, volume control issues) so I tried them.They do not sit well in the ear, they are advertised for use during sports, among other things, but are really poorly thought out , the Airpods frequently fall out making them unsuitable for cycling. They leak sound really badly , making them unsuitable for use around other people or in quiet locations. The battery life is terrible at 1-2 hours. The sound quality is OK, on a par with earphones like the ones airlines give away.Definitely not worth the price and to top it all Apple appear to be limiting the compatibility with other wearables (or are doing something with licensing fees) for example the Airpods do not work with Fitbit Versa devices (no volume control) . Even with the iphone there is no way to adjust the volume without getting out the phone or invoking Siri. Just imaging having to say ,hey Siri turn the volume up/down during a flight or similar.Purchased a set of TaoTronics bluetooth headphones, better fit, battery, have a volume control and work with the fitbit Versa, oh and a fraction of the price.

title: ***LOUD*** calendar alerts may damage hearing and no way to adjust volume
body: The loudness of calendar alerts is a deal-breaker. It is maximum volume and seriously likely to damage your hearing and/or give you a heart attack. When the alert goes off you claw at your skull to rip these out of your ears it is that bad. I have thrown them across the room.This is a known bug with these.I researched this for days and the best i found was one method to adjust volume (kill all apps, reduce volume, use as normal( but as soon as you adjust the volume through a different app or on another set of Bluetooth speakers, it goes back to maximum.There is no practical way to control this.If you use your Airpods and phone for business and have a lot of calendar alerts, the Airpods suck.On the positive side, the sound is pretty good.They also charge very rapidly in the case - very pleasantly surprised at that. I get about 4 - 5 charges from the case's battery before that has to be recharged.The feature for checking the battery on your iPhone by opening the case near the phone is also very cool.If they could fix the calendar bug they would be 5/5 rated.

title: If you have Samsung phone, read before you buy!
body: I have a samsung s7 edge phone and these things ONLY work if I am sitting still or hold the phone up near my face while walking(you know how dumb you look when you do that?!?!). If I am walking and put my phone in my pocket, music on spotify cuts out. I have spotify premium and the songs are downloaded to my phone so internet/cell service isn't the issue here. Also my Sony headphones work perfectly fine and have been for over 4 years now. Want to work out with these airpods? Good luck, I can't even walk without music cutting out. The only thing these airpods are good for is calling someone, there are no issues with that. I took the airpods to "genius" bar at apple and they gave me new ones, it's even worse now. This is why I stay away from apple all of these years....they just put out sub par products. The "tech" at the "genius" bar said it could be my phone even after I told him my Sony headphones work fine. There is nothing genius about them or their products. Trust me, save yourself some $$$ and frustrations and get other headphones. Don't make the same mistake I did.

title: Convenient but lacking in Features and Build Quality
body: I'm an Apple fan and really wanted to like these!...QUICK TAKE -- Convenient, but UNCOMFORTABLE, TOO QUIET, ONLY ONE CONTROL, and NOT Water-ResistantThe setup and automatic pairing make these really convenient to use. However, for the Apple premium price, there are a few things that just come up way too short for me:- Build quality is poor, leaving sharp edges where the plastic pieces meet. This gets really painful when you're taking them in and out throughout the day (I've tried 3 pairs, thinking I might have got a dud, and only 1 of the 6 pods had smooth edges).- The ONLY control is double-tap. This is Apple -- I'd expect a few more touch options. Can't control volume.- Way too quiet. It also seems obvious that pods that have no isolation or noise cancelling (for the sake of the design, which I understand) should have a lot of headroom for volume. Not the case with these.- Not even water resistant, which is really disappointing. I'm nervous walking with them if it's raining or snowing. We have the technology...

title: Very buggy and infuriating!
body: I’ve only had these AirPods around a year and a half. They have developed multiple annoying issues, to the point that I’m scraping them and buying another brand. For the $150 I paid for these things I sure expected better. Here are a few of the issues I’m having:1. The sound isn’t loud anymore. The max it goes up is around 60% of full volume max. If I’m cutting grass and wearing them, I can’t hear what’s playing through them anymore.2. They don’t charge reliably anymore. Not sure if they have a short in them, but sometimes after putting them in the case, they won’t charge.EX: I’ll take them out to use them and only have like 12% battery life left and that’s after supposedly charging them overnight.3 When I move my head they disconnected or turn off the music.4. Many times Im having to frequently disconnect and reconnect them to get them to work.Going to try another brand and see how that works out? I’ve never tried a non-Apple earbud before, but this last set of AirPods have pushed me into that direction.

title: Apple, Why?
body: I am unsure who ears Apple had in mind when creating these ear buds. I like to think I have average, regular ears but these headphones make me feel like perhaps my ears are too small. I have to adjust the ear piece every few seconds so much so I had to buy one of the ear attachment pieces sold here on Amazon just so the headphones don't fall out which coincidentally has happened. Watching my $160+ Airpods fall to the ground nearly broke my heart. Aside from that the sound quality is fine... I can feel the bass. I hate the fact that there's a button now instead of the tap, tap feature the Air Pod Second generation has. So I have to deal with the ear piece constantly trying to fall out, being shorter than the Sec Gen and now I have work to find this miniature button on the ear piece just to skip songs. Listen to me sounding spoiled.... I'm at a cross. I was "forced" to buy these because my 2nd Gen was staticky and everyone complained. I thought perhaps these being the newer gen they'd be greater. Oh well!! At least people can hear me now!!

title: Not everything and a bag of Doritos.
body: While initially enamored of my purchase of the Air Pods, I soon discovered that keeping them or one in my ear was uncomfortable. I can go for the length of a conversation, but more than that and my ear begins to hurt. To be fair, I have small ear canals, so... Beyond that, the right AirPod has begun to malfunction, cutting out innumerable times during a conversation despite being fully charged. I have to manually change the mode back to AirPod each time. I also am not so taken with the inability to answer an incoming call with the AirPod. You can "tap" it off, I guess, after finishing the call, but one must hunt down one's phone and manually accept the call for the AirPods to work. I had a BlueTooth for years that was seamless in every way: comfortable, convenient, adjustable in sound, -- just the best and then they stopped making them. Too good, I guess. I tried another kind; it lasted for about a day. Why do these things always claim to be "improvements'" but just prove to be less than the initial?

title: Nope. Just setting yourself up for disappointment.
body: I love how comfortable these are. The sound quality is good and the Bluetooth range is impressive. The battery life is fine. Nothing incredible, but it will get you through 3-4 days before you have to charge the case again. However, they DO NOT last! I bought a pair of the original AirPods and they lasted about a year before the microphone went out on the left ear. 3 months later the microphone on the right AirPod went out.Because I have small ears I have a hard time finding earbuds that work so I caved and bought a gen 2 about 6 months ago.Guess what happened?! Yep. The left microphone went out. I fully anticipate the right one will go anytime now.So even though these are comfortable and have great sound quality I do not recommend. If I’m spending more than $100 for earbuds they should work for more than 6-12 months.I’m on the hunt again and I’ll find something that lasts more than a minute. Or something cheap enough that it won’t hurt my feelings to have to replace every 6 months.

title: EARS ARE NOT ONE SIZE FITS ALL, VERY VERY LIMITED BATTERY LIFE
body: These ear pods are wonderful in quality of sound, ease of use. I particularly like how you just lift it into your ear and it knows to switch. I have not figured out how to answer and end a call without having to tap the phone, but haven’t looked it up. Tapping the airpod activates the music and switches the song, which is nice, but I have to find out if I can stop music play.Downside: Battery life is surprisingly and disappointingly brief. I used them for a phone call that lasted about 1 1/2 hours. The buds went dead before the call was finished!Ears are not one size fits all. I must be careful when using them or they will fall out of my ear. Having to pay an additional $100 for the “pro" model, which is supposed to have adjustable fittings, is a rip off. It’s like buying a pair of shoes, advertised at $100. “Oh, you want them in YOUR size? Well, that will be an additional $100." So, if purchasing these, beware of the fit.I will not recommend these or purchase again.

title: Not sure if this was a good investment
body: Overall i have really enjoyed my airpods but have only had them for a couple months. Sound quality is pretty decent. Noise cancellation isn’t noticeable but then again these are not designed for noise cancellation so I Wasn’t really expecting that aspect. I gave noise cancellation 2 stars in case someone might be looking for that. Battery life is as expected and there isn’t a complaint there. Here’s my main complaint and I consider it a big one. For the first couple weeks i have really enjoyed the AirPods and used them for hands free calling. Within the last week people have started complaining they cannot hear me very well while speaking on the phone. I have tried adjusting the position of the buds in case the mic wasn’t close to my mouth and I have tried cleaning them but things haven’t improved. Here’s the kick in the teeth...yesterday was the last day for me to return them through Amazon. I’m currently waiting to speak with apple.

title: I wouldn’t recommend it for the price.
body: I purchased a pair of these a few months and they aren’t worth the money. They last about 4 hours but they charge really fast so that isn’t to bad. My biggest problem is with the controls. To play/pause, forward, back, and Siri you have to set it, and a double tap will activate it. However I usually only use one so I can only use one command at a time. The worst thing though is to turn them on it is just supposed to take putting them in your ear but after just a month one of them will only start working is I put the other one in first or SOMETIMES activate it manually. On top of that the same ear piece no longer works with the double tap so I can’t pause or play or answer a call or hang up with it.To top it off they are easy to drop and lose if you are not careful. I haven’t lost them yet. All in all not with $170 maybe $100 just because they are Apple and they usually make good quality items.

title: Returned Due To Muffled and Low Volume Microphone
body: I've been using some cheaper options, but decided to upgrade to the real deal. I've seen people using these in TV interviews and figured they must be the best.Unfortunately, for my uses, which are mostly recorded video interviews via Zoom, etc., the microphone was awful. Versus a cheapo $30 lav mic, the mic on the buds sounded very muffled. No crispness on the high frequencies at all. Worst of all, people could not hear me on the other end. The output volume for the mic was extremely low versus everything else I've used. I tried adjusting the audio settings in Zoom and other programs, but still no good even at 100% volume.Maybe it sounds great when used with iPhone or an Apple device (I don't know), but performance when paired with my Windows 10 laptop was too poor for my uses. It paired fine and I could hear fine but the muffled, low-volume microphone was a deal breaker for me.

title: Great headphones (update)
body: I love these air pods. Price finally came down from the original price when they first came out so I went for them. The last pair of head phones I owned was back when the wired version was around so it’s been a few years. These sounds great.. love having no wires. They stay in my ears no problem. I didn’t want noise cancelling headphones so these are perfect. I like them especially at the gym hearing what’s going on around me. I feel almost unsafe with noise canceling headphones.. battery life is great. I highly recommend buying theseUpdate:The right AirPod has been slowly going bad over the last couple weeks and now it’s always at 1% life when I open the up to pair them. That’s with 100% charged case. They’ll start charging when I open the case... great while they lasted. Used indoors only to.. disappointed especially for the price I expect better from Apple

title: Honestly Disappointed
body: Ok - these were great at first and I used them every day. But after a few months:1. The microphone stopped working whenever I tried to use them on a call or on FaceTime and the person on the other side couldn’t hear me2. The sound quality just went completely down hill - the volume was so much quieter than when I got them, and it sounded so staticky. Even at full volume it sounded like the music was so far away.3. Sometimes they charge, sometimes they don’t. I can leave them plugged in charging all night and the next morning they are still completely dead. Other nights the charge completely fine - it’s honestly a game of roulette.I love the idea of small Bluetooth earbuds, but these are just so expensive for the quality. They work great at first but deteriórate so fast and they end up being way more trouble than they’re worth.

title: The Apple Tax
body: They are okay wireless headphones, when they work. Every other day, I lose connectivity and have to restart my phone or go through a long process to reconnect. It is very frustrating when you are on the move.I only purchased these because my LG wireless headset finally broke after 4 years. I was going to purchase another one, but the cost difference was about the same. After using the AirPods after a week, I much rather prefer my LG Tone headset. Better quality and functionality.There some features I like, such as Siri, and immediately pausing sound, but that doesn’t make up for the flaws. Lose one piece, have to buy the whole kit. It can be a chore to keep track of these things, taking them off when not in use and you are moving around.Get this if you want an Apple fashion accessory. Otherwise there are better products out there.

title: Great for Short-Term : Long-Term Sucks
body: Take this with a grain of salt. My family owns 1 first gen airpod and 2 second gen airpods. All of them worked great in the beginning. It connected well, regardless of device (Android, Apple), and it was convenient to just pop in.However, after a year or so of extensive using, THIS WAS THE MAIN PROBLEM: Often times, only 1 AirPod would connect, so we would have to pop it back in the case, wait, and then hope both connected. Let me tell ya, many many times it failed to connect both, and we still have this issue today. So we have to sometimes restart our computers, restart Bluetooth, re-connect devices, etc. Obviously, it's not a big deal, but it gets REALLY REALLY ANNOYING. And this happens across all 3 of our Airpods, so something ain't right okay. I personally would not get these again TBH, but I am gunna keep using these for money's worth sake.

title: First Apple Product that I bought that is a piece of junk
body: Despite my earlier review of these AirPods ; they turned out to be a piece of junk! I kept having troubles and sent them back into the Apple Repair center. They returned them with an email message they couldn't find anything wrong....but when I received the package back there was in insert stating that they had "loaded the latest software, checked them out and they should be fine. They worked for about 2 weeks, now I'm having the same issues as before in that one pod displays a low charge and the other at 100%, they drop off completely and I have to start the sound over again on my I-Pod. I'm really angry and feel like I've been sold a defective product. So I will have to deal with the repair center again and go thru that merry go round . What a real disappointment these things are. I'm glad I bought a 2 year warranty, but this is really frustrating.

title: Uncomfortable, hard to pair with iPhone 12, one earbud not working at all!
body: I returned these to Amazon after receiving a defective pair of Airpods. One earbud refused to charge at all. The other, even when fully charged, was totally hit or miss when it came to pairing with my new iPhone 12 Pro Max. Sometimes while I was listening, it unpaired for no reason and refused to re-pair. I did not order a replacement pair because, on top of the charging & pairing issues, the hard plastic ear pieces are flat-out uncomfortable. Apparently, one has to spring for the top of the line ($$) wireless Airpods to get an earbud that can be adjusted for comfort. I went back to my $25 Treblab bluetooth earbuds, which pair effortlessly and fit comfortably into the ear canals with soft silicone covers.PS When the Airpods were working, sound seemed fine, but I listen to podcasts & books so don't know how good they are for music.

title: Great headphones…. For one year until one AirPods inevitably breaks
body: I love these AirPods, but this is the SECOND PAIR of 2nd generation airpods I’ve had where one AirPod just stops working. Both times, this occurred right after my one year mark and right after my warranty expired. My first AirPod started having poor sound quality out of nowhere, while the second pair had the same issue while also having mic quality issues where I can’t use it to speak in the phone. I’m both cases, Apple did not help me to replace the AirPods, even after the second pair of AirPods shocked the inside of my ear while running.All this being said, ear hds do get damaged and I won’t claim to not ever drop them. However, the fact that two pairs of AirPods stopped working exactly a year later, right when the warranty ends, doesn’t sit well with me. As much as I love these AirPods, I can’t bring myself to buy them again….

title: Pros and cons, but overall disappointed.
body: These don't stay in my ears, I had to buy separate in-ear style pieces. They don't always connect with my iPhone and I have to "forget this device" in Bluetooth settings and start over. The auto pause function when removing from ears doesn't always work either. (Very annoying when you lose your place in your audiobook!). This happens whether I have the in-ear pieces on or not. The sound quality is good, but I don't think there is any noise cancelling effect at all. I can't hear the audio clearly when vacuuming like I can with other cheap wired headphones. The only reason I didn't return these for another brand is that they can be used one at a time unlike other brands. Overall I'm disappointed with Apple, especially for the price. They would be totally worth it if they worked well and had different options for earbud styles.

title: Complete let down...
body: I have the first gen. AirPods for several years before I lost one to a dog incident and the other to a washing machine. LOVED THEM but didn't have the money to invest in a new pair at the time. Tried a few knockoff brands for $20-$30 and had decent success but when I started travelling weekly for work, it was time to up the game again. Bought the new generation model and have been wildly disappointed ever since. Static regularly, battery life in average, on Zoom meetings they start going haywire at about the 40 minute mark and they only intermittently connect to my Microsoft Surface (which the knockoff brands had no problem doing). Maybe I just got a bunk pair of these but if I could go back to the originals I would. If I could also get my $200 back, I'd do that too. Sometimes newer isn't better.

title: Don’t let them sit without using them.
body: My wife bought these for me as a gift. I tried them out one time and put them back in the case. At the time, I didn’t use the earbud. About a year and a half later I decided to use them. However, they will not connect once I take them out of the case. As long as they’re in the case they are connected. I called Apple and they tell me they need updated, however they will not update I tried everything as I’m supposed to and apparently the firmware or whatever is so old it won’t update.Apple tells me I will need to take them to an Apple store and I will be charged for repairing them. They are not broken mine to you, but they still will charge me to get them working. I don’t get it, I wore them once for a few minutes they were almost completely unused. I would not buy them again. I feel cheated

title: Not good for music.
body: Wow, so many incredible 5 star reviews. For real? I hate ever having to write a negative review but just can't let this one slide by. I bought a different brand some months back (Tozo) and although there are minor flaws, the sound quality of the Tozo is FAR superior to the Apple Airpods. Geez, for the price tag, thought for sure the Airpods would rank number one. Received these Airpods as a gift a few days ago and I'm sadly disappointed. Where is the bass?? The only thing I can say positive is that they are a more comfortable listening experience. But, I'd rather have the slightly less comfortable pair with superior sound quality. If you only want these for talking on the phone or maybe watching a movie, guess they're ok. But if you want a good listening experience for music, save your money!

title: SKIP- it’s what they do
body: I’ve spent most of the time I’ve owned these trying to decide if they’re were real. They’re much lighter and cheaper feeling then my old gen1’s and the connectivity on them is miserable. If my phone isn’t in my hand, like in my pocket or purse they go in and out which with music is merely annoying but if you’re trying to talk on the phone or listen to an audiobook it’s enraging. AND I can’t wirelessly charge them. I wish I could return them. Save the money, buy the gen1.As far as the new audio chip? It’s not better enough to justify the price change and certainly not when you add in how crappy they are. Source: I’m a professional audio engineer. I have headphones half the price sound better. But gen1 or wait for the next set and hope with me they suck less

title: Optimistic But Disappointed!
body: The Airpods arrived very fast, clean packaging, ez to pair. Charged and placed into opn quickly.I read as many comments as one could stand. Though very cautious, I leaped in.Two most important Airpod features are Sound & Battery.If u r lookn to hear without straining, these ARE NOT the product for u. No onboard volume adjustments, so phone is dialed up to the max. TERRIBLE Audibles!!!!Also, the one size fits all earlobs does not work...need to add form of clear ear-sleeves. Battery seems to be good so far.Manufacturer, unless they do not work well with certain I-phones (I have a 6+), u should not sell this junk or place a caveat in the description. I will try them with my I-ph 7+ & 8 as well.

title: May the odds be ever in your favor
body: I was a little wary when ordering these and when I was unpacking the air pods. I first checked the serial number on the apple website and then it wanted me to associate it with my Apple ID. I took that as good news. Had no problem connecting to my iPhone 13 Max Pro.I started listen to music and it sounded fine. It was a bit different from my Samsung Buds I still have, but I was still optimistic.Things turned when I answered a call. The call kept going high and low where I can’t hear the other side but they could hear me. When I called, they could only hear me on speaker phone, like the previous call.I’m not sure if these are real, but these definitely are making me think if I actually need another pair anyways.

title: Dissapointed if Apple Airpods
body: I bought my airpods on Feb. Didn't use them until March because it was a present for my hubby, well now I was listening to them and the right air pod went dead. I figured, maybe I just need to charge them. So when I got home I charged them, and the next day I went to use them again and the right air bud was still dead.I put them to charge and when I went to retrive them from the case I notice the one with the issue was stuck and felt very hot, it overheated (melted) and now it's completed dead. Very dissapointed.I chatted with an Amazon customer service and explained the problem and he was a ble to assist me and said he would send a repacement and exchange the bad ones for some working airpods;He made my day. Thank you Amazon

title: Does NOT Last
body: At first I really liked them. They stay in my ears and didn't hurt that much, and the battery life seemed to last for a long time. I've had them for about 6 months now though and they have stopped working the way they are supposed to. When I want to use them I have to fiddle with them a lot to get them to work and most of the time only one ear bud is charged. They don't pop up on my phone when I'm trying to see how charged they are and when they are charging only one of them is charging at a time. I don't know what to do because it seems like a total waste of money to buy Airpods for $130 for them to only last 6 months. Now I'm looking into buying new wireless earbuds but I'm definitely not going to be buy Airpods again. :(

title: Great at the start
body: I've had my Apple AirPods for more than 2 years and while I loved them at the start I no longer can recommend them. The battery life has diminished to the point they are unusable. When I get a phone call the right airpod will disconnect because it can't handle the power of a phone conversation. Otherwise, the right will only last 30 minutes with bluetooth audio. I only get 1 hour of life on the left side. I understand battery life degrades overtime with a product like this however for the price and Apple name I expected better quality. They now feel disposable and there are cheaper options with same or better quality sound available from competitors so I will be switching when I replace them away from Apple.

title: Defective
body: UPDATE: ever since I bought these AirPods the right ear will occasionally stop working. I have to remove and charge again, and star over. Poor quality as I expect more from an Apple product.ORIGINAL REVIEW: I bought these to use on a trip. My first attempt didn’t produce the sound I needed; so had to start over. They worked fine after that; the sound was good, but I wouldn’t say these are noise cancelling, which isn't important to me. I Iike to know when someone is talking to me. Charging is easy, connection to my devices is quick, and I like the visual display on my screen when they become connected. The price was reduced which is why I bought them, and they’re good value for the money.

title: Low quality it seems
body: So I have just bought these AirPods and have had them for a few months now. Normal routine gym and around the house. I’ve already had bring them in to Apple for repair. But I did get Apple care thinking it would be a easy repair or trade out. Nah, I’ve been waiting a week and change for my AirPods. Overall they seem very cheaply made unfortunately. The quality is about the same of my $10 gym head phones that I have had for a few years now, yes they still work.But there is some positive things. They work great for iPhones when they work. Fairly easy to control and change songs by a tap of my ear.Overall I would not recommend this product hopefully the newer AirPods are better.

title: Only last a handful of months before problems.
body: I use these for high intensity, sweaty exercise almost every day. I’ve bought these twice. They are great for a few months and then quickly deteriorate to the point where one or both will not charge. You think they are charging based on the indicator light but you’ll be disappointed to find one didn’t connect and charge up. Then, there’s a feature where when you flip open the lid case, a dialogue box pops open on your iPhone and displays the charging levels of each AirPod and the case. That feature will begin to stop working over time as well for no apparent reason. I would not spend so much money on these and go with a half price competitor if I can’t expect these to last more than 4-5 months.

title: The sound is not as good as wired earbuds
body: I hesitated a lot on reviewing this item for fear I wasn't being fair to the product, but I now can say with confidence that the sound out of these wireless airpods is definitely inferior to the wired earbuds for iphone. It's great that there are no wires attached and that's a plus. I use them mostly when I am watching some series on my ipad, while walking on the treadmill. As far as noise cancellation, it doesn't work because I can still hear the treadmill in the background. Could my airpods be defective? My son has a pair that he swears cancel all the outside noise, so maybe I got a floozie.As an alternative, I am still using the wired earbuds while I walk on the treadmill

title: Very Faulty after awhile
body: I purchased my AirPods almost 2 years ago. At first they worked great but the common faulty problems of the right AirPod not charging or holding a charge is a major problem for such a high price tag. Also if you don’t have the most recent update only one will work for some odd reason.There will be multiple times I put the AirPods in the case and when going to use them would only get sound from the left. This seems to be a larger problem since the internet community all mention the same problem.I replaced them with the Bose AirPods and so far just great. If you can live without Siri speaking every text message to you I would go that route and save the money

title: Garbage.
body: the sound SOUNDS okay, but it's so quiet. there's no noise cancelation at all. that shouldn't even be a category. the battery life started good and has been rapidly decreasing. sometimes they wont link to my phone. sometimes they randomly disconnect. if I turn the volume down for a second when I turn it back up the volume is still quiet until I put them back in the case for a minute. then the volume bar is still all the way down but when I turn it up again it gets louder. same thing if you turn it up accidentally and try to turn it down. I've only had them for 6 months, and I don't even use them that much. I had a better experience with 30$ Bluetooth earbuds from home depot.

title: Unreliable, in my case :>(
body: These were great for 5-3/4 months. Then the charging got wonky. Suddenly, after leaving the airpods charging overnight, one or the other airpod would give me a low battery warning 5 minutes after removing them from the case. Then the next charge would be fine. Repeat whole process. Very disappointed in this product. Because of COVID, I can't take them into the local Apple Store. Instead, I get a $220 hold on my credit card, and wait for a refurb unit. I should have the hold removed once they get my defective unit back. I won't buy these again. If you do buy them, I strongly recommend the AppleCare (which you must buy within the first 60 days of placing your order.)

title: NOT a good match for Android users
body: Arrrrrgh! After a long time putting off this purchase because I was unsure whether they would pair well with my Galaxy phone, I finally relented. Only to be disappointed.The Bluetooth pairing often drops or is sketchy. When that happens, I have to carry my phone close to my ears to catch the signal. Which, essentially, eliminates the need for the earbuds.When I went to return them, Amazon offered tech support. Which was great because it fixed the problem... for about 2 days. But by the time I got back around to returning them, the window had closed.Very disappointed that I'm now stuck with a lightly used product I can't return.

title: Easy to use, Reliable, Best Call Quality but ....
body: LOOKS:-----------This is either Love it or Hate it at first sight kind of design. Some do love the way it looks, but many still criticize it for the way it hangs outside of your ears.The materials used are well thought for its application. The case is small, yet secure. Magnetic clasp for the lid and attachment for the pods themselves are top notch.FIT:-----Another commonly debated topic about the AirPods is the FIT. Many do not find it secure. At best they are a loose fit. However, I have put this through the pace at Gym, and Daily life and it has never come off on its own unintended.SETUP:----------Say it with me, "1-2-3". By the time you finish saying it, you are already connected and ready to go. Apple sets the benchmark for the Ease of setup and use. To the extent where Samsung has started mimicking the same process on their Galaxy Bud lineup.BATTERY LIFE:--------------------Yet another benchmark. 5 hours of listening/talk time per charge is fantastic. Never ran out of juice at any given time during its use. If one ever does, 15 minutes of charge in the case gives you 3 hours of listening time.In my experience, Apple has always downplayed the Battery Life. So in real life I got more than the stated times on the spec sheet.CHARGING CASE:--------------------------Well pocketable and convenient. Charges fast, and keeps the Pods themselves charged. Simply does what it is designed to do; efficiently.Easily scratched. Expect your blue jean colors to stick to it overtime. Needs regular cleaning to keep it pristine.CALL QUALITY:---------------------Phenomenal. Terrific. The benchmark. With all the bluetooth earbuds that I've used, including dedicated Bluetooth headsets from the past and present; this one sounds the best when it comes to Microphone quality. The callers that I am talking to never suspected that I am using a headset. Its that good.Stereo call playback is also a welcome bonus. The phone call sounds natural and true to life through this headset.SOUND/AUDIO QUALITY:-----------------------------------The major drawback of the AirPods is its overall Audio quality. Although the sound quality is well above what is needed for a phone call, the overall Sonic quality is lacking for Music use.Sound is open and vibrant, but lacks in Bass severely (No, I do not prefer too much bass; I seek balanced audio signature). Usually this is where I would go into detail about the sound signature as an audiophile. However, the product simply doesn't have the merits to warrant the time. If I were to keep it short and simple, the sound quality is "Meh". The EarPods that came packaged with your iPhone sounds very similar.The open design of the AirPods also allows for the ambient noises to come through and diminish any very little bass that is present. GYM is a classic example where this headset fails (background music playing, various equipments chiming left and right). It lets every sound through as there is no isolation between the outside and your ear canal. You will be using higher volumes than you are used to with other headsets of this class.PRICE:---------The asking price is a bit too much for what it offers. You may find other products in the market that offers more for the same money or less.The audio signature is not that different than the 29 dollar (Free with your iPhone purchase) EarPods.The difficult question to ask is, is it worth 160 dollars to just snip the cables off of your EarPods?If the convenience means a ton to you, then Yes. But for many the answer will be a Hard NO.VERDICT:-----------If you are an audiophile that expects higher fidelity out of your headsets, then the AirPods are not for you.Although AirPods will not win wars with other headsets when it comes to sound quality, it sets the benchmark and performs terrific when it comes to everyday usage and reliability. The overall user experience outweighs the only poor aspect of the AirPods (Sound Quality).Hence, If you are a novice user, you'll feel home with this product.

title: Still not up to the level of quality one would expect from a $140 pair of earbuds.
body: I am comparing these to my ~$40 SoundPEATs Q29 model i bought 2-3 years ago. Its almost as the the pros and cons for these two competing earbuds are reversed.Apple AirPod PROS:Battery Life: ~5 hrs listening time. And the case holds charge for, i don't even know how many charges. I haven't counted but its significantly higher than the SoundPEATS.Ease of Charging: SO SIMPLE. The magnet inside secures the airpods to the charging electrodes. The soundpeats lose their connection to the electrodes constantly so it never fully charges either earbud. I would have to constantly twerk and manipulate the buds in the case to make them charge.Ease of Connecting: The chip helps them connect without any problems.Audio Latency while watching video: NONE!!Custimization: Really like that you can customize the controls for either right or left AirPod. However, would like, more gestures I'd like to customize. Like, instead of just a 2 tap, also have a 3-tap. That way you can customize 4 controls. I'd really like to be able to repeat a song, or turn up and down the volume.Apple AirPod MEHS:Audio Quality: Just a little better than the SoundPEATs... not much but it is noticeable.Apple AirPod CONS:Connectivity: Because of the Apple's forced upgrading, we can't use these on devices running anything before iOS10. That means I can't connect to my First Gen iPad. (the thing is still chugging along and great for streaming NetFlix, Hulu, and Videos.Comfort: The SoundPEATs were super comfortable and fit snuggly in the ear. The shape of the AirPods bothers my earholes. I even purchased some vinyl condoms with earplugs to cover it up. It makes it more comfortable, but its still noticeably more uncomfortable. Also, those vinyl covers don't allow the AirPods to fit in the case, so I have to remove them all the time.... which isn't too bad since I ended having to do that with the SoundPEATS because I couldn't get them to charge properly with the earbud cushion? on.Look/Style: The AirPods look dumb as hell. The SoundPEATs are small and round and fit right in the ear and don't have a white wang hanging down over your earlobe.Dropped Connection/Choppy Audio: Ok. Here I was thinking that the AirPods would correct this issue with the SoundPEATs. When I'm oustide working in the yard, audio was choppy regardless if the phone was 10 feet away or in my pocket. Nothing I did seemed to correct it. It was like this with the SoundPEATS. I THOUGHT that the chip in the AirPods would keep this from happening. NOPE. SAME PROBLEM PERSISTED and was made worse by the fact that they were 3.5x more expensive!!!!! Work fine in doors and while I'm at work in the lab. Must be some sort of interference outside. But really, Come on. Its 2019. GET IT TOGETHER.Exterior Audio: UGH The AirPods fail spectacularly here. Not only do they get choppy while I'm out doing yard work, but because they don't have a rubber tip that seals up the ear canal like the SoundPEATs, that means I get to hear the FULL sound of my lawnmower and weedwacker and it drowns out my jams. And also my nagging boss at work and rando plebians i come across in my travels. Also this means they will be practically useless on an airplane. The SoundPEATs helped block out the engine drone and crying and shouting babies, children, and MAGA douchenozzles.Overall, I like them enough to keep using them for regular use but I will need to go back to my SoundPeats for airplane travel and yardwork. Son of a....

title: Love Hate Relationship
body: I'm fully in the Apple Ecosystem: iPhone, iPad, Mac. It was time to replace my wireless headphones and heard so much about the Airpods and so many friends love them, decided to stay Apple. Indeed Airpods work great with Apple products. If that's all you're going to use them for, don't hesitate. I've bought and returned 3 other airbud products. Primary issue was the device staying in the ear, then sound quality. Airpods stay in your ears and the sound is very good for the price point.HOWEVER........I'm primarily interested in wireless help with hearing especially my TVs. I do not have Apple TV. One TV is a smart Samsung with Bluetooth and the other is Panasonic that I've added a Bluetooth transmitter. Anything that's not Apple has no issue pairing with the TVs. When it comes to pairing the Airpods, my blood pressure rises each time. Sometimes they pair, sometimes not. But that's not the real issue. The Airpods do not have independent sound control. If you're using an Apple device, you control the sound through the device like phone, watch etc. However, my TVs (two major brands) do not have volume control for output. Very few TVs do. So if the volume is too high or too low, I need to disconnect from the TV, pair with an Apple device, get the volume where I think I want it, then disconnect, then re-pair with the TV and hope the volume is somewhere near where I like it.I spoke with Apple about this and their response is buy an Apple TV device. That way the Airpod will pair and sound can be controlled. In my case, I would need to buy 2 Apple TV devices. The low priced airbuds come with volume control, apparently there isn't any technology magic required. Apple is choosing to not include.I'm still undecided about returning the Airpods. The fit, comfort and sound are well worth the price. The volume annoyance is a reminder each time I use these things that Apple is not the customer focused/design company we think they are. Before I bought the Airpods, I spoke with 3 friends who use and highly recommend. Each one told me I would be able to control the volume with my iPhone or iPad. After talking with Apple, that is not the case.While on that subject, since my last Apple purchase this is the first time in about 18 months that I've needed to talk with Apple support. What has happened over there? Four calls in two weeks; each time I feel like I'm speaking with someone that is just reading a script and doesn't really understand the issue. The solution to most any issue seems to be, buy something else Apple. As a long time shareholder who's gone through a lot of stock splits, I appreciate the focus on Apple products but come on, there's more solutions to improving an Apple product than buying another Apple product.

title: Sound = 10, Battery = 10, Comfort = 2
body: It has been quite a while since I've bought a set of Apple earbuds to use as my primary form of listen-wear. I truly miss the design of the Apple earbuds from the pre-2010 iPod days. I used the heck out of them as a kid, and thought the 2nd generation AirPods would hopefully be the closest thing to that. For me, they aren't...Sound quality is as good as I always thought the Apple headphones were, nothing really lost there.Battery life is fantastic, though admittedly, I haven't really charged them since I initially charged them when I first got them and it's been several days (not continuous use)...so...take that for what it's worth...Comfort is the absolute biggest miss here. You have to shove these things down into your ear to get optimal hearing and my ears are not a fan of that. If you look up the external parts of the ear, the "Helix Crus" part of my outer ear is so irritated that once I take the AirPods out, there's no way they are going back in. (So just leave them in then)....well...that's not always possible, and because they hurt so much, I find myself trying to adjust to make them more comfortable, and that just doesn't work.(So get some earbud cushions....) Got 'em...I got a whole pack of 20 or so from Amazon that people were adamant would make the AirPods more comfortable based on their own experience......didn't work. You could say in some regard that they helped to some degree, but they definitely didn't take away the uncomfortability of the AirPods, and if it doesn't eliminate the comfort issue completely, they might as well not even be there...(Could it just be your ears....?) YES, it absolutely could just be MY ears. Ears come in all different sizes and some may not even notice these things are in their ears - that's what I was hoping for...but sadly didn't achieve. Perhaps if there was a smaller pair, the discomfort may not be such a problem, but obviously that's illogical.I also had an issue with getting the sound to play on them from my phone. Bluetooth was enabled, Airpods were showing as "Connected", but the sound wouldn't switch from my phone speaker to the AirPods. I can't remember what all I had to do in order to finagle that....but it took some doing, and no other non-Apple product that I've ever used has had this issue...and if you research it, it sounds like it's a common issue with no real straight-forward resolution.It's truly a shame. I could have done with lesser quality sound and even poorer battery life in exchange for greater comfort. Apple hits the mark on just about everything here except that one VERY important aspect, so buy at your own risk. I probably won't be using these the way that I thought I was going to be because of it... $100 down...

title: Decent but not always functional...
body: AirPods are definitely very convenient to use, especially if you like to listen to music or videos while you’re working/moving around. Sound quality is standard and pretty much the same as the wired earbuds. The battery life is really good and you could listen for the entire day relatively uninterrupted if you use one AirPod and swap when the battery runs low. Even when I don’t use them for several days, the battery doesn’t drain so they can last a long while if you leave them around. However, there’s a frustrating issue that I’ve encountered more times than should be acceptable for rather highly priced earbuds. This is mostly relevant if you tend to only use one earbud at a time though; it’s not a major issue if you normally use both at the same time.The first pair I ordered came brand new and sealed, but within a few hours, they had already stopped working properly. The right AirPod would not connect unless the left was also in use. Troubleshooting guides suggested resetting the AirPods, reconnecting, testing on other devices, and etc., but nothing fixed the issue, and it was consistent throughout multiple devices (iPhone, iPad, PC). I ended up returning these and requesting a replacement.The second pair I received, which is the one I currently own, worked fine for the first couple weeks. However, the issue from the previous pair started occurring with these as well. I decided to keep these despite the problem because unlike the first set, these seemed to function properly at least sometimes, so they weren’t consistently dysfunctional. Resetting the AirPods also temporarily resolves the issue, but this isn’t the most convenient thing to do when it 1. happens rather frequently (pretty much daily) and 2. I have a case, and you need to hold onto the button on the back of the charging case to reset/reconnect. Also, weirdly enough, this pair had the connection issues with both AirPods. Sometimes the right wouldn’t work without the left, and other times the left wouldn’t work without the right. It’s probably a weird coincidence, but it always seems like the one I want to use at any given moment is the earbud that doesn’t want to connect without the other.Anyway, I kept the second pair because they were functioning well enough, and I had purchased them for $99 which I guess isn’t too awful for brand new and legitimate Apple AirPods. But if the price was higher and I knew about this connectivity issue, which seems to be rather common judging from all the troubleshooting forums I found, I probably wouldn’t make the purchase.

title: Not magical, but still the best.
body: There are so many things AirPods get right but fails to deliver a perfect product.First, connectivity is not what people make it out to be. Yes, pairing is as simple as opening the case, unlocking your iPhone and pressing the button. But changing from my iPhone to my iPad and to my MacBook is definitely not a breeze. You have to go to home screen, go to settings, bluetooth, and then choose AirPods. A 4-step process is not "magical" as they advertised. The actual connection though, it's absolutely awesome. I can leave my phone in the car (not that I do that on purpose) and still walk around my house listening to music without so much as a hiccup, ever.The case is the perfect size and so well thought out that I can't be without it in my pocket, even when I don't use it. I just miss having that darn thing just to play with it. Each bud enters perfectly without thought into the case and immediately starts charging. Every time I take one out it's ready to go and resumes playing when I put it on my ear. That is magical. The issue? I just can't keep the case clean, I'm sort of a maniac about keeping my stuff looking new but this thing just won't let itself be maintained, so there are stains inside the charging case that won't come off.Even though I'm a musician and I consider myself a little bit of an audiophile (not all the way though) the sub-par audio quality actually does not bug me, that is not it's main purpose. I can wear my AirPods for long periods of time without pain or discomfort, and having a good enough music quality is, well, enough.Making and taking calls with the AirPods are one of the main features I was looking for when I bought them as my hands are always occupied and they don't fail at all. The call is crystal clear and I never had any complaints about the other person not hearing me clearly. I just wish I could change from one Apple device to the other "magically", but alas I have to go to settings and yada yada (the 4-step process).The lack of volume control is another heavy caveat in my book. I can skip a song with a tap and call out Siri with another, but I should be able to control the volume without taking out my phone (first-world problems, yeah).In conclusion, I haven't found any product that comes near to this one, but it's not nearly a perfect or "magical" product as Apple and their infallible marketing team advertise.

title: Perhaps not the best option for serious runners
body: I only use them for running but while doing so there's a few features that I like to have by the quick press of the button/ear bud instead of fumbling for my phone: skip forward, skip back, pause/play, adjust volume and a nice bonus that is rarely used is calling up siri. While you can customize each of the ear buds to perform one of these actions, you can only pick two, and volume adjust is not an option which is a huge problem for me while running at times (i.e. going into and out of traffic areas). The sound quality and battery life is excellent as you'd expect for an apply product but w/o these other capabilities I'm considering returning them because they just won't be used that much and I feel as though the lack of controls will make them annoying. It is nice that they pause automatically when you take them out but sometimes I want a quick tap instead. They also aren't super sensitive so sometimes I double tap and it doesn't do the desired function, then I'm tapping again a little harder. I can't figure out if I'm not hitting it hard enough, quick enough, or the wrong spot. When you're running you really don't want to mess with this too much, it needs to be quick and responsive. I realize this is an earlier generation but not sure why they didn't at least incorporate a single tap and double tap option to provide more features.I was replacing some much cheaper Mifa X17 buds that only lasted about a year before I started having problems and a few months later they might be totally dead for good (at least one of the two buds). But for the price, I could get 3-4 of those for the same price as these, still have pretty good quality sound and all the features I need for running plus it has wireless charging. I mean that's like 3-5 yr of earbuds for the same price with much more features... I'm not sure I can ignore that. Just another option to consider depending on what your intended use is.

title: I just don't understand the hype
body: I finally made the switch to Airpods from my 2016 Jabra Elite 65t and it most certainly been a downgrade. Here are my reasons for feeling so:1. Battery life is atrocious. With daily use I would charge my Jabra's once a week. They had excellent battery life and the charge case was great with keeping them ready to go. With my Airpods I have to charge daily. Full being fully charged I took them on an hour bike ride and maybe used sporadically throughout the day and by night I had the warning of only having 5% battery, after being in the case.2. Sound quality is mediocre. The Jabra's fit in-ear and had an element of noise cancellation. The Airpods sit in your ear but have no noise cancellation. Aside from that, the my old Jabra's had far superior sound. There's not much else to say, they're just better than the Airpods here.3. Price. I paid $150 for the 2nd gen Airpods at Best Buy. I think they're down to $128 now on Amazon. The newer model of Jabra Elite (the 75t) is currently $129 on Jabra's website. Airpods are severely overpriced for what you get and I have the feeling if you looped in other competitors (Bose, Sony, Sennheiser, Klipsch) you would find similar results.Overall the one Pro I'll give the Airpods is connectivity. I own an iPhone, iPod, and iMac and the Airpods connect very quickly to Apple products. I have been an Apple user since the early 2000s so they've gotten their money out of me from brand loyalty but their quality has really dropped in recent years. I think the only reason they continue to get glowing reviews is based on this loyalty to them. I would strongly encourage anyone looking at these Airpods to check out some of the competitors first. You will find vastly superior earbuds for the same or lower price. But on the other hand you won't be one of the cool kids with white buds hanging off your earlobes.

title: Headphones are so-so, if you don't have an iPhone I'd look elsewhere
body: As most Apple products are, these work pretty well. I know this isn't the case for everyone, but these fit in my ears perfectly and comfortably. I can wear them for hours at a time without noticing them. I've been using these with an Android phone with some interesting results.Things I love about them:- How quickly they connect. These things connect almost immediately once you open the lid for the case!- You can use either one on their own if you want, it doesn't matter which- The call quality is fantastic! I love taking calls on these things!- The range is pretty great. I can set my phone down and walk around nearby rooms most of the time without any issue.Things I don't love:- The audio quality is alright. It's about the same as the wired EarPods. Not bad, but nothing amazing.- It's too easy to hear what's going on around me. They have no noise cancellation whatsoever. I could literally carry on full conversations with people while listening to these if I wanted.- Connectivity issues. The range is usually good, but sometimes the connectivity is awful. I can walk around with my phone in my pocket and put my hand over my phone, and these will skip in and out. Sometimes they'll even get out of sync a little bit. This has happened extensively. I made a warranty claim on them and Apple said they couldn't find any problems so they sent them back, and I still have those issues. I also wonder how much this has to do with my phone though, because my phone (Samsung Galaxy S9) Bluetooth doesn't seem the strongest sometimes.If you have an iPhone, I'd recommend giving them a go. But I think there are definitely better deals out there.

title: Great hardware, but caution for any Samsung S10 users
body: Great bit of hardware - slim form factor, but otherwise it's like a tiny box that you can take with you anywhere.Earbuds themselves are great, though the fit has me concerned if I'm going to do anything active... but otherwise great for lounging activities. Very comfortable since it's so lightweight, which is huge.Now if you're like me and had an S10 before buying these... you might be in for some frustration.Without a firmware update, when paired via Bluetooth the earbuds initially don't output audio. You can fix that by setting in the Bluetooth config to use the device for calls/audio... THIS SETS THE VOLUME TO MAX FOR WHATEVER GOD FORSAKEN REASON (!). When you change the volume after setting it to use the earbuds, the volume is good... until about 10 seconds later when the Bluetooth disconnects and you need to repair, and then experience the joys of random MAX VOLUME blasting again.To fix that, you can do a firmware upgrade that resolves these issues. And in short the earbuds are unusable with an S10 until you do it.But the firmware update requires iOS -- so unless you specifically have an iPad or iPhone (Mac doesn't work), you'll need to borrow someone's device.To top it off, there's no way to manually trigger it -- it just happens automatically once paired with the case charging by the device, so you'll probably need to borrow their device for several hours if you don't own one. For reference, the faulty firmware is the 1A661 firmware, and there's no way to tell what firmware you have unless you check it with an iOS device.But hey once you resolve the above it's great hardware.

title: Nice, but not good enough.
body: I like mobile Apple products and quality. When I first got these (I did not buy them online. I went to an Apple dealer and bought them directly.) I was impressed. But I owned them for 2 years now and it goes downhill. I am reviewing the first generation Air-pods and not the later version.Pros:1. They are faithful with connecting each time I pull them from the case and place in my ears. There are few problems with that as there are with other Bluetooth devices.2. They fit well in the "ear-well" and stay. I have an anomaly of a smaller left ear well and canal so the device will shift some. But that is not the fault of the device design.3. I wore them when I go walking/jogging. They stay in with no problem.4. I tend not to listen to as much music as talk so the quality of sound is good enough for me.Cons:1. After time the battery life diminishes. As I said, I owned them for 2 years now and I may get 1-2 hours out of them before I need to recharge. To compensate, I use one in one ear and when it begins to lose power, I pull the second from the case, place it in the other ear and ensure it is connected before removing the drained one. Considering the price, I would expect it to last longer. I now use a bone conduction audio device for exercising. It allows me to hear outside noises rather than muffling them with something at my ear canal, stays put and last longer.I gave 4 stars on sound quality because I know it is not great. But it is good enough.I give 3 stars for noise cancellation because these are really too small to adequately muffle exterior sounds.

title: They're OK. I wish I bought them elsewhere though.
body: I was hesitant to purchase Airpods because of the steep price so when I saw there was a deal a few months ago (early 2020) I hopped on the bandwagon! Since I have many Apple products, these DO make it easier to sync up to my Macbook, iPhone and iPad. However, even with that being the case the airpods I received were defective. I spent hours talking to customer service and at the Apple Store (pre-COVID) and they ended up having to replace the case and one of the airpods. At times I still have issues with connectivity and the microphone isn't the greatest. Sometimes my airpods would be in the case and I wasn't trying to connect to my phone yet the music would play WHILE THEY WERE IN THE CASE. I even reported this to the apple care team and my customer service representative told me it was something they'd never seen or heard about before. (Seriously wish I was making this up, I just wanted to be able to have wireless earbuds).I'll admit that I don't fully regret my purchase, but regret buying it via Amazon instead of from the Apple Store or a major retailer (i.e. Best Buy). Not sure if my logic makes sense, but I feel like there would've have been less of a chance of getting a faulty item.TLR; Airpods aren't a bad investment, but be wary and monitor your item if you buy it via Amazon. ALSO they aren't noise cancelling and the microphone isn't the greatest. The sound is OK but if you want to still spend a good amount of money, you probably can get better quality headphones elsewhere. The best feature to me is that it is super connected with my apple products and I can tap to change the songs, etc.

title: slips out of my ears so fast even while I am not moving
body: I like the quality of Apple products. I wanted to upgrade from my first generation pods which have been around my home for many years and have been used on a daily basis. I am very happy with the connectivity to different connected devices back and forth. Battery life is not an issue for me since the charging case can always be plugged. BUT, and this is a big BUT, this one slips out of ear so easily within minutes. I did look up the reviews before buying this on sale for $79. Most reviewers said they dont have the issue while some reviewers faced the issue I am currently having. At least, with the first generation, silicone tips were somewhat helpful to make them stay in my ears for a while and provided a decent noise cancellation because of that. This one does not have the silicon tips, and has a weird design that wont fit my ears or my wife's ears. May be I am one of those outside the bell curve for the ear fit with these Airpods. I am never going to buy these airpods again. Some times, i dont feel like using over the head earphones and wanted these ones to really work out for me. But unfortunately, i have to still resort to the over the ear headphones (which obviously provide better sound quality and great noise cancellation- i know it is not an apple to apple comparison). I currently have Bose QC35 (using for many years) and sony-WH1000XM3 (using since a year or so) that are far superior than this. At a local store I tried some sports headphones (around the ear) called SHOKZ that actually is better than this Airpod because it goes around your ear and connected behind your neck. I will be returning this today!

title: Just okay…I will likely replace them with a different brand.
body: I really debated between these and the pro version but ultimately, I didn't feel I needed the adjustable fit or the full noise cancellation. I'm so happy with this purchase! The battery life is great (longer lasting than the pro version actually), charging is a breeze, small and compact so they go with me everywhere. I love them. I'm sure the wireless ones and the pro ones are really good, but these still hold their own in my opinion and are a great price for anyone who doesn't need the features of the pro version. I think wireless charging is kind of a gimmick anyway so I don't care about that in the least. I wish I'd bought them sooner.UPDATE: I’ve now been using these for 16-months and I took off one star because they don’t connect and switch seamlessly between my iPad and iPhone, which is the main reason I purchased these over a different brand. Also, they often don’t auto-connect to even one of them to start with. They are supposed to connect as soon as you put them in your ears to whichever of your devices is activated or whichever one you used last (you can adjust that in settings) but they don’t. I often have to go into the settings and manually connect them. They also often only connect in one ear - hugely annoying! I’ve had them randomly disconnect when on a phone call or watching something as well. They worked great for about six months but now I constantly have issues with them so I’m looking at purchasing another pair of wireless earbuds from a recognizable brand that is known for their sound quality. Sorry, Apple.

title: Make sure you know what you’re buying
body: I feel like I got scammed! Advertisement and the sale for the AirPods 2nd gen is as follows: one pair wireless charging more expensive, they second pair cheaper and plug in. The way you choose your AirPods from this seller is the same so it’s easy to come to the assumption that both are 2nd gen. So I feel like the labeling was on purpose to sale (Obsolete merchandise) which cost me money and I ended up buying a product I didn’t want. I feel taken advantage of as a consumer I’ve never owned AirPods because they’re pricey however being that my iPad and my iPhone have different charging ports it’s hard to have just one pair of headphones for them I broke down and decided it was worth the convenience. Its sellers like this one that made me want to buy directly from the website and as soon as Amazon reopens it’s call center I plan to make a full complaint. Please know what you’re buying be careful today sellers will take Advantage of consumers With no sense of guilt after all it’s nothing but profit for them. I gave this product three stars because it is an Apple product it was in new condition although it was not what I thought I purchased. Don’t trust amazon to make sellers sell quality products, not everything you buy comes from them. The pair I current own work fine but I just paid 140 plus tax for first generation!!!! Get me once shame on you!!!

title: Ok?
