body: The Apple AirPods have received a lot of hype and attention since their release, but unfortunately, they do not live up to the high expectations. One of the most significant issues with the AirPods is their battery life. While Apple claims that the AirPods can last up to five hours on a single charge, many users have reported that they do not even last that long.Furthermore, the charging case that comes with the AirPods only provides an additional 24 hours of battery life, which is not enough for many people who use their AirPods on a regular basis. The charging case is also quite bulky and can be inconvenient to carry around.Another issue with the AirPods is their fit. While some users find them comfortable, others have reported that they fall out of their ears easily or do not fit properly, making them uncomfortable to wear for long periods.Additionally, the sound quality of the AirPods is not as impressive as some other wireless earbuds on the market. Many users have reported that the sound is not as clear or powerful as they would like, and there is no noise-cancellation feature.Overall, while the AirPods may be convenient and stylish, they are not worth the high price tag for many users due to their subpar battery life, fit issues, and mediocre sound quality.

title: This is my second set of Apple earbuds
body: I have always worn an earbud in my ear. I am a working mom who is always on the go, always on the move, and always working. I love to listen to music or Audible while working, driving, house chores, etc., and because of this, you will always find one earbud in my ear at all times. I've been a huge Apple fan for years now, but I am getting tired of the dying of products after a year or so.I had a previous pair of Airpods, and literally after one year of use (please keep in mind I wear these everyday), no one can hear me. I can hear everything anyone says, but no one can hear me on either Airpod. My tapping to start and stop audio has failed in one pod also. I assumed they were dirty, and had someone clean them, but no amount of cleaning would get the other person to hear me. So on to #2 set of Airpods. Again, a year later, same issue. I can hear fine, but no one can hear me. Tapping to start and stop is starting to fail again also.I love my Airpods, like the fit as I'm a small female, and these have never fallen out of my ears, and I can wear them all day in relative comfort. But I'm over spending money on expensive earbuds that die a year later.I think I'm going to try the Samsung earbuds this time around, and see if I can't get some lasting ability with those.

title: Hyped but worth it?
body: I was a hold out for years on these. Finally I caved because having them would come in handy during the day so I could listen to my podcasts or music without disturbing anyone else in my house (I work from home) and it was easier to answer calls obviously as well.They fit well in my ears and the sound is great:Cons are the battery life. At a full charge of both airpods and my charging case I still only get around 5 hours before they’re giving me the warning beep. Then one will die 30 min or more before the other.The other big issue for me is how far I can walk away from my phone before a caller can no longer hear me. I walk from my office where my phone is to my bedroom to grab something and they immediately realize I’ve walked too far, mind you I live in an apartment and they’re on the same floor. Only two rooms (living room and kitchen) separate the bedroom from the office. Perhaps it’s interference from having brick exterior walls but that doesn’t seem plausible when the interior walls are plaster. I deal with it and either now tell the caller to hold on while I walk away and walk back closer or throw my phone in a pocket so it’s near to me.If the battery life was better they would easily go up to 4 stars for me if not 5.

title: Sound quality is not so great
body: I bought these so I could listen to music while out on walks with my dog and Zoom calls. After using them for a few months, I have some thoughts on this. I have a pair of over the head headphones that are noise cancelling. They were cheap relative to the air pods and they deliver great sounds when I play music on them. The airpods were only okay at 3x the price of the headphone. They don't fall out of my ear while walking or jogging as some people have reported, but if I go to tuck my hair behind my ear or rest my head on something, they fall out because of the long tail it has on them. The AirPod pro looks like it would sit better in your ear and not get easily knocked out since it has a much shorter tail. All that said, I tried cheaper blue tooth ear pods and they were terrible and kept disconnecting from my phone so I gave this 3 stars because the sound is good enough for walks and stays connected to the bluetooth. The battery life for using it for calls is pretty short (around 2 hours) but I've never run the battery out on them listening to music. The wireless charging case recharges them very fast and I consider that a huge plus. The AirPod pro is likely much better but I'd still buy these over the AirPod 2 just because of the huge price difference.

title: Won’t stay in my ear
body: The AirPods arrived in great condition, nice packaging but they do not stay put in my ear at all. I attempted to wear one around the house while doing light chores and it fell out several times and required adjusting multiple times. I should have known that these wouldn’t work for me, my regular corded earbuds do the same thing. I don’t imagine that I’d be able to workout or do much of anything without them falling out. I’ll be returning them and purchasing a much more inexpensive pair that allows me to adjust the ear cover for a better fit. Good product for most, but not a good fit for meUpdate 5/14/20So these are advertised as being the latest generation. My case that came with the AirPods have a model number that would indicate the case is 1st generation. The AirPods have different model numbers that would indicate that they weren’t manufactured as pair. There’s something fishy with these although they work. Check your numbers......my settings on my phone show a model number that is in fact different than what’s printed on the actual device. I wouldn’t be surprised if these were refurbished, all the different serial numbers confirm that this wasn’t an original pair because they have different manufacture dates

title: Not quite what I was expecting.
body: I own a Pixel 2XL for personal but have an iPhone 8 for business as does my wife. I ordered the Air Pods 1) because they were on sales and, 2) for business calls expecting high end quality as Apple is known for. First, the quality build of the product is excellent. They connect easily to Bluetooth and I must say I found the sound quality better than expected. However, the lack of a rubberized "ear piece" causes them to sit on the ear rather than in it. This diminishes noise cancellation. It also feels like they will fall out if I move around too much but admittedly, that is a personal preference and based on my ear "anatomy". What surprised me most (and disappointed) was the short battery life compared to other non-Apple ear pods I have owned. The specs say 5-hours (which is short to begin with) but tests reviews I have read say 4.2 hours at best. Not for me. I'm barely getting 3-hours. So for someone who is constantly on conference calls, I'm forced to switch back to the wired ear pods. And when one considers what Apple charges $159.00 plus (when not on sale) for this and all its products, the value just isn't there. Unless you are an Apple fanatic, there are better wireless ear pods on the market that offer a better value.

title: Can't control volume without stupid Siri
body: I bought these AirPods so I could watch my LG C1 OLED TV, which I found out doesn't let you control bluetooth volume.The only way you can control volume on AirPods is either:1. Through the volume controls on whatever device you're listening to (My LG TV doesn't have volume controls for with bluetooth 😠)2. Saying "Hey Siri, turn volume up/ down". 😠I don't want to say "Hey Siri, turn volume up/down" out loud 200 times during a movie. First, there would be a significant loss of immersion and secondly, it will sound like I'm talking to myself in my apartment like some nut. Especially in the later hours.Dear Apple, can you PRETTY PLEASE WITH SUGAR ON TOP create a little remote control (that includes volume + - buttons) for these AirPods? I bet you would make a LOT of money selling them maybe $10-20 a pop.All I want is to watch my TV quietly while being able to turn the volume up and down with a simple click or tap. Otherwise, I love how they feel (barely reach the ear canal, no itchy ears) and sound (when the volume is at the right level).But because of the volume thing, they're useless. Returning.

title: I am disappointed with the lifespan, but sound cant be beat.
body: I got some G1 airpods back in 2018. I loved the way they sound, and I also value the non-isolating feature. My Airpods now only last about :30 - :40 minutes, and wont charge all the way up now, after only 2 years. I have some high quality wired buds for isolating. It's important to me to be able to hear what's going on around me like people talking to me, security reasons, etc.These sound fantastic to me, but I have been trying to find an alternative, because I am used to better longevity from Apple products. I will pay a premium price, if I get a premium gadget, but at only 2 years, I am disappointed. But, BIG SIGH, Its getting pretty hard to find ANYONE who make non-isolating buds now. No reputable brand that I can find is making them. (C'mon Bose, Klipsh, Cambridge, SOMEBODY, ANYBODY...) I have tried another brand with lots of good reviews and promises of HiFi sound, (and I am not even a HiFi weenie, LOL) but the sound was NOTICEABLY, UNARGUABLY, far worse that AP's.So, here I am doing it again. Please Apple, for the environment, at least : build stuff that LASTS, like I expect from you. Everything else I have from apple AT LEAST lasts...

title: its apple, recommend for frequent apple iphone/ macbook user + frequent wireless head phone user
body: This is design to work with iphone macbook etc. After all Apple desgin their product to work best with their product. It can work with android phone just like a regular bluetooth device but you will lose those features that are unique to apple. And you will be better off buying those generic bluetooth that is much cheaper. Indeed, I do not feel any sound difference between Airpods 2 and those I bought off street. But, if music quality is something you want + noise cancellation feature, go for airpod pro which now I feel it is more worthy. However, 2 main problem with me is that 1. even though I have iphones and macbook, I don't wear headphones so it's a waste to spend on such expensive gadget 2. my friends who reccoomend this to me about how "they sleep without feelign its in ears" but it doesn't work.... I feel strange wearing them, I can definitely feel I am wearing headphone, and the left ear one keeps falling out of my ear on its own!! I was sitting and watching movies, not even moving at all and it slip out every 60s. So ... I will say ear type matters too... If air pods isn't for you and you really want apple, go for the pro.

title: Just okay wireless headphones
body: The gen 2 Apple Airpods are whelming. Starting with the positives: like most Apple products, they have a solid build quality and work well within the Apple ecosystem. They are also lightweight and do not cause ear fatigue like some in-ear options. The charging speed is quite nice as well, as they seem to charge back to full in about 30 minutes. Sound-wise, they are just okay. They have a rather neutral sound and do not have much bass.As for the drawbacks, these are a little lacking compared to other options. The battery life is surprisingly limited for an Apple device, lasting only about 4 hours on one charge. This particular area feels quite underwhelming compared to other options as my other set of wireless earbuds easily lasts twice that amount of time. The Airpods also do not have noise cancellation, so outside noise gets in quite easily. Not ideal for noisy environments. There are also no controls on the Airpods, so this makes pausing music, skipping songs, etc. cumbersome as you will need to always do this from your phone/device.

title: Mic Failure after 9 months of use
body: I bought the Airpods new. they work great for listing to music and phone calls. However, about 9 months in the mic on both airpods have a defect where the caller can barly hear you, or voice recordings using the airpods is very low. Being out of warranty at 9 months the only option is customer paid replacement. After checking the support pages, there are quite a few people reporting the same issue with Airpods. Buying AppleCare for the AirPods at $30 sounds good, but it cost $30 per incident for repair/replacements. In this case it would cost $60 to get two replacement Airpods. $60 for the airpods and $30 for the AppleCare puts it at another $90 for a new pair, when a new pair costs $130. Even though the warrenty is for 3 years, it only covers 2 incidents....and replacing both for the mic issue is 2 incidence. So the 3 year warranty endes at 9 months to get the mic fix...which allows for maybe 2 years of service.Apple needs a recall program for this issue, because $130-$170 for AirPods what will fail at 9 months is a problem.

title: not all that impressed
body: typical apple quality, these airpods are of excellent build and sound. they perform equally well as the corded version, of course without the cord. but the only advantage i found was the lack of cord, not enough benefit to justify the $160 price tag. but i could justify the returning them and that's exactly what i did. i guess i'll just stay with my corded apple ear buds - and I get to keep the extra $160.UPDATE November12: i bought a second pair of these airpods recently thinking maybe i didn't give the first pair enough time to grow on me. i took them on a cycling trip and was reminded why i returned them in the first place: whenever i wanted to change the volume, i had to reach behind in the jersey and make the changes through my iPhone. what a nuisance. i'm glad i returned them. in addition these pods do not pair automatically between a MacBook and iPhone like they do with the watch. they must be reconnected between the two each time you change devices. just could not justify the cost with those drawbacks.

title: Love them minus the popping noise
body: The battery life is OK. Thankfully, I use one at a time and I can put one away to charge and pop another one in my other ear. I wouldn’t suggest wearing both and expecting to wear them for a long period of time. They do charge very quickly though!! I love that. I noticed this popping noise pretty soon after they arrived. Within the first 24 hours. I contacted Amazon customer service about, spoke with a very nice gentleman who assured me he would call the next day to follow up after suggesting I disconnect and re-pair them with my phone. This did help initially but within that 24 hours, the popping/crackling noise was back. Of course, he never followed up. I went ahead and kept them after reading this is an issue with AirPods. Didn’t want to go through the trouble of returning just for it to happen again. Anyways, I figured I’d hit em where it hurts, the review. Despite the noise, they are great. I use them quite a bit BUT if you find another brand that does the same thing for half the price, that makes sense too!

title: Perfectly ok, wish they had more commands.
body: Recently, I decided to switch to wireless earbuds because I keep getting tangled up when I go out for a walk. Apple was the no-brainer choice, as Apple earbuds have always fit fairly comfortably in my ears. I've always enjoyed that their basic headphones don't create a seal or have silicone coating on them as well.They synced up easily. They fit comfortably in my ears. They only have...one command per airpod? Is that my fault for not reading it? Yes. But it is also baffling that it's $129 for airpods when the wired ones that came free with my phone can Pause, Play, Forward, Backward, and Adjust volume with one tiny little piece on the wire.So anyway, these worked exactly as they were supposed to and my purchase experience was pleasant. If you know exactly what you're getting, you'll be happy! I took away two stars because for this much, I could have just bought the knockoffs for $20. Yes, that's on me. But also, the product could be much better.

title: You can find better
body: First of all, the product title says "Latest Model" but I received the air pods generation 1, which may or may not explain my disappointing observations? I had a lot of expectations for these air pods because they were $140. When I used them for the first time, I was shocked at how cloudy/faded out the audio was! It also has very little to no noise cancellation. How do people settle for this? The battery only lasted me roughly 3 hours and then it had to go back into the charging case. The only pros was that these air pods looked nice, the fit was comfortable-- I wouldn't recommend it unless you just want it for the hype. I've bought cheaper wireless earbuds that sounded much clearer and better and lasted me a few days without charging (7-8 hours of use), I just don't think I got my money's worth with these. My recommendation is to definitely do your research on different types of wireless ear buds and what you want out of them before purchasing air pods right away.

title: Works when they work but so many glitches
body: These work well enough but the whole reason I bought them was not wanting to shove my phone in my waist and to walk ten steps away while listening and the range on them really stinks. I have plenty of blue tooth devices and I’m aware they aren’t meant to range a million miles away or anything but wandering into the next room & having them go out is really annoying! Also they won’t connect with my Apple Watch another reason for purchase to cut the phone out of the equation with audibles download to watch with wireless headphones feature. I feel like they’re really glitchy too - randomly refusing to play when tapped and skipping forward when I try to tap to play. Finally the mic is iffy - I’ve had way too many people going “what what what I can’t head you” while talking with them who hear totally fine if I switch to my cars Bluetooth or my cell speaker. When they work they work and the charging case is neat but lots of issues in my opinion too.

title: Should have worked out the bugs prior to releasing
body: I will start by saying I never owned the 1st generation AirPods. I bought my 2nd Gen pair in April 2019. During the first month and a half, both the left and right AirPods (at different times) ended up becoming extremely muffled and Apple had to replace both (for free because of the one-year warranty). Eventually, they ended up swapping out the entire set for a new one, including a new charging case. Even the new one had issues with the right pod being muffled. When I took it into the Apple Store, the rep said that this was a known issues with the AirPods and that the only fix right now is swapping them out for new ones. She also speculated that Apple was so hasty to release a new version that came in different colors, etc. that they did not properly take the time to test for such bugs. I would opt for a 1st generation pair until the 2nd gen bugs get worked out. The minimal new features are not worth the potential hassle of technical issues.

title: Not my favorite purchase over time.
body: I’m a fairly big Apple fanboy. But only because their products are usually high quality and it makes my life easy to just buy Apple brand and not have to put a lot of thought into other brands - and I’ve never been disappointed.Until the AirPods.I use my electronics more than most people, so that could be part of the problem, but I still expect better from Apple than my experience with AirPods.After a few months, my right AirPod would not charge until I very carefully placed it in the pack.After about a year:-my right AirPod’s mic does not work at all-left AirPod mic works sometimes-the “auto ear detection” (when AirPod is removed, media is paused) triggers any time I move instead of when the AirPod is removed.-occasionally AirPod case does not charge if on a wireless charging pad, even though the case says it’s chargingVery disappointed in my experience with airpods.

title: Sound quality not bad
body: Waited a good 5 day’s to write a review as I wanted to give them an honest chance. After being told these are the best and researching a good month. I will say the sound quality isn’t bad. It’s not the best I’ve heard but for wireless headphones there’s not a static in the background like some of the cheaper models. The double tap feature is a little strange as it takes a bit to find that perfect spot. Also it’s double tap being pause and fast forward I haven’t been able to ge the music to skip forward only pause. So that feature I haven’t used all that much. They do slip a bit in your ears so I recommend buying the ear hooks. I bought them and they work pretty well with them. There’s also silicon options for covers to help the slipping. I’d also recommend a case for the charging case as it’s quite small and kinda slippery. So all in all I think they’re a decent pair of headphones. Just don’t go into the purchase expecting perfection.

title: If the wired earpods included in the iphone box don't fit your ears, neither will these!!!
body: I want to like these. They're easy to use, sound decent for music, and they work well for phone calls. But Apple's "one size fits all" scheme doesn't work for me. I should have guessed these wouldn't fit me properly. Apple's wired earpods that come with every iPhone tend to fall out of my ears easily. Why would the wireless version of the same thing be different?Apple really should offer these in more than one size.On battery life, I was on the phone listening to music and/or the news at a reasonable volume for about an hour, then I was on the phone for about two more hours. Indicated battery charge went down from 100% to only 25%. Granted, it's a brand new device, so the batteries might not be quite "broken in" yet, but I don't find that impressive.I'd give these a more of a chance and a longer review, but since they simply don't fit right, they don't work for me. They're going back.

title: Don't buy it
body: The air pods may seem really cool and popular but it is in it's name "air" how is something supposed to stay in your ear if it't always in the air imagine skydiving, the air pod is going to fall out and float in the air like it always does, I mean who would buy this like half the time I would smack my friend on the side of the head thinking a bug was going to crawl into his ear and kill him, he then hits me back and says "You idiot, for the 20th time it my Air-pod"Clearly air pods are just another way for apple to make millions. First their crappy chargers that you had to buy over and over, now these things called air pods which makes everyone who wears them act cool. When really they are being brain washed my the aliens up above making them buy more apple products. One day all air pods will float off into the air and everyone but me will be sad they wasted so much money on a produced to fly away.

title: Good sound, bad fit
body: First the good- sound quality and ease of use are amazing. Yes, this was a genuine Apple product, I verified the serial number before I opened the box. They connected to my phone right away, no weird extra steps to pair. They sound amazing. They are not noise canceling- I thought that wouldn’t be as big of a deal as it is. Now for the reason I’m upgrading to the pro model- the fit is awful. The right AirPod hurts my ear after only a few minutes of use. The pro model has more fit options and noise cancellation. I am glad that I tried these because they are by far the easiest Bluetooth ear buds I’ve ever used, the sound is great, and if they fit better I’d be 100% sold. So there you go. Give them a try for sure, but once you do, you may want the better set. And GET APPLE CARE. I work on the repair side of things and we send them out all the time. I would not think to get these without the added protection.

title: Good product, but the loudness is not noteworthy for me personally
body: Pros: Voice activated with Siri (awesome feature); secure compact charging case; Very good bass; Very good sound quality; Battery life seems good, they were at about 70% when I received them.Cons: Volume is not noteworthy (though the sound quality is very good); though it's on par with other quality ear buds that i've tried. I can hear outside noise;Although you can play and skip songs via touching the ear buds while they're in your ears, you can't adjust the volume by sliding your finger along the earbuds, which is a feature that I can do with my current tic pods.Conclusion: These are not worth $150. My current Tic-Pods that I paid [overpaid] $129 for are equal quality and equal volume. In fairness, i'm not pleased with the volume level of my current Tic Pods, but I was hoping that these AirPods would surpass them in volume level, but they did not.

title: Hmmm..
body: I’ve owned these for a month and debated returning them a few times. 1.) I’m pretty sure the pair I received was not “new” even though they appeared to be. It seemed like someone may have tried them, returned them and they were repackaged to appear new. I thought I might be being paranoid having received multiple used items across Amazon at this point, so I cleaned them and decided to keep. 2.) I am currently waiting on a replacement for the left pod less than a month in. It had a defective battery right out of the box. I’m not sure if that’s Apple’s fault or if someone else got them and returned for the same reason. Either way Apple is replacing it free of charge since it dies within 30 minutes on a full charge (so at least they’re real).I like the AirPods but I’m not sure I trust Amazon with these purchases anymore. I probably wouldn’t buy them on Amazon again.

title: Used - Not So Acceptable
body: Bought a pair for a very low price Used from Amazon Warehouse but listed as Acceptable. When they arrived, there was no cosmetic issue at all, they looked like brand new. But once they started playing..a constant metronome type of click noise was coming from the right airpod and the battery in it would quickly drain. Tried all instructions for resetting, nothing helped. Attempted to process a return but was only able to return and would’ve had to pay much more for the next set I would have purchased. Then I noticed the AirPods were only registered a month earlier, so they were still under warranty. Took them to the apple store and they replaced the clicking airpod right away. Someone must have not been truthful with their initial return and the problem was just passed along to me. But I was able to remedy and keep the amazing deal.

title: Meh audio quality, good connectivity
body: Pros: connectivity is great. Typical apple simplicity in pairing, cracked the case open and whaddyaknow, they were instantly paired. Feature rich when used with my iphone. I do like the auto pause when taking one out, etc.Cons: if you care about audio at all, like even a little bit, you should know the audio quality is mediocre. Music is soulless, flat, almost no bass response. Sound is maybe slightly better than the free earpods they used to give us. Also, I’m not a fan of the tampon hanging from my ear look.If you enjoy music, buy something else. There are much, much better options including the typically overrated beats Powerbeats or for half the price, Creative Outlier Air.If you just want something that works, has convenient features and don’t care about audio or looks, these are for you.

title: Amazing customer service from Amazon
body: Let me start off by saying amazon is amazing with customer service.I had an issue with one of the pods going dead very quick compared to the left one.I called Apple they told me the steps to fix the issue but it didn’t help. They put a hold on my card of $75 and send out a new right bud only.These are only about a week old and already having issues, I will not accept just replacement parts. I expect a new unit.So I called amazon they issued me a full refund or exchange of a brand new unit with absolutely no additional holds on my card.Not only is Apple going down In quality there customer service is horrible. Rerouted to at least 8 different people before getting off the phone 3 hours later.Amazon took care of me without even a phone call. Good job Amazon! You just outshined Apple!

title: 5 stars if they fit your ears
body: I LOVED these headphones for what It was, but they don’t stay in my ears very well. If they did, it’d probably be a five star from me. When I’m walking around and not doing anything they stay in just fine, but I can’t workout in these and even a medium wind will make them feel like they need to be pushed back in constantly. I’d recommend trying them to see if they fit your ears and returning if they don’t. The other functions are great. Decent sound, tapping features for song skipping/Siri/play and pause, double as a mic, and best of all the convenient case that charges the AirPods when stored. Also I find the auto connect feature to apple products flawless, albeit beats have the same feature. If these fit you without falling out, then I would highly recommend. Otherwise, it’s not worth them constantly falling out.

title: Bit better than the previous one, redesign needed.
body: In the box, you will find AirPods with charging case and cable.The case is pretty standard white-colored, fits AirPods, and charges it as it should; nothing special. I liked the magnet, which firmly holds the lid.The Airpods looks the same as the old one. It's not perfect fit for the ear and doesn't seal the ear properly, which causes problems; outside noise is very loud and easily falls off while moving or shifting. On the other side, it is very easy to connect, and the battery lasts longer than the previous one. one thing about AirPods I liked is that I have to connect to one apple device and use it on my any other apple device. I still use it during work out although they are not waterproof or sweat-proof. If you are looking for AirPods for activity and comfort, then I would recommend going for pro.

title: DOESN'T FIT EVERYONE - EAR SHAPES ARE DIFFERENT!
body: As with (in my opinion) all Apple products , the quality is incredibly good. The sound quality is amazing. BIG PROBLEM for me however - My ear shape causes these AirPods to fall out constantly. I have tried everything including rubbing the inside of my ears and the Airpods with Isopropyl alcohol to make sure there was no body oils that could make things slip. Nothing helped. Unless I was pretty much motionless, these things fell out. Trying to run or ride a bike is out of the question. I have ordered an aftermarket rubber tip kit to hopefully help this problem. The reason I write this is to let people know that while the product quality is great, if your ears aren't shaped the right way these AirPods will be useless and a source of great frustration. Especially since they come at a premium price.

title: They were oddly dirty for new airpods..
body: I got these from my husband for my birthday, I've been wanting them so I was super excited!Once I got it all unwrapped a noticed a small black piece of fuzz on the outside of the case. I brushed it off, thinking it came from me and didn't think twice about it. But when I opened the case, there was also some very tiny brown specks of dirt? dust? inside and a piece of dirt on one of them that when I wiped it, it actually left a smudge I had to scrape off with my fingernail. I didn't think to take a picture until I removed the second earbud that also had specks on it and it was hard to get a picture since the specks are tiny (theres three specks by the part that goes into your ear, and two on the longer part).Very strange for new Airpods. Other than that, so far so good.

title: Like most Apple products, they work well until they don't
body: Disclaimer: I didn't want to get these. I would prefer to use wired earbuds. But with the removal of the 3mm headphone jack in all iPhones, I can no longer simultaneously charge the phone and use the earbuds which is a huge problem.So I tried these, which work pretty well most of the time. Audio quality is just ok - I am frequently having people on the line say "what?" because they can't hear me any time there is ambient background noise. A bigger annoyance is about 10% of the time when I answer the phone, the airpods just don't work - they don't pick up the call, but block the call from working on speaker or phone either, so both parties sit there saying "hello?" "hello?" "hello?" until somebody hangs up and tries again.I'd rather just have my headphone jack back,

title: Good but Disfunctional?
body: Hi to anyone reading this! I recently orders these and so far have had three issues with these. First, they would disconnect or my music would turn off if I put my phone more than three inches away from the earbuds themselves; second, my left earbud would not connect but my right one would; and thirdly, now my AirPods won’t connect to my Bluetooth at all -this is probably my fault though because I read somewhere online that if you reset your AirPods and reconnected them to your phone it would help. On the bright side, they came way before they were supposed to and everything else seems functional. If you’ve made it this far, please help me and let me know if you have any solutions to these immediate problems. Was really looking forward to these and ended up not being as joyous as I wanted.

title: They work great when they work  !
body: Very nice . Only ear buds that fit* update * after 6 weeks the batteries wouldn’t charge completely .Apple support directed me 3 hours from my house for repair . When I told her there’s a Best Buy 4 miles from my house , she confirmed that they could handle it ! I was told if the problem was not my fault , they would be replaced on the spot . In reality , if you don’t purchase from Best Buy , they send them in for repair .I really like the product , and am looking forward to their return .** update ** just received repaired airpods . 2 days . Excellent service . They failed again after one use . Same problem . Bestbuy offered to return them again . I declined. I bought a new pair from Bestbuy and I will return the haunted pair to Amazon tomorrow .

title: Perfect for those who loved their wired apple earbuds, fit is the same and not for me
body: So these would have been perfect, if only they fit my ears. The sound quality is pretty good, they connect so easily, they charge fast and last a while, they even stayed in my ears pretty well, but for me they are painful to wear. This shouldn’t have surprised me because I remember my first pair of apple wired EarPods when they changed the design, those literally made my ears blister and bleed. These fit exactly the same. Many people love that fit, though! If you’ve historically liked the pod design, these will likely be great for you. If you’re hoping for something different, go pro or another brand. I’m gifting to a family member who loves their wired earbuds and I think he’ll love these. I upgraded myself to the pros with the customizable fit, way better for me personally.

title: 100% honest review
body: Taking the Apple AirPods at face value, they're a solid headset for the money! If you already have the original, first generation AirPods and just want to upgrade, not a bad option either. The 2nd generation AirPods feature an improved software chip & runs off of BlueTooth 5.0 vs 4.1 (Gen 1). The software chip allows a faster, stronger, and more stable connection with your device(s). This particular item for $139.00 does NOT feature the wireless charging case. This one must be plugged in via USB (cable provided in the box) to an outlet or USB port. The wireless charging case model is $169. Now if you're looking to upgrade to the wireless charging case later on, you can. Apple does sell it separately. If you want the wireless charging option right out of the box, I recommend sending the $169 vs the $139 product here.Sound quality: Straight out of the box, sound is pretty solid. No crackles, static, pops, or any abnormal noises created by the more economic-friendly headsets. You have a lot more adjustability & tuning capabilities if paired on an iOS/Apple device vs using for example paired with an Android OS device. So, ultimately Apple users may or may not get a better sound quality experience.Noise cancellation: AirPods do fit fairly well in the ear without having to be jammed inside your ear canal. Generally they create a snug fit using Apple's "universal" earphone design, it is one size fits most not one size fits all. Some users have upgraded or opted to spend the additional funds on a set of AirPods Pro due to the rubber tips for a better/snug fit. I believe only the AirPods Pro currently has an active noise cancellation utilizing two microphones but I would double-check Apple for accurate information. Also, for Android users. The Galaxy buds by Samsung feature active noise cancellation & wireless charging case on every model so you might want to look into that product before making a final purchase.Battery life: For optimal battery life, I highly recommend charging both AirPods inside the case. The case itself is a battery pack with a life. So if one or both AirPods are low on battery, you can toss them in the case and charge them up. Apple I believe rates 5 minutes of charging time is equivalent to 1 hour of talk/listening time and 15 minutes of charging time is equivalent to 2 hours of talk/listening. I'm sure this is accurate when they're brand new but I'm sure like anything, normal ware & tare might diminish that over time.Warranty: Apple provides a 1-year standard manufacturer's warranty on this product. This ONLY covers defects/defaults from the manufacturer. This will NOT cover physical and/or liquid damages to AirPods, case, or charging cable included.AppleCare: It's $29.00 and 2 years and well worth the extra money. You're already spending this kind of money on a headset, may as well have protection. We get it, accidents happen! But protect yourself and get your AirPods repaired/replaced at no additional cost to YOU! There is no additional fees or deductibles involved.AppleCare tips:You don't have to install AppleCare right away. You have 60 days from the original purchase date to contact Apple by providing your AirPods serial number (on outside of the box) and Apple will tell you if you're still eligible.You can renew AppleCare for another 2 years for the same fee of $29.00 provided you want to keep the same product. If you decide to upgrade to the AirPods Pro or whatever the latest model is, you would have to purchase AppleCare for that specific product.You can go into an Apple store location and get live repair/replacement on your AirPods.Using AirPods with Android:Apple has allowed Android and other non-iOS devices to use AirPods since the AirPods pair/connect via BlueTooth.If you're using AirPods on an Android platform such as Samsung or LG for example there's 2 ways to pair the headset to your device. You either turn on BlueTooth on your device, pop both AirPods inside your ear, find "AirPods" under available connections, and connect. Or you might have to pair manually using the button on the AirPods case to make available, pair, and connect.FAIR WARNING:You will NOT have access to all features that the AirPods have to offer on an Apple/iOS device. You will be limited on functions you can perform.I was able to find an app on Google play store for $1.99 called "AirPopUp". It is a paid application that unlock access to certain/more features of the AirPods similar to what an Apple/iOS device would be granted. Be aware that you will NOT have access nor the ability to have every single feature since you're not on Apple's network. There may be other apps (most likely paid applications) you'd have to find that may unlock more features such as: AirPods battery life percentage, case battery life percentage, programming LEFT & RIGHT AirPods for certain function(s), skipping/pausing/resetting songs, answer/decline/hang up phone calls, find my AirPods, and more.For Samsung users specifically, you might want to look into the Galaxy Buds. They're a little cheaper than AirPods, I believe Amazon has them for $109.99 + $22 3-Year insurance through Assurion that does cover physical/liquid/accidental damage just like AirPods.Galaxy Buds features:Made by SamsungAudio powered by AKGRubber ear bud tipsPair with Samsung device to get the mostWireless charging caseActive noise cancellationComes in multiple colors (black/white/silver/yellow/red & black combo with Star Wars edition Galaxy Note 10+)Includes changing cord and replacement rubber earbud tips.I know if you pair the Galaxy Buds with a Samsung device it will automatically download an application titled "Galaxy Wearable". This allows users to check battery life status of the case and each bud, prompts you for software updates, ability to program LEFT & RIGHT earbuds to do certain function(s), answer/decline/hang up phone calls, pause/skip/reset songs.Apple may charge a lot of money for their products on accessories but they never fall short of being a revolutionary, well-designed, reliable product that works as advertised.

title: Compared to 1st Generation
body: This is the 2nd generation airpods List of changes: 1.  H1 chip (vs W1 chip) supporting hands-free "Hey Siri". No need to double tap on earbuds, just use voice controls. It's like a portable Amazon Echo, Google Home smart, Apple home pods device on your ears (but only for Apple devices).May feel a bit odd talking to your airpods in public, but as it catches on, people will just shrug it off especially in a city setting2. Better battery life. Up to 1 hour more talk time depending on volume setting and distance away from phone, apple deviceYou get around 4 hours talk time and approximately 6 hour music playback per full charge3. Faster device connection times. Not by much, maybe half a millisecond. Answer calls 2x faster than original (less lag when picking up calls)===Misc info/features:Audio remains the same as first generation. Sound quality very similar stock wired Apple earbuds. Audio very smooth, no lag or audio hiccups Has two microphones. Audio quality from recipient are similar to wired counterparts. Apple claim it helps filters out background noise, but does not work as well as other noise cancelling headsets. I personally can't tell difference from wired ear pods. Unlike wired, you don't have to lift/hold up mic to your mouth when other party can't hear you which is a plus.Fit. Varies by person due to ear shape. Slightly tighter/snug fit than wired counterpart. If those don't fall off on their own (without tension/tug on wires) when out-and-about and doing physical activities then these won't either. Built-in accelerometers and optical sensors. Able to pause audio by tapping on earbuds or double tap for Siri. Again able to use voice controls by saying "Hey Siri" without tapping. Detects in-ear placement, which enables automatic pausing when one ear bud is taken out of the ears. Can resume with one ear piece on ear, but need to hit resume on Apple device.Compatibility. Works with any device that supports Bluetooth 4.0. Also works with Android, but won't have the seamless auto switching between devices via icloud/Apple ID. When using Android it acts like regular bluetooth headset. For the price, it's not worth it on Android unless you like the visual aesthetic, as there's better options out there. For Apple users with multiple Apple devices it very convenient; just set it up once via bluetooth/sync button on case, and don't worry about audio syncing across Apple devices. This feature is compatible with devices running iOS 12.2 or later, macOS 10.14.4 or later, or watchOS 5.2 or later.Optional wireless charging case can be purchased separately and is also compatible with 1st generation AirPods. Wirelessly charge via any Qi wireless charging pad, takes 1.5 longer for full charge compared to wired charging. This version charges via lighting cable. Takes around 3 hours for full charge.Very easy to drop wireless earbuds in case for storage or charging. Has a very satisfying click sound when closing lid.Airpods and charging case has own separate battery indicators viewable via Apple device. Case holds 4-5 charges for airpods when full. Has a find phone feature and vice versa. Airpods will make a sound at max volume and give approximate GPS location on app. No water resistance, no soft touch texture like on Apple pencil (feels like wired Apple earpods with cords cut out and longer/thicker stem).Conclusion Overall, wireless version of the stock wired Apple earbuds you get with your iphone with better features. Seamless audio pairing across all Apple devices with Apple ID registered, very convenient and life changing after you use it for a while. Not recommended for Andriod users unless you like how Apple Airpods looks. If you own first generation, not worth the upgrade unless you plan to use the hands free "Hey Siri" function often. Another option you can get is the Power Beats Pro which also have H1 chip and similar features. Additional features include around the ear design for better fit, sweat and water resistance, noise isolation, in-ear buds, better audio, and has multiple color options. Bad thing about Power Beats Pro is that it may not be as fashionable, has a huge case, has no wireless charging option and cost a bit more MSRP.

title: Almost seamless integration
body: I'm cheap. This was in large part why I resisted shelling out the money for these. Consequently, I tried several other Bluetooth earbuds, several on the decidedly cheap end of the scale. One of the problems I experience is finding earbuds that fit my ears. I have very small ear canals, so the earbuds that have the cone shape tip that fits into the ear canal either just won't fit, or are extremely painful after a short time. The only shape I've found that actually works for me are these, which I discovered with the first pair of wired Apple earbuds that came with a new iPhone several versions back.I included this picture, which shows the Airpods on the left and the much cheaper knock-off I tried. I thought I'd be okay with the shape of the cheap ones, but as you can see, they are actually much larger than the Airpods, so they were frequently falling out of my ears, especially when I was doing any physical activity.So, I bit the bullet and shelled out for the OGs. Here's my take:Pros:-Integration with other Apple products. I have an iPhone, iPad and Apple watch and these work quite seamlessly unlike any others I've tried. I put "almost" in the title because I'm sure it will be improved on in further versions, I'm just not sure how. Oh, and I've never used "Hey Siri" as much as I do with these. I love being able to adjust the volume (among other commands) hands free. True story: Yesterday I was riding a scooter home from work, and not wanting to remove either hand from the handlebars, I wanted to use Siri to increase the volume. There was enough ambient downtown noise that Siri was having trouble hearing me, so as I was tootling down the busy city street I was saying "Hey Siri" repeatedly at increasing volume, until I was shouting it. Not one of my finer moments...-The fit (see above)-Quick charging. I will have something to say about the battery life in Cons, but one saving grace is that they charge remarkably fast. I do a lot of work from my computer (like a lot of others during the time of COVID), so I appreciate being able to get a quick charge on them between meetings.-Sound quality. I am not an audiophile, so my opinion here is very pedestrian, but they seem to have a good dynamic range. I've been blown away by the bass response coming out of such a small thing.Cons:-They still are not very secure in my ears. I suppose that could be more my issue than the Airpods, but I did have to buy an aftermarket accessory kit that included the silicone tips with wings and a silicone lanyard. Sometimes I commute to work on an electric scooter, and if it weren't for those (particularly the lanyard) I would have dropped one of them in the street multiple times. Sometimes I walk over a grate in the street and have a brief moment of fear that one will pop out of my ear and through the grate. With my luck, it isn't so much an issue of "if", but "when". (Side Note: if Apple doesn't sell replacements individually, they should)-Battery life. I figure I get about 2 hours of active use, which is fine and I think what they advertise. The curious thing is that the two discharge at very different rates. I'm trying to keep track so I can take them into our local Apple store and ask about it, but it is odd, and I have seen other reviews that also noted that issue.Overall, I give them 2 thumbs up!

title: Great when you need to listen music and your environment
body: Let's first start off with noise cancellation: It's non-existent. You WILL hear everything going on around you, with turning the volume up just damaging your ears. This is due to the AirPod kind of laying inside your ear, which doesn't create a seal as in earbuds with silicone tips. This not only kills any noise cancellation, but will cause sound to bleed to the outside when loud enough (say around 50%).The issues above are also benefits in environments like offices or schools. This is thanks to the lack of noise cancellation. With music playing, talking to someone sounds just the same as without them. In addition, without a seal being created in your ear, they have to be louder than usual, which they certainly are (Not by a huge margin, but a noticeable one). Now for the stem of the AirPods, they aren't just for show. The microphones in the stem help make calls sound clear and usable.The shape of the AirPods are a hit-or-miss. They'll either fit you or they won't. The best way you could test this is by trying on some EarPods, as they are pretty much AirPods with wires (design-wise). For me, they fit my ears, but start to slip off on their own. They don't fall, but they give me the sensation that they will. You can fix this with some rubber hooks that go on the AirPods to hold them in place inside your ears (You'll need to remove them when putting them back in their case though).The battery life is...fine. It's not abysmal and will last you a good while, but it's not anything to write home about. At least the pop-up when opening the case will remind you of charging them (On an iPhone, I don't think it'll do this on Android). It charges via lightning, but there is a version available with a wireless charging case if you'd like.Sound quality is good, but not perfect. Bass is not it's strong point, and some instruments do get muffled out on some tracks. Vocals can be heard well (which helps out on calls) though. Disclaimer: I'm not an audiophile, just a guy that listens to music for hours.Initial pairing is relatively simple on an iPhone/Mac, just open them while being close to your intended device. The AirPods connect nearly instant when taking them out of the case. While I can't speak for Android or Windows devices, I would assume that they would pair the same way as any other devices, but don't take my word for it.Also, there is no official water/sweat resistance to them. I haven't used them in these environments, but have heard stories of them surviving washing machines (Which I will not be testing). Take that with a grain of salt though.Overall, they are great earphones for me, but make sure you know what you need of your earphones first. If you make a lot of calls and want to hear everything around you, these are the ones to go for.

title: I love these.
body: I like the fact that they fit perfect in my ear without me having to keep pushing them in. I bought the AirPod 3 first and they was hurting me so bad I had to keep jamming them in my ear to even hear. Gave them to my daughter. I got these, the 2s and these are great. I’ve had them in all day and have been dancing around and playing with the kids and they are still in my ear. I had forgot that they were which is scary bc I can easily lose them. When I purchased these they arrived half charged and the charging cord and case didn’t work at all so I had to send back and wait two days for the replacement pair. When I got the second pair, the cord didn’t work again however I had a few around the house to use. I couldn’t tell if the case was charging bc this model has no charging indicator unless it’s the light when you open the case. Anyhow, the only way that I was able to see if the case was charging was either one or both of the pods have to be inside the case. If both are in my ear I wouldn’t be able to see if it was charging so I would think that the case was broke and defective. I finally put both pods back in and the case showed up on my phone indicating that it was charging. I took one pod out and was still able to see it charging. When I took both buds out I could not tell if the case was even charging at all until I waited about 30 minutes put both pods back in then the indicator showed back up on my phone on full percent almost. That’s going to be annoying. So far so good on the battery life. I just got them today and have had them in since like 1:00. And they are not dead although I just had them in my ear while doing homework. Not listening to anything. The sound quality kind of sucks which I needed the noise cancellation due to me working at Amazon where it is very loud. It’s clear and good for anywhere besides a loud environment. I have not tried them at work yet but I had a knockoff brand that were louder than these so I know I’ll still have issues with the sound on these. I’ll get the pros for better sound later and give these ones to my son. Overall, I’m satisfied and I’m happy that I have these. Hopefully they perform well and lasts a while. I’ve heard others having there’s for more than three years. I recommend these if you have issues with ear comfort with other ones. These are comfortable. Love themUpdate… I did not like them after a couple days. They did start falling out with my oily ears. They wouldn’t stay in. I ordered the 2 generation pros and I’m Inlove and boy are they loud. Spend a few extra dollars and get the pros for better ear support and comfort. I’m never going back to anything else. My last review

title: Some Great Features
body: Ever since I switched from Android and PC, I've become Apple-centric. Since Apple products like Apple products, I decided to buy some Air Pods. Amazon's Prime Day price of $114 finally brought them down to what I considered a little bit better price, so I bought them.Pros:1. They connect very quickly with all my Apple devices.2. The sound quality is a few steps above some of the other ear buds I've had. The stereo is good, and while they don't have enough bass for me, they don't sound tinny either.3. They fit fairly comfortably in my ears, although the right pod fits better that the left one does. Maybe I have an oddly-shaped left ear!4. So far, neither pod has fallen out of my ears. However, I've worn them mainly in the house, so I don't know how it would be if I was running. That, I'll never know because I don't run anywhere.5. The case opens very easily, and the pods are fairly easy to remove. I really appreciate this because my Jabra case is so hard to open that I would often have to get my husband to do it for me.6. I love, love, love the fact that I can say "Hey Siri," and get so many things done. I was watching an Amazon Prime show on my iPad Pro the other day, and I told Siri to mute the volume, and she did it! However, when I said to un mute, that didn't happen. Maybe I need to figure out some other terminology for that. When I said to rewind a couple of seconds, it did that, too. This is a really great feature.Cons:1. Other than the price, the one thing that kept me from buying these was how silly they look with that long white stem protruding out the ear. One person said they look like a hair dryer, and they do. I have noticed that the Air Pods Pro have a much shorter stem, and thus they look more normal. I guess when the get down low enough in price, I'll probably buy them and give these to a friend or relative.2. They don't have noise cancellation, which I would really love to have. I guess I'll have to wait for the Pros for that.3. I didn't realize that they had wired and wireless charging versions, and I ended up with the wired ones. However, I bought a wireless charging case for less than $10 from Amazon Warehouse, so I'm going to see if that works.Overall, other than some of the cons I mentioned, I really do like these. They connect quickly, have a pretty good sound, and the hands-free Siri feature is great. However, I would say that if you can afford them, you should get the Air Pods Pro because they look better, and they have more features. If you can't, these would be a great stop-gap measure until the Pros go down in price.

title: A good choice but super EASY to lost
body: I have always hate Apple earphones since they used to be inferior sound quality and and not comfortable to wear. However, they changed all these stereotypes when they released Earpuds and then followed Onkyo, they offered TWS airpods. Good sound quality considering its price, CHEAP. Highly competitive product. As a tech enthuast. I have purcahsed quite a lot of all different earphones, the TWS type. I used to hate all wireless earphone but now they seems improved quite a lot. I have Sony WF-1000XM3, Master Dynamic MW07, countless all different Chinese brands model (One did impressed me but it did not last longer than 3 months and dead) plus 3 pairs of this Apple AirPods. As well as some Senn and Bose products. Thus, I think I am 100% qualified to say something related to a product.Pro:1. Very easy to use, swip, double click and you have full control. Very little learning cost. The easist one to use by far.2. Good sound quality. Apple used high efficiency code to encode and decode bluetooth signal, AAC. It performs just like a pair of wired earphone. I think they can compete with Senn CX300II or so. Great.3. Tiny case, premium look and just right material used.Con:1. You would be very very easy to left it somewhere. As I said, I have purchased 3 times of this exact same product from Amazon. Why? I lost it earlier last year, bought again in Black Friday and left somewhere a while befoe, it did not last more than a month. And now I am getting my latest 3rd pair. What a funny thing to mention. Apple should consider to provide insurance to this product. lol2. Poor noise cancellation function. I know it is only a passive NC earphone but Senn CX can do far better job than it. It just make no sense why Apple cannot improve it. Particualrly when I am also using a Sony ANC earphone. Hell and heaven difference.3. Short battery life. neither of the charging case or the earphone itself. They claims 4hours battery life but in real situation, you get up to 3hours which is far from enough. For music enthuast, you should consider to have at least 2 pairs of them, then you can switch when you have to charge.PLUS, Comparing all my other earphones. I think Apple is more like a good compromise combination, it excelled in some aspects like sound quality, signal but not that good in noise cancellation, or feel of this case(I prefer MW07 case much more than a plain plastic case made by Apple). But, for 139bucks, you really have an awesome deal.In a word, I recommend this product.

title: Fantastic earbuds, but underwhelming compared to 1st gen
body: Owned a pair of 1st gen Airpods and they were fantastic; I was blessed with compatible ear canals and all was good. That pair was stolen/misplaced, so I figured I'd just "future proof" and go with gen 2 when replacing them. Got my 2nd gen Airpods and they were also fantastic. If you're looking for a quality pair of BT earbuds that "just work" then I'd absolutely recommend either G1/G2 (assuming a typical wired pair of Apple earbuds fit you well).However, the changes between generations is incredibly minimal and if you aren't someone who cares/uses the quick charging, it's doubtful you'd notice a difference between the two. Sure a new chip and wireless charging are indeed improvements to specs, but they offer limited actual use-case improvements. I assumed that the battery must've been improved in the 2nd gen models, but it's literally the same exact battery: 398 mAh case battery & 93 milliwatt battery for the actual pods. I'm a longtime Apple user who grew to love their products, but within the last couple years I've branched out and started building PCs as a hobby and it all started, because I just got fed up with what the company has become in the Tim Cook era. It's why I wasn't surprised when I figured out gen2 was essentially a rehash. About a month ago, my late 2008 unibody Macbook (not pro) had finally been retired; after a solid 11 years of service that included upgrades w/ a SSD + 4x stock RAM I decided it was time for a new notebook not because of hardware performance, but actually due to Apple locking those older models out of OS/Safari updates. I took a look at some current model MacBook pros and it's so disappointing to see how far this company has fallen. I became an Apple user because at the time I knew I was getting top quality hardware that would justify the pricetag by providing longevity and overall value thanks to the ease of user-repairs. The hardware is still quality today, but the price premium is no longer justified as rather than paying for the option of future hardware upgrades/longevity, you're now paying a premium just because it's Apple. It's a slap in the face to consumers and a practice I can no longer support. I'll continue supporting innovative products like Airpods where there (wasn't) another market competitor, but I refuse to continue buying Apple because Apple.Tl;DR: Fantastic earbuds, but don't upgrade if you already have a pair of gen 1 pods.

title: Comfortable wireless earbuds, worth the price
body: These air pods have surprisingly good battery life and are really comfortable to wear. I primarily use my air pods during work. So probably 5-8 hours a work day. I use them intermittently due to having to interact with my coworkers. I charge them every time I have to give someone my attention. As a result of that I've never ran out of battery life in a single day. I also find them extremely comfortable, like I forget they are even there sometimes if I paused my audiobook. While I don't exercise with them, I haven't had one fall out yet during regular use. But I am cautious enough to not use them around water as I fear they might fall out and be damaged in such circumstances.Unfortunately, we are only allowed one earbud in while we work. Some areas of my workspace are loud due primarily to large AC units and conveyors converging in certain areas. Due to this, I do have one complaint about them. They don't cancel noise very well. I used to have another pair of wireless ear buds and they were much better at cancelling out sound, even with one ear free. As a result I am frequently having to pause my audiobook in these areas as I don't want to blow out one of my eardrums trying to overpower the external sounds with raising the volume of my device.As to regards about sound quality, I find them good for my purposes. Listening to podcasts and audiobooks in standard quality. I have decent headphones at home if I want an audiophile experience with high quality audio formats especially with music, games, and shows. But the air pods don't necessarily sound terrible. They are just a little annoying to pair between my iphone and pc back and forth. (Not a fan of Macs, sorry apple crowd)As I only use them while I work, I don't take calls, so I can't comment on the sound receiving end of the earbuds and their use as a headset.Overall I think they are pretty great product. I did receive the newest model, it was factory sealed and did not appear to have been used. I read a couple of bad reviews and I was particularly worried about receiving a previous model or returned item sold as new. Thankfully everything was fine.

title: Prefer to Beats Powerbeats Pro primarily for running
body: I wanted wireless headphones mostly for running, but also to use casually for conference calls and listening to music/podcasts. From the outset I assumed the Powerbeats would be the best for me and got those. While they are very good, it didn't blow me away. It wasn't so much better than the $30 bluetooth headphones I got 3 years ago. I also struggled to get a good fit in my one ear. What made me send it back though, was that it is just too bulky. The carrying case is huge and even the earphones without the case are big, and I didn't just want to put them in my denim pockets, as they have protrusions that might get damaged or break off. Bottom line, you can't just keep them in your pockets "just in case". Every time you want to take it somewhere you have to make space for it somewhere.So I got the Airpods. I was very skeptical that:1. They would fall out when running2. They would not survive the sweat I generate in the Atlanta summer during 13 miles jogsI watched some YouTube videos testing their water tolerance and I was surprised that people reported them fully functional after a wash/dry cycle (obviously not recommended). I pulled the trigger and bought it and am happy to report that they work perfectly for running. The sounds is good (not quite as great as the Powerbeats), and they don't fall out at all while running. I thought I'd might need an after market silicone casing or ear hook, but found I didn't need it.The main reason I removed 1 star is because they are very slippery. Even when the Airpods and your hands are dry, it's slippery and prone to falling when taking it out. When the Airpods and your hands are both wet and slippery, they become like a wet piece of soap, almost impossible to handle. Apple should make it a bit more grippy.Overall, great product and what really sealed the deal is the portability and the fact that I can carry the Airpods on me all the time.

title: Great deal love it ! But right AirPod didn’t charge
body: UPDATE, the right AirPod stopped charging continuously, frustrating for expensive device to have problems so quickly. But I still give 4 stars because Apple replaced them for free under my limited one year warranty. My advice is make sure you start your warranty when you receive them. Just incase you have problems occurring. I was happy Apple replaced them let’s see how long they last.I was hectic to order from past reviews people saying it’s fake, but do not worry about those reviews ! Amazon may make mistakes At times doesn’t mean they don’t hAve good customer service. or for bad customers reviews sometimes they can even lie, because 300k reviews with 4.9 rating means it’s pretty legit. All in end other buyers reviews could be from third party sellers, but if you buy shipped and sold from Amazon it’s 100 % authentic. I thought it was fake because it says made in Vietnam instead of China on AirPods case. but I chatted with apple they confirmed it’s 2nd generation and real. And it could be made in Vietnam just different distributors because AirPods are worldwide.Regardless Amazon offers 30 days back and apple itself has 1 year warranty so for ALL THE BAD REVIEWS. People have many solutions to fix the AirPods. They are just lazy to go out there way to apple store. They will replace natural problem within 1 year to AirPods. Anyways I will update as I use my AirPods for futures. but for now I love everything about my AirPods, I scooter and it’s super simple to use without wires, doesn’t get in the way. They don’t fall out at all. I prefer they over pros I don’t like buds although noise cancellation and transparency is awesome(if you don’t mind ear buds pros might be better)I listen to audible books and it’s perfect and even during work it’s very easy and portable. During dishes and cleaning it stays in my ear does not fall out easily at all! For the price amazon gives its a very great deal for $130!

title: Theres a reason why everyone loves them
body: I ordered these and a pair of Bultone ear buds in the same month. These are one of the best purchases I have ever made in terms of convenience, lifestyle enhancement, and general happiness.Pros:1. sound quality - perfect for music - great, even tone and quality bass, reliable never any static or wonky pitches 2. Bluetooth connection - só reliable and easy to connect. Once paired with a device it’s seamless; just pop them in your ears. Can stray a pretty good distance from your device and still have good reception.3. Call quality & Siri - call quality is awesome and works without the other party even noticing I’m on Bluetooth. Have used them for both business and personal calls in the car and at home for hour long conferences as well as quick hit calls. Never had a problem with people hearing me or hearing the other parties. Really love that Siri announces notifications when I’m using them.4. Personalization - Love that you can tweak the controls to your own preferences.ConsCharging - While charging the ear buds is super fast and easy I will say that the charging case doesn’t seem to hold a charge as long as the Bultone case does. The buds themselves also don’t hold a charge as long as my Bultone buds do.Fit - I have very small ears and these are comfortable but they fall out occasionally. I am not comfortable wearing them out and about in public after having lost one on several occasions when it just spontaneously popped out of my ear.Price - would be nice if these were closer to the 99.00 price point. Just feel the 150.00 price range they hover in is a tad high but I would buy them again for sure

title: Worth it!
body: At first I was skeptical in buying this off Amazon and thought I’d be getting a knock off but I can say I’m definitely impressed. I decided to buy 2 pairs of these, one for myself and one for my husband for a Father’s Day gift. Working from home, I was getting tired of wearing a headset taking calls and having zoom meetings. This has definitely been a game changer for me. I love that I am able to connect it to my phone, laptop, and car with Bluetooth. It’s great that when I want to figure out what my battery percentage is for both the charging case and AirPods, it’ll tell me on my phone just by opening the charging case and the percentage for both will appear on the bottom of my phone screen. You can also tell when your case needs to be charged because there will be an orange light and if it’s charged enough, the light will be green as I show in the pictures. Same with the headphones. If you place the AirPods into the charging cradle, there will be an orange light if the charge is low and a green light when they are fully charged. These are not something you can wear for an entire day because the inside of your ear can become sore so I wear them when it’s necessary during my day. I love that there’s a feature where if I do have my headphones on, Siri will read my messages to me and you can send a reply as well. Siri feature is definitely great for when I am in the middle of something. Battery life, these haven’t lasted me for an entire day. I also love that when the AirPods are about to die, there’s a DUN-DUN noise that plays to let you know put me on the chargerrrrr. Other than that, I’m satisfied with my purchase and my husband will love the surprise when he receives it.

title: A good but not perfect product
body: This product really gives a good first impression. - No trouble at all connecting like other Bluetooth devices where you kinda have to brace yourself to deal with trouble connecting - The battery life is great too: you can go for around 2 days without charging. - But the sound quality wasn’t like ~great. It’s just like the regular EarPods. No bass, no pop, just mediocre sound qualityBut the more you use it, you’ll find more trouble: especially with the auto-connect feature - I like to watch youtube on my iPad while I’m watching lectures or on a Zoom call. - And these Air Pods really struggle with, which devices am I gonna connect to. - It’s kinda annoying to have to manually switch between devices (and it lagged too) - I think this is kinda short-sighted of Apple since it’s pretty clear that their users are likely to have an ecosystem of Apple products already and like to use them simultaneously.2nd problem: you don’t know if it’s connected or not - This has happened a couple of times: when I have my laptop and iPad, both devices will show that the AirPods are connected. - But it’s a guessing game of which audio it’s gonna play. - So many times when my laptop just plays the audio out loud and I have no idea. - There’s no way for me to check if the audio if from my mac or is it from my earbuds.Overall: It's pretty affordable Apple wireless earbuds in comparison to other models. So if you're on a budget and dying to have decent wireless earbuds, go for it. I would not recommend it if you're looking for really good sound quality and noise-canceling earbuds.

title: EAR SPECIFIC and QUALITY
body: A FEW DAYS AFTER MY REVIEW...CHANGE THE 5 STAR TO 4: It’s stilly excellent. My friends can hear me very well and I, them. Their complain is there hearing a lot of background noise and apparently, I can do nothing to stop that. I thought I’d use them for conversation in store, but now, I hesitate to do so, because friends would be side tracked with all the sound around me. Still the best I could find and the best that is recommended by my friends. FIRST REVIEW WAS 5 start—as follows: Silly thought, right? Everything about these ear buds is what I hoped.....quality. How hard It is, purchasing anything these days and being anxious about the quality or lack there of, no matter what you pay for the product. (If this is confusing to you, please read the novel Atlas Shrugged by Ayn Rand.) The ear specific fit, sound quality, apperance.....all are wonderful. Ease of connecting to any of my devices....WONDERFUL. I cannot rate construction longevity nor battery life, because I have only had them for a few days, and before that, I had the plug in buds that I rarely used, because I kept sitting the phone down to use my hands in the kitchen, and dragging my phone off Of a surface by moving around. Trying to hear friends on their speakers, because they are busy, too became frustrating. This WAS the perfect answer. They hear me better and finally, I can hear them even when they turn their back to their phone. You will hear from me if they break down anytime soon.....promise. Since my iphone is the new SE2020, trouble shooting, if any problem arrises, will definitely be a breeze. I am so enjoying these airbuds. Voice activation helped type this review.

title: Great sound, but it can be too great of sound..
body: I have used the Airpods Generation 2 for approximately a year now and I overall really enjoy this product. It has great sound and it is portable! I can use my Airpods for multiple hours without having to charge them! They have a 24-hour battery life and the product comes with a USB cord for charging. The charging cord is the exact same as the iPhone charger. Another detail about the Airpods Generation 2 is that if you have an iPhone and it is unlocked and you open your Airpods case, your phone will tell you how much battery you have left! You can put a decorative case to express yourself! You can use Airpods to listen to any content on newer devices by connecting to Bluetooth. With Airpods, you can use them anywhere without the hassle of having tangled headphones. A fun feature is to use two sets of Airpods with a friend on one device! On the negative side, they could get lost very easily if you do not keep track of them since they are wireless. Something that I have noticed is if no music or sound is playing and a notification comes through, it is super loud and hurts your ears. I have tried keeping the volume low, but it doesn’t seem to matter. Since these earbuds are in your ears, it is super important to keep them clean to maximize their use. I would recommend Airpods Generation 2 because there are so many benefits for using it in everyday life or working out! As a student, I feel at ease using these because they truly are hassle free. They are trendy and in my opinion one of the best inventions of technology! I can’t believe I ever used earbuds with wires before!

title: When nothing else will do
body: I have tried several so called “alternatives” and none of them can deliver. Most cost around $100. Jlab, had horrible sound quality and volume levels while they were difficult pairing, and the touch sensor controls were more than confusing to use.Ankor, paired easily, but then again, sound quality was lacking. Then a couple others, but not even worth mentioning as they were just complete garbage.These airbuds paired within seconds to my iphone, then ipad and switching between Is as easy as it gets without all the bull****Sound quality is same as headphones normally included with your iphone. Only thing i haven't had success with, was making and receiving phone calls while they connected. I think you can only make an outgoing call using Siri. I dont like or trust siri, so i will have no choice but to use manual phone functions to make call, answer call and disconnect call. That wasn't the biggest issue for me but did keep me from giving a 5 star review. Oh and another good thing is that you can set different tap functions for each left and right. Tapping will forward or go back to previous songs, pause, play, siri connect, (annoying as siri dont always do what you ask). So i dont use siri for that reason mostly. Volume is only controlled manually on device, or again, by using siri. By the time i get siri to do what i want, its faster using regular functions. So i have forward song on one side, previous song on other side. What works for you is your choice. 5 stars when calls are easy to do and siri is more accurate with my requests.

title: Good device but they need to sit in your ear just right
body: Great device, but make sure it is fitting your ear correct or they will fall out. I haven't been able to narrow down exactly what it is, but every now and then one will fall out or slip to some crazy angle. I am super careful but it will get me sometime, I am near positive. I have seen it quite a few times on planes where people fall asleep and wake up to find their ear bud gone. It happens.For being a tiny little device, they seem to do pretty well on holding power. To mitigate any real possible problems, I use the charging case whenever I can. Go to lunch, charge them for 1/2 hour. Driving home from work, charge in the truck for that time while charging the case as well.Sound seems to be pretty good. I can jack my iPhone up quite a bit and not really hear any distortion.
