I use these with a MPow Bluetooth adapter and I get distortion there, but I have proven it is the MPow.Rarely do I use the Airpods with my phone in a noisy condition so i don't have much to say about noise cancellation. I also rarely use the touch features minus the "oops I just turned them off again" accidental.One thing that is a bit annoying is in regard to the charging case. I work in an industrial environment. Somehow, that case tends to find every single metal shaving known to man. Getting it away from the magnetic pull of the case can be a real pain in the butt. I use a tiny plastic screw driver to lightly scrap it away so I don't dig into the plastic. Depending on how much you have there, This can be tedious to get out.

title: Buyer Beware!  Read the Description Carefully!
body: These aren’t exactly “latest model,” using my definition of those words, or my 13 year old daughter’s definition. My daughter specifically requested the most up-to-date generation 2 AirPods on her Christmas wishlist. Shopping in a hurry, I saw “latest model” and a reference to 2nd generation and bought these for her. The actual AirPods themselves are gen 2. They do have a model number with a “2” in the second digit. However, THEY DO NOT HAVE THE EXPECTED WIRELESS CHARGING CASE! This is basically a gen 2 AirPod, in a gen 1 case. If you do a browser search on how to identity gen 1 from gen 2, you’ll quickly find an Apple support webpage listing the methods for identification. The case is one of those methods, along with model number etc. This explains why my daughter expected the “latest model” would include the wireless case. This feels like an intentional bait and switch by Apple. I do not think any of this is Amazon’s fault. I ordered a pair for my son online from a National Retailer. That one also said “latest model” and the same thing happened. So, if you are buying these for someone who specifically requested gen 2, don’t get this one. Read carefully and get the one that specifically mentions the wireless charging case. At this point my daughter is so disappointed with her gift, I am going to have to buy the wireless case separately, which in the end, is going to cost way more. Don’t make the mistake that I made. On a positive note, overall, these are great quality genuine Apple headphones!

title: I like them. I’m trash lowkey.
body: It’s been about three months since I’ve been using my AirPods and aside from memeing with them on the daily, I’ve enjoyed them a great deal. They work really well; the mic transmits my voice well when I call and the sound quality as well as latency is basically the same as using good quality wired earbuds (but much more comfortable in my opinion). They don’t fall out btw. One thing I’ve noticed is that the case and AirPods are prone to getting weird dirt particles attached to them (like fibers from my pockets end up in the case) and stains are a pain to clean. They are white after all soooo makes sense.Although the sound quality is really good; around this time I’ve started experiencing weird disconnects and lag. Like the signal is getting disrupted but it only really happens when I’m on break at work. At home or when I’m out and about nothing really happens except for a long delay before they sync with my phone. Battery life seems okay; but I think it’s bothersome how I basically have to always leave the case and pods on a charger or they die when they’re not in use. The case I kind of get, but I don’t know why the pods get drained when they’re not connected/playing. Overall I’m satisfied with my purchase; the slight cons don’t really change much for me since I mainly use them when I’m making commutes from home to work or from my room to the bathroom. If you’re an apple person or you wanna buy them just so you can be like “sorry; i didn’t catch that. airpods.” Then go for it. Pop off.

title: Airpods Regular vs. Airpods Pro
body: I bought the pro version three months ago and accidentally tossed them (I was drying them with tissues) so I went for the cheapest replacement (Airpods 2). Here are my thoughts as to which one to choose.If this is the only listening device you have and you care about music quality, then buy Airpods Pro (noticeably superior music quality that falls far below earphones such as Sennheiser HD599; the quality is also below that of Boss QC35II).If you use this to listen to audiobooks, or if you have better earphones for music at home, by all means go for the regular Airpods. Why? I personally find it far more comfortable. The regular airpods are like the earbuds that come with Iphones -- while not the most comfortable, wearing them for two hours is not a big problem. I found the pro version extremely uncomfortable for more than an hour. The noise cancellation is cool, very cool, but not essential for my usage.If you travel frequently on domestic/short flights, then airpods pro might be great for its noise cancellation feature. But bear in mind Pro airpods are not the most comfortable. Moreover, battery lasts about 5 hours so airpods won't be ideal for longhaul flights.What I like about both: if you have lots of Apple devices, switching across these devices is very easy (2-3 clicks/taps).So for this regular version, I rate:3/5 for music quality;4/5 for comfort;0 for noise cancellation.

title: Better Than I Expected!
body: I wasn't sure how much I would use or like the AirPods Gen 2 when I first purchased but have really been pleasantly surprised by how often I reach for these over my old trusty wired ear buds.Fit: I had no issues with fit; the Apple EarBuds always fit comfortably and snugly and these were no different. I would say though that if the EarBuds didn't fit then the AirPods probably won't fit either. I've seen some custom accessories to make more snug but imagine they wouldn't sit right in the charging case.Connectivity: Apple has really made the bluetooth connection seamless which was always a tedious process on other wireless ear buds I've used. I just pop them in my ear and within seconds I hear the chirp that they're connected - huge plus.Sound Quality: I would say the sound quality is better than good but not quite excellent. I think there is room for improvement in that regard. No interference or anything but the base isn't very deep.Battery Life: Again, I would say this is any area where it is better than good but not quite excellent. On a full charge the case and buds will get me through about a day and a half before recharging but that is with relatively light usage. It is convenient that they are recharged with a lightning port as I have them everywhere now (work, car, office).Overall, I would say I'm very happy with my purchase of the AiPods Gen 2.

title: First time Apple user
body: I have been using the airpods for about a month now, and I am a first time Apple product userI have purchased less expensive airpods/earbuds and these are the best by farShipping was fast, and packaging was in tactConnectivity: I have an android phone, and connecting the airpods to my nonapple product was super easySound: When making and receiving phone calls, the sound quality was good. Way better than the other 3 earbud brands I own. There was no choppiness, no computerized sound and the other party/myself did not sound like we were at a distance. Music and video sound was crispDistance: I live in an apartment. So I was able to leave my cell phone in room A, and go to rooms B and C without losing connection. I also was able to go outside without losing connectionBattery life: Kind of short. If you're on a 5+ hour road trip or flight, you'll have to recharge your pods. Overall, 5 hours is not badFit: They don't fall out of my ears. Also, It's more comfortable to use headphones for extended amounts of time. Only because there is no cushion on the airpodsThe charging dock for the airpods is small and compact. I usually keep it connected to my laptop to keep it chargedOverall the apple airpods are worth the price, and I do recommend this product

title: Best Bluetooth option for Apple devices, that I’ve found.
body: Pros: they fit comfortably in my ear, the sound is clear and not muffled, and they sync instantly/ with my iPhone AND STAY CONNECTED AS LONG AS IT’S IN MY EAR!! That is HUGE for me, and why I was willing to spend so much on these, considering how irritated I was with my old Bluetooth headset that kept disconnecting from my phone. I also LOVE the magnetic case they come in: it’s the perfect size to fit in my pocket or purse, taking up very little space but big enough that I haven’t managed to lose it yet... 🤣 the ear pieces are magnetically drawn into the case so they fit right every time, no chance for them to accidentally fall out or fail to charge, and the lid is magnetically closed, protecting the ear pieces. I also like that the charge for the case AND both ear pieces ALL show on my phone’s screen when it syncs, so I know when the case needs charging, as well. Last but not least, I like that I can wear one ear piece at a time, so I can swap sides as comfort/charge level dictates.Cons: they are made from a very hard material, as expected based on stock photos, which makes these uncomfortable for me to wear long term. This is mitigated by the fact that I only need to wear one at a time, as mentioned previously, so I can swap back and forth between sides, as I need to.

title: Solid purchase
body: I finally got tired of trying to find ear buds that could compare to Apple, and I decided to just spend the money and get Apple. I’m not sorry that I did, but I do wish that I had gotten the Pros. I had Apple phones for years & always promptly gave away the included wired earbuds that come with the phones. They never ever stay in my ear if I move my head at all. The reason I went ahead about bought these anyway is because I found that I could purchase a set of silicone hooks that slip over them to hold the bud in my ear. They are perfect with that little hook which reminds me of the build of my favorite Bose headphones. The hook is simple enough to put on, however…. I don’t love having to take them back off after each listen because they obviously won’t allow the bud to charge in the case. I knew that before I ordered and simply didn’t think it would be as annoying as it is. Bottom line, the ear buds themselves are awesome; best wireless listening experience I’ve ever owned & I am not at all sorry that I bought them. About a month after I got them, my daughter bought the Pros. I put them on one time & was immediately sorry that I hadn’t chosen to pony up the extra money for them. They are worth it & in my personal opinion, actually sound better due to the complete seal in the ear.

title: At first I really liked them but then I noticed some flaws
body: I’ll list a few things I think are Good, and What’s Not So Great.The Good: Ummm…Owning an iPhone and a Apple Watch I already have a few lightning cables lying around.Not So Great: The cost. They are not inexpensive. I think I got the cheapest pair and that comes with a wired charging case and really not that impressive of features or sound quality. I am not that thrilled with the fact that the right one went “out” on me. I had to google a remedy for that issue. I believe it involved cleaning them and re-pairing them with my devices. It just wasn’t what I expected from Apple. I’m not thrilled with the fit and comfort. I, honestly, have to CoNsTaNtLy mess with the fit so I can EVeN HeAr TheM. I find that to be pretty unacceptable. If they are pushed in to far, I can’t hear them at all. I need to position them just right (up, down, depth, and turned in my ear at the correct angle) so I can hear them. If I’m talking, chewing, or otherwise moving…forget about it, they’re as useless as my nipples. It’s stupid to have to keep messing with them so I can hear them. “Ain’t Nobody Got Time For That!” I’m kinda wishing I would have bought the Amazon Echo earbuds. Alexa is vastly superior to Siri and I’m hoping that the use ability and fit will be better.

title: Good product and worth the money - but there is better out there.
body: Android user here - Galaxy S9Complaint 1- is at the gym with a high concentrated amount of other Airpod users roaming about, that it seems to suffer from interference of some sort. Anywhere else where there might only be a few people also wearing airpods, it seems to be fine. But if you get too many in one place, I've noticed it skips, or seems to get interrupted by something. It's not the internet speed, or cell signal, I've gone earlier when less people, no problems.This might have something to do with Apple product on an Android device - As a coworker who visits the same gym, has no problem and he runs apple tech / phone.Complaint 2 - Being on android - you can't see remaining life on your airpods - So you have to just guess when you need to charge it. Not a big deal.. But is slightly annoying to not know whether or not you can go without charging. On apple devices you can easily see battery life remaining for it. - Confirmed by friend who has apple.Other then that - It's a surprisingly good. As a Non apple fanboy, it's good. Stays in your ear even while running, jogging or doing most gym exercises. Just.. $150 is a bit pricey for my liking.. but I'm getting good use out of them, so was an alright purchase in my books.

title: Great headphones, but wish I bought the AirPod Pro...
body: I finally convinced myself to buy a pair of AirPods. First off after buying these I wish I would have went ahead and bought the AirPod Pro for the active noise cancellation and sweat and water resistance, but other than that I am very satisfied with these headphones. I’ve had $30-$40 pairs of true wireless headphones and honestly these don’t sound much better, but other pairs I have purchased always had issues with connecting and dropping out and also never lasted more than 6 months for me. With AirPods, connecting is so seamless and simple and the quality and feel of the headphones are so great, it’s just another level of experience with these headphones. I’m very satisfied with my AirPods. I gave 4 stars for the sound quality because for me the price for the sound quality was just too high when I compare them to my $30 true wireless headphones from Soundpeats which sound great for me. I usually only wear headphones at the gym (but I might start wearing them more now) so again I wish I would’ve gone ahead and went for the AirPod Pro for another $100 for the active noise cancellation and sweat resistance, but for now I’m satisfied with these AirPods. I haven’t owned these for long, so I’ll see how long they last me and how they do over time.

title: I like them.
body: They sound great, are easy to charge and store, the batteries last a very long time, and the noise canceling feature works very well. They are really easy to connect to any device too. But they have a tendency to fall out of my ear, and that costs them a star. Maybe it is just my particular ears, but almost every time I use them, one manages to fall out and hit the ground at some point. Because of that, I have to be careful where I use them. Motorcycle rides and river activities are out of the question. Once one fell out while I was on the riding mower, and it took me about ten very stressful minutes to find it. I have since learned that it helps to wrap a bandanna around my head and ears when mowing. Unfortunately this isn't a perfect solution either, as the other day one still managed to fall out and hit the ground when mowing. Fortunately I found it right away that time.Thank goodness they certainly do seem to be very durable. My kid's Airpod Pros got washed and dried, and they still worked! I heard them clanking around in the drier for 30 minutes, but thought it was just a nickel or something.Anyway, for around the house or office they are great. But don't think you can trust them to go anywhere.

title: Airpod flex with convenience, good integration and a questionable price tag.
body: I got caught up with the hype/trend of the airpods. And there are good reason why it is popular. The first thing is the convenience of quickly and easily pairing with your phone or even computer. In terms of sound quality, the airpods doesn't sound at all different from the regular apple earpods. Essentially it is basically bluetooth earpods with the siri assistance built in. (both look the same/same size and everything). There is hardly any noise cancellation since its not that kind of headphone. The mic is okay but, I used it once and it sounds muddle when I tried it to record. One of the biggest plus using airpods is not having to deal with TANGLED WIRES. The airpods have great battery life and it charges really quick. I would say using airpods for around 4 hours leaves it around 70-80% on the airpods it self. Last thing to mention is the price tag, yes it is expensive but, If you're someone who wants no bells or whistle and want a good reliable product, and is okay with the price tag then go with this. Yes there are other headphones that offer more features and maybe even cheaper price. However, It is kind of hard to beat something this easy to use and part of the meme. So it is a great buy!

title: One Size Does Not Fit All
body: Given Apple’s price-quality continuum history, I had high expectations for this pricey set of earbuds. Out of the box and after fully charging, I was amazed at how fast they paired with my iPhone7. I have about 20 sets of earphones, ranging in price from the cheapo Monoprice to the Bose Sportsound. So, I put them in my ear canals and headed out for my 3-mile loop. I had expressed doubt that they would remain seated in my external auditory meatus while running. I had not gone 100m and the left one popped out and hit the pavement. This happened three more times before I was done. The right channel never moved. The sound quality was excellent. Not so great for excluding the ambient sound of passing cars. So, bottom line: great packaging. Long play life. Excellent sound in environments where ambient noise is around 80dB. If your ears are like mine, you’ll likely be picking one of them off the ground. The touch feature is great for taking phone calls. But, the Bose wins hands down for fit and exclusion of background noise. Plus, you have a fail-safe wire connecting both buds, a comforting thought when riding a bike. Who wants to retrieve a lost earbud when cranking along at 20mph?

title: Convenience and ease of use is amazing, fit and sound is EH
body: Pros:- I carry them everywhere in my jean's pocket because they are so small- They "just work" no lag or trying to get them to pair to bluetooth, they always connect and workNeutral:- Battery life is good for how small the buds are, but the case is great in that it has extra charge capabilityCons:- I can only wear them for 45mins tops before my ears start to hurt. Very uncomfortable any longer than that- Sounds quality has a lot to be desired. I cant tell if they are just bad or that the Beats PowerBeats Pro are amazing.Overall:You cant win. If you want longer lasting, more comfortable, amazing sound wireless buds, you need to get the PowerBeats Pro. But if you get those, you will NEVER bring them with you because the case is HUGE. I ended up buying both these airpods and Powerbeats Pro and couldnt be happier.Though I dislike the fit and sound, these are still 4 stars because for basic podcasts, quick music at the work desk, calls, and of course how small they are, it more than offsets the cons. But trust me, there is no way these could ever be rated 5 stars.

title: Works well with Android devices but have some issues
body: I wanted wireless headphones because I wear them while I exercise outdoors in the morning and while I clean the house etc and was tired of getting hung up on the cord. I bought these to use on my Galaxy S8 because there are no Android headphones on the market of this shape. I can't stand the type that push into your ear canal so I was happy to read that these would work with android. You don't get all the features with an android device obviously but that was of little concern to me. I don't need to tell Siri to turn up the volume or battery life to be displayed on my screen. The only issues I have are with the headphones themselves that have nothing to do with android usage. First, they are a bit uncomfortable for someone with smaller ears I guess. The hard plastic rubs on my inner ear with long usage. My husband doesn't have this problem with his. I am also have trouble with them cutting out a bit and sometimes going out of sync when I am using them to stream. It is worse when I put my phone in my pocket. I have read online that this is an issue with bluetooth so seems to be an issue with both apple and android devices.

title: Seamless integration with your Apple devices
body: The 2nd Gen AirPods sound crisp and clear. The bass is at the right level and doesn’t sound distorted at all like some earbuds. Where they really shine as some reviewers have pointed out is in their seamless integration with Apple products. Say you are using your iPhone and decide to do something on the iPad. By just picking up your iPad the AirPods will connect to it. All of this happens without you having to do anything besides picking up your device. It’s also easy to keep track of battery life. Opening the case displays a 3D image of pods and case with the battery status of each on your iPhone’s screen. If you happen to misplace your AirPods you can use the Find My app to locate them. You can play a sound on each pod if they’re outside the case but it isn’t that loud so I’m not sure how useful this would be in practice. One of things I like is that I can still hear my surroundings when they’re in. With the volume at the right level it’s possible to hold a conversation and listen to music too. So these aren’t noise cancelling. They’re a deal at $99. They’ve been out for a while but they’re still good.

title: Wish I had splurged for the Pros
body: They do get a little sore in my ears after a while. I wish the battery lasted just a smidge longer. I generally use them while doing things around the house, and they will last a few hours. The sound quality is very good. The input quality is better than the standard corded ear buds. It picks up my voice better. Noise cancellation is comparable to the standard ear buds.My biggest complaint is that these were not clean when I received them. I opted for the open box to get a discount on these. The description indicated that the box may have been opened and possibly damaged. However, it did not indicate that the item had been previously used. And especially considering the current pandemic situation, particular care should have been taken to ensure that the item had been cleaned. I spent about 20 minutes scraping ear wax out of the ear buds! Gross! And if you have had any Apple ear bud before, you know that it is very difficult to get into all of those tiny little crevices! I sincerely wish that Amazon had taken a little more care in the processing of returns such as this!

title: Sound good but are they worth the money?
body: These sound better than I thought they were gonna sound. They are not noise canceling, even with an ad on of earbuds that make them sounds terrific. These are made a little different than the wired ones. I can feel the difference with the rubber add-on makes it kind of uncomfortable but without them they don’t sound good. I’m more of a over the ear headset person where you can really hear the sound. And can’t really hear nothing around you unless there off. But they sound pretty good. cons: on the down side Battery dies fast if you are listening to music can’t even hang two hours, if you’re listening to music these will suck for talking time because the battery will die fast I mean extremely fast ! I might return For this price might buy the Bose Apple ones really long battery life if you’re listening to music. But they cost around 150 bucks! Don’t even waste your money on the new Apple pods Their loud as hell with a high-pitched mono sound for 250 bucks! The only good thing battery lasts a long time That’s great if you Don’t like sound quality. (Maybe death rock hate music!!??)

title: Great sound - buy the accessories for them!
body: The pluses: wow!... the battery life and then the cases can charge the pods too... I have yet to run out of power and I listen to headphones everywhere I go... the case is a little slippery if you have bad grip but I bought a cute case for the case and that solved the problem... Sound quality is better than expected and I marvelous... i love that you can use just one and it still sounds great...the noise cancelling is good but.... now the bad... I have tiny ears and they don’t stay in well... luckily they just slip a little at first so I can poke them back in... I have the same issue with the old corded iPhone headphones so I was expecting it... but These fit much better than the knockoffs (which I ended up returning)... If they fall to the ground they don’t break (yet)... but they do roll under inconvenient things so... if they don’t lock in your ears then I would suggest ordering the leash for them... I don’t notice the leash and am super happy it’s there when they fall out.. . The only thing I really think is a design flaw is the no volume control... you have to use the device.

title: Gosh they're comfortable...
body: I'm furious. I don't buy apple products. The last one I bought was a 2nd gen ipod. I gave in and bought these after trying many many many earbuds, from all across the price spectrum, and none of them worked for me. Which is why I'm furious to know that these were out there the whole time, and only my hatred of apple products kept me from enjoying them.Obviously there's no noise cancelation, but the fact that I can wear them for hours comfortably is fricking amazing. I'm a side sleeper and fell asleep with them in!I had recently tried the Samsung live buds and the Sony link buds, both hurt my ears so badly I had to return them. Went with the 2nd gen over 3rd or pro because it seemed others with small ears like mine agreed this was the best fit, and I couldn't agree more.Only reason for 4 instead of 5 stars is apple's obnoxious desire to make you buy into their proprietary system. I have an android and a pc and neither are changing anytime soon, which means a lot of the features that would make them a 5 star buy are not available to me.

title: So why only four stare?
body: Pros n ConsProsGreat battery life ( over 4 and a half hours average).When pods are almost out if power it gives a decending musical notation ( unlike others that scream BATTERY LOW in your ear while trying to go to sleep).Battery case gives two full charges before IT needs to recharge.Fits nicely in ear canal.Good sound quality.Cons..( getting picky here)Programming turns off when removing pod from ear ( ok..that's a plus). But it doesn't resume the program when replacing the pod. You must manually hit the start button again.Also when listening to a podcast and you wish to switch to another you cannot just push the button on the newly decided upon program. You must shut down the previous show and then pick the new one.( I told you this was picky...didn't I)Otherwise a great product that outshines any knockoffs. These are more expensive, but more enjoyable than buying eight cheapo lookalikes and getting frustrated at the inferiority.Great quality here and well worth it!!

title: My Choice in 2022!
body: Every ear bud I have tried to wiggle and shove in my ears popped right out. That includes a pair of Apple Air pod pros I received as a gift. The only ones that worked were the over the ear type and I could never really find a pair with good sound.Then the generous person that bought me the pros exchanged them for the Air Pod 3's. Like magic they sort of sat in my ears and did not come out! While I liked the 3's they became a bit uncomfortable after about 15 minutes or so. A friend suggested the 2s and that was the pair for me. They are smaller than the 3's and felt good no matter how long I wore them.Comparing the sound between the 2's vs 3's. The 3's had a fuller sound but the Air bud 2's still sound good for listening to music, and are loud enough to drown me out when I sing LOL! A couple of negatives negative is the longer stem but that's not a big deal but what is important for me is mot having a volume adjustment on the pods. Despite that I plan on getting a second pair when I find a deal on them.

title: Convenient and fast charging
body: The AirPods are about what you would expect from Apple - spendy, clever, and extremely functional. Sturdy, comfortable, and convenient wireless abilities allow alot of movement whether working out, skating, or mowing the lawn. They have never fallen out of my ears. While there is no real noise cancellation function (or, at least not that I have noticed), and sound quality is pretty good. Where this product really shines, is in the battery charge/recharge ability.After a year: These are still awesome. There has not been a noticeable decline in charge. They still last about 2 hours between charges. The case only has to be charged about every two days.Having tried the air-pod pros, I think these are still more comfortable.Sound quality is still good to excellent.Mic quality is pretty good.Only real drawback is the case has a tendency to collect lint and stuff in the cracks and edges from being in your pocket.Totally still recommend.

title: Good...BUT DEF JUST GET THE PRO VERSION!!!
body: To these work well and I like that you can hang up/answer a call by double tapping. The new pro airpods don't have that feature (or I'm just not knowledgeable of such ....as I am not tech savvy). I orders this pair about a week and half ago to avoid touching my phone to my face for constant contamination (work as PCP NP & if you want a hygiene clue... cell phones one of dirtiest items we use daily!!!! So COVID applicable.However, they were killing my ears after wearing a short (I am a small person to note); and noise cancellation was like 0…That is a big deal to me. I went ahead and took the leap for the new pro pair & OMG AMAZING. I HONESTLY DIDN'T KNOW HOW GREAT TECH CAN BE. It literally cuts out sound by at least 80-90% in noise cancellation mode, and with easy tap,....off mode or transparency mode.JUST GET THE NEW AIRPOD PRO!!!!! They are comfortable, yes expensive... almost ridiculous but since using I don't regret buying at all... Now I just have this left over set....

title: Great buds for the price; I like them better than the Pros!
body: Ok there not Hi-fi, but there pretty darn good! I initially bought the AirPods Pro for the noise canceling (I fly a lot)! However, after using them after several flights, I hated the white noise they produced, as well as, the fit just did not work for me. I tried several ear tips but they kept falling out. That being said the AirPods 2 are great for my ears (no white noise) yes they don’t have active noise canceling but I think the sound great for the price. They also fit my ears perfectly. Reason for four stars is that as an Apple product who owns beats I expected better highs and mid-tones. They do have pretty good base for the size of the ear pod and the sound is not blown out. Unlike the beats pro. I also got Apple care via Apple (recommendation: use amazon to buy Apple care, you’ll save yourself a headache trying to get apple to verify your purchase it was about a half hour of work to get apple to override the system and apply the Apple care. But they were friendly and did Got it done.)

title: Worth the name brand, in this case
body: Extraordinarily comfortable, since they rest in your ears rather than stick inside them like other true wireless earbuds with rubber tips.Super easy to pair across all devices, including Android phones and Windows PCs. Other premium brands seem to only focus on pairing with smartphones and have spotty pairing performance with other devices like laptops.The sound quality is very good, better than anything I've tried except, of course, for full over-ear headphones. These even sound better than other premium brand's true wireless earbuds that cost $100 more and have better on-paper specs. (Looking at you, Sony WF-1000XM3s.)The only downside is the microphone performance on phone calls is very much hit or miss. I often have to turn off bluetooth and hold the phone up to my ear old-fashion-style. Not because my sound quality is bad, but because the caller on the other end can't hear me clearly. Disabling "HD audio: AAC" for the bluetooth connection may help.

title: A good baseline wireless earphone.
body: I recently bought these last month and after a month of usage I find this product to be very well designed. But I think personally there are still some design flaws.1. You can't wear it very long- After a certain point maybe after 2 hours, you ears start to hurt especially if your playing loud music or something.-The battery on these earphones aren't the best either 4 hours are the longest they've ever lasted after a full night's charge too 100%2. This is not an honest product- I live in New York City, a very loud place, so when I see noise canceling, I want to be able to not hear the noises on the train on my commutes to work, school and home. So in terms of noise canceling, I don't think this product is very honest.But otherwise the product is great, the sound quality is great (unless your on a call at the same time), the case doesn't need to be charged to often, it's very good if you need a quality wireless earphone.

title: AirPod 2 vs Jabra Actives
body: I have both the AirPods and the Jabra Elite Actives. I like them both but for different reasonsAirPods Pros- light,-comfortable,-Wonderful phone call performance (at both ends)- great charging case-5 hour battery life- open design allows you to hear traffic, conversations, etc (this is a negative in noisy environments)AirPod Cons:- will not stay in without earhooks- Not water/sweat proof.Jabra Pros:- great sound isolation (especially with Comfort tips)- better base than AirPods,- good battery life,-not as obtrusive as the q-tip AirPods-Much better water/sweat protectionJabra Cons:- the absolute worst customer service you will EVER find. Hope you will never have to use it. I had to return two before I got a pair that worked. THANK YOU AMAZON!If I had to buy just one, it would be the AirPods. But, the Jabras are better in the gym or in noisy environments.

title: Buyer Beware of Old Charging Case
body: I am not an Apple fanatic, but I have been using their devices since ios 3G. I do not “jump” at any product they sell, nor do I live for the next upgrade... heck, if it were up to Apple we would all give them our old devices and buy their next big thingy every 1.5 years. Having said that, I waited to get the AirPod 2 and was exited to see an Amazon “sale” of the “latest model”, that is until I received them and found that only the pods are the latest model... the charging case is the old case with the battery indicator light on the inside of the case. This detail could have easily been provided in the description of the item for the consumer to determine if that would be a deal breaker... but nope, buyer beware!!The air pods fit perfectly for me, and the sound is rich & full, so once my music starts playing I get over the fact that I was hood winked by seller!! The transaction was excellent with the exception of the non disclosure of the old charging case.

title: A bit hyped up
body: Here’s a pros and cons list;PROS* Answering calls and texts hands free is nice. As someone who works full time and always has full hands, this is really nice.* They’re small and there are no wires, so I don’t have to untangle or worry about wires getting messed up when I put these in my bag. I can also put them in my pocket.* The charging case is convenient. I like only having to charge the case every three or four days, and always having it on me in case my earbuds get low.CONS* They don’t fit in my ears. They constantly fall out, so I’m always readjusting. If I walk at a fast pace, it seems to speed up the process.* They’re not noise cancelling.* The sound quality is a bit weak. I have 8 dollar headphones that are better with sound quality.* They cut out sometimes for no reason and I can’t figure out why.* They aren’t very loud.I think that for $100, these could be much better.

title: Great Light Weight  Device, But.....
body: I bought these because I had trouble keeping similar wireless headphones in my ears while walking or exercising. Other wired or wireless headsets with interchangeable, difference sized soft ear pieces never fit right and had a tendency to come loose or slip out entirely. Very annoying at the gym in the middle of a class or on the treadmill. These are so light and hang on the ear that you don't notice they are there.Frankly I don't use them on an 'i' device but on an Android phone. That shouldn't matter. The only negative is that every once in a while they seem to go out of sync. This causes an echo effect. It becomes difficult to understand spoken word because of the echo. The only way I have found to fix it is to go to Bluetooth settings, disconnect, then reconnect the AirPoids. That means I have to stop my exercise program to restart them. Very annoying. The connected device used shouldn't matter if the Bluetooth is compatible.

title: Pretty Good -- But Get the AirPod Pros for Working
body: (Honest review: I paid full retail price and received nothing of value for this review.)I liked these a lot--until I tried the AirPod Pros.Sound quality is more than adequate, not the best, and convenience with iOS devices is great. I primarily bought these for use working on videoconferencing and telephone calls. They worked very well.For these purposes, however, get the Pro version. In addition to being more comfortable* and less prone to falling out than the AirPods, the sound is at least as good and the noise cancellation is awesome! It makes all the difference for working. After trying the Pro version, I gave away my AirPods.*Usually I find "intrusive" ear buds uncomfortable and expected that the AirPods would be more comfortable for me than the Pros. This is not what I found. The Pros are the most comfortable "intrusive" ear buds I have ever tried (including many premium manufacturers' offerings).

title: Definitely worth every penny and then some
body: I love my AirPods. I thought about getting the AirPods Pro but I didn’t wanna spend that much more just to have the noise canceling feature. Now I will probably upgrade in a year or two now that they’ve even improved the AirPods Pro even more.Coming from somebody who has an iPad and iPhone and an iMac at home having the AirPods and the ability to Switch between them so effortlessly without having to unpair and repair is so nice.I’ve had a number of Bluetooth headsets over the years from Plantronics to generic to a number of other different companies I don’t even know if I could remember every different manufacture that I’ve had. I always hated that they were bulky they never fit in my ear just right. I just never felt like they were great and it was always complicated they seem to fuss to pair up and especially if they ever unpaired then it was starting all over again. I love that the minute that I got my AirPods I flipped open the case and right away my iPhone registers that they are there and with the top of a Button on my phone and the top of the button on the AirPods in a couple of seconds I am paired up.I also have a very nice charger that I bought right here on Amazon for about $25 and it will charge my Apple Pencil my AirPods my Apple Watch and my iPhone all at the same time which is amazing and so nice that I can just click my AirPods case on there and they charge up in a very short time.First off I’m not a sound engineer or an audio file so I can’t say that they have the absolute best sound quality and I’m sure that they don’t I can’t imagine that if you went out and got some of the beads or some other type of wireless headset that fits over your entire ear with a bigger driver in it that of course it’s going to have better sound quality.But I love that they are tiny small and easy to just throw in the case. The battery life is great if my case is fully charged with the AirPods in and charged I can go to complete times to almost dead with both AirPods being used. If I only use one way talking on the phone I can go even more so in between case charging times is sometimes over a week which is absolutely amazing. The talk time on them is very long as well. I’ve never sat down and figured up exactly whether I get the exact time that Apple says.I love that the minute that I put them in my ear I get a nice little tone to let me know that they’ve connected if I’m listening to music and I take them out it automatically pauses. I love that it will even pause my music if I get an iMessage and it will read my message it will tell me who it’s from if they’re in my contact list if not it will read me the phone number and then read me the message and then asked me if I would like to respond all without touching my phone.I love Apple products even if they are a little more expensive in some areas but I love that for instance with the AirPods home they tie Effortlessly Into my phone my Mac and my iPad.I absolutely hate when I am talking on the phone for more than a couple of minutes to have to hold the phone. Also I run a business and so I have to multitask sometimes someone calls and I need to be able to pick up a remote to navigate to something to check I may need to grab the computer etc. and having the ability to have both hands free to do something like that while still talking and not worrying about the AirPods falling out of my ear is worth more than the money that I paid for them by far and then all the other features that come with it are great.So in the end I think that they are amazing and I’ve had it again a number of different Bluetooth headsets and they never fit right they fall out of my ear or the ones that took over my ears are always so uncomfortable after a short period of time that I never ended up using them I always ended up using my wired earbuds that come with my iPhone.

title: First pair was defective; second pair works well
body: Update: I exchanged the defective first pair for a new pair. The new pair paired with my phone quickly, and actually they have really good sound. They work well with my iPhone and if I have my iPad nearby they will pair to that easily.They seem to stay in my ear well, the sound is crisp and clear and the bass is even decent. I wanted an option to have earbuds that didn’t totally block out the outside world that way people won’t yell at me at home and I won’t hear them.This type of earbud that is ‘not isolating’ actually is easier to use for dictation and phone calls. The ones that seal tight in your ears are muddy and have skull resonance that makes it hard to make phone calls.I am not sure of the long-term durability yet, and I am really considering buying the AppleCare protection just in case this new pair has problems. I am upping the review to five stars.Original review: only two stars.Now that the price has come down, I decided to try a set of these 2nd gen. airpods. I got an authentic, new pair, they have nice finish and feel, and the pairing details shows I can buy the Applecare extended warranty, so I don't believe they are fake.I followed the setup instructions, but it only paired one earbud. The other was staticky and then had no audio. When I took out one earpod and put the other in, waited couple seconds, that one has audio. Then when put the second one in, there is only stereo audio for perhaps 5-8 seconds, then one ear gets staticky or drops signal, then just one earpod.I spent the next 30 or 45 minutes (or maybe an HOUR) 'forgetting' the earpods, resetting the case, re-pairing them, it has the same problems. I have multiple other bluetooth earbuds, headphones, etc., and have not had problems like this. I followed the instructions from Apple to the letter, and searched other websites for ideas.They just will not play both earbuds at the same time. The signal also loses connection and has signal interruptions, static, loss.When they do play together, the sound is very good to me, clear, with some bass, and they fit in my ears well. I wanted to try a pair of these so that I can hear ambient noise around me and people, other kinds I have seal tight and you can't hear anything around you (and also phone calls are harder to make since you can't hear your own voice as well.)I am going to order another set and see if that one will work properly. For $110, and the $30 extra, these Airpods should work perfectly and without all these issues.I have, for instance, 4 other sets of Taotronics earbuds and they work flawlessly and easily, and they were only $40 each. The Apple earbuds are triple that cost and can't do a basic pairing to my iPhone 13.If the second set doesn't work correctly, I'll just pass and go with a different company.

title: LOVED IT!!
body: I recently got my hands on the AirPods 1, and I must say, they have exceeded my expectations in every possible way. From the moment I opened the sleek white charging case, I knew I was in for a treat. These truly wireless earbuds have become an indispensable part of my daily routine, and here's why they deserve a solid 5-star rating.First and foremost, the sound quality of the AirPods 1 is exceptional. The audio is crisp, clear, and well-balanced across the entire frequency range. Whether I'm listening to my favorite tracks or engaging in video calls, the AirPods deliver an immersive and satisfying listening experience. The bass response is surprisingly punchy, and the vocals are rendered with impressive clarity.Pairing the AirPods with my devices is an absolute breeze. The seamless integration with my iPhone, iPad, and MacBook is something to marvel at. The moment I open the case, they automatically connect to my device, allowing me to dive straight into my audio without any hassle. The connection is stable and reliable, ensuring uninterrupted playback even when I'm on the move.The design of the AirPods 1 is both elegant and practical. The sleek, minimalistic earbuds fit snugly in my ears, providing a comfortable and secure fit. I can wear them for hours without experiencing any discomfort. The lack of wires is liberating, allowing me to move freely without any tangles or restrictions. Additionally, the charging case is compact and pocket-friendly, making it convenient to carry the AirPods wherever I go.One of the standout features of the AirPods 1 is their battery life. The earbuds themselves can last for hours on a single charge, and the charging case provides multiple charges, extending the total usage time. This means I can enjoy my favorite music or podcasts throughout the day without worrying about running out of battery. When it's time to recharge, the Lightning connector ensures a quick and efficient charging experience.Lastly, the convenience of the AirPods 1 cannot be overstated. The built-in microphones allow for clear and hands-free calling, and the touch controls on the earbuds make it easy to adjust volume, skip tracks, and activate Siri without reaching for my phone. The smart sensors also automatically pause playback when I remove an earbud, which is a thoughtful feature that saves both battery life and playback progress.In conclusion, the AirPods 1 have proved to be a remarkable companion for my audio needs. With exceptional sound quality, effortless pairing, comfortable fit, and long-lasting battery life, they have truly earned their 5-star rating. If you're in search of a truly wireless listening experience that combines convenience, style, and superb audio quality, look no further than the AirPods 1.

title: These really made me wish I hadn't wasted time and money with other brands
body: THE SHORT REVIEW -If you are in the Apple universe, get these.Don't bother wasting your time and money getting non-Apple pods. I already did that for you.THE LONGER REVIEW -I've had three other brands - two of them, very reputable ones - and had issues with all of them, ranging from connectivity to sound quality. The biggest issue was with phone calls which sounded fine to me, but, judging from the complaints, not so much for those on the other end of the calls.Soooo, I finally bit the bullet on Prime Day and got real AirPods. The minute I put them in, I could see and hear that there was no comparison.WHY ARE THESE SO MUCH BETTER?— The sound quality is noticeably superior and connectivity is a dream.— The battery life is great and you can see how much power each pod has.— The case is the charger and it holds a charge for a very long time after it is unplugged. You can pop the AirPods into it and they recharge without having to be connected to anything.— You can use Apple's "Find My..." app to locate them if you misplace them. The Find My app locates the case, not the pods, so I now keep the case in my pocket or purse when wearing the pods. That way, when I take them out, I can put them in the case so that when I put the case down, it can be found wherever I've left it.— You can choose to have Siri announce incoming calls and ask whether or not you want to answer each one. Every time I get a call, I think about how much I love that feature.— Siri also reads to you the Messages that you get while wearing the pods.— Using AirPods is generally intuitive, but do read the instructions to know what and how to tap to activate different functions.One little glitch is that, sometimes when I'm listening to music through the AirPods, I'll say something to my husband and Siri will think I'm talking to her and butt in. It's a little annoying, but anyone who has a device with an "assistant" knows that that is part of the deal.I got this version because my son has had them since they debuted and loves them. I saw no reason to pay more for features I probably wouldn't use. I love them... and I mean LOVE them.I cannot say how they work with non-Apple products because I only have Apple.THE BOTTOM LINE:As I stated at the beginning of this review, get these. They have been worth every penny.Don't waste your time and money getting non-Apple pods. I already did that for you.

title: Pricey but great fit, and the sound is very very good
body: These are the Apple Airpods Gen-2, not wireless charging, and not the Pro (I hate the round rubber tips that everyone seems to love). First of all, these are very easy to use. The sound is fantastic. I suppose I am an audiophile since I love music... the sound is clear, balanced, and the bass is very present. The advantage is that the design of these airpods does not require you to jam rubber tips in your ears, and the quality of the sound is therefore dependent on the tips staying in place; which, most of the time they do not. If I have to keep reaching up to readjust the buds for a better sound, what happened to the hands-free experience? Also, I am not sure what some people do to get the Apple Airpods Gen-2 to fall out of their ears. I have literally tried to accomplish that and unless you go full-on metal head mode I don't think you can do it. So, let me make it easy for you, and do take my word... I have read reviews by people who were using, and listening to God knows what. Pros:Great sound, easy to use, nice size case... some tws earbuds have those cases that are so huge, YEESH! Also, very secure and comfortable fit. Cons: the Voice Memos app on the iPhone is affected... when you record with those on you will get a sound as though you were on the other side of a phone line and not in the same room. They are great for working out and listening to your music, podcasts, etc, also, only one gesture per earbud. I found the Ticpods and will try them today. They have the same Apple design... no crazy rubber tips. You get a lot more gestures, and if the sound is like that of Apple I will be returning my Apple airpods. Update, the sound of those pods was not in anyway bad, the fit was ok, slightly too large, and I prefer the opened balanced Apple sound. The tick pods gave me more of the Bose sound, it’s not as opened, it’s more compressed. Last... I don't care about noise cancelation... unless I am on a plane; and even then... I hate to purposefully deprive myself of one of my so important senses; it intuitively feels like a really bad idea. Here is an update A few months later: The Apple AirPods are simply a wonderful product. I know they’re pricey but they’re that good. The sound, the fit, the look, great

title: Excellent for calls on my Pixel 4
body: I love Apple's hardware/build quality, but I don't like their software / find their ecosystem restrictive, so I try to avoid them.But they've totally nailed it with the Airpods.Stop overthinking this purchase.I've always liked the Apple wired earbuds, and these fit the exact same.A friend had the Pros so I could try them too, but I didn't like the feeling of them being deeper in my ear for long periods of time. And oddly enough, noise cancelling makes me feel physically ill (which happens to a small % of people with devices that do active noise cancelling).I chose these because my priority was microphone quality to sound clear on calls, whether at home or walking + talking. I like to walk + talk, but using my Apple wired earbuds made me feel very encumbered and it picked up all sorts of random background/street noise.Based on reviews, I can't seem to find Bluetooth earbuds that score as well on calls as Airpods.Airpods connect quickly + reliably and music sounds solid, too.And their charging case just feels convenient/awesome too.I think Airpods 2 are the perfect everyday versatile Bluetooth earbuds for anyone, from work calls to personal use (e.g. podcasts/music).I wouldn't recommend them for running/working out since I'd be worried about them always falling out of my ears. In which case, I think there are plenty of ~$40 Bluetooth earbuds that work well for that (and you're less distraught if you lose them / they break).If you don't mind earbuds deeper in your ears for longer periods of time, Airpods Pro could be great for all uses, including exercise.Unfortunately having an Android phone limits Airpods functionality, but I recommend the AirBattery app so you can easily see their battery levels.If you're in the market for Bluetooth earbuds, and you're not an obsessive audiophile - do yourself a favor and follow the masses by buying some damn Airpods (or Pros)! I wasted a *ton* of time researching and trying to find good alternatives outside of Apple...and they just really don't seem to exist.

title: If you doubt their durability, don’t! 🌨❄️☃️
body: Storytime: this past winter while riding our quad through our back yard and around our property, I somehow dropped my white iPods out of my snowsuit into the WHITE snow! It was about two foot deep in the low spots and the plow had mountains of it everywhere else over our heads. Needless to say, my efforts to find them were unsuccessful and I was in tears because I literally wore them all the time ( obviously if I felt the need to even take them outside with me then I wore my iPods excessively) and to me it was not logical to spend that much money on earbuds. It was quite literally a splurge on myself that was not normally something I would ever do. So realizing they were gone when I used them daily reminded me how boring my life was before them. With them I was able to multitask and get my mundane daily chores, work tasks and also play mom and wife all while escaping into an audible book that made time pass so much more enjoyably. I know this may sound minuscule to some but when your life is the same day after day after day, they really did save me from some depressive moments by allowing me to be productive but also do something I enjoy, AND THEY WERE GONE WITH THE BLIZZARD! I cursed myself out and damned myself to the depths of hell for the rest of the day and even with all the heat I put myself under, the snow still stood strong when I awoke the next day and every day after that for the next 2 1/2 months. Eventually I forgot, kind of. I should say I moved on, lol.Then one day came the 3rd week in March and my oldest daughter came to visit me. Are you guessing what I’m going to tell you? Yeah! You’re right! There in my thawed spring ready driveway sat a lonely case with two iPods inside. And that isn’t the best part! They still had a 3/4 charge and both work perfectly to this day, 4 months later.So, my point to those who made it this far? (Nobody made it this far.) Buy them!! You do not need a fancy wireless charging case or the fancy $200 plus dollar newer iPods with the noise cancellation. These are not built to withstand Michigan winters, yet they do. Crazy but so amazing and funny.

title: Why have I waited this long?
body: I am an android user, and I generally stay away from Apple products due to their higher price, and inflexibility of integration with non apple products. My son had a pair of pods, which I tried with my Samsung Galaxy phone - when I saw how great they are, I had to purchase a pair for myself, and I have had it over over a month now.1) Pairing is effortless and quick2) Quality is amazing - crisp, clear3) Battery life has been great so far. I've been listening to audio books while walking for 2-3 hours at a time, and battery lasted more than that without needed to recharge in the case4) I don't feel that they are in my ears. They are so light and comfortable, that sometimes I have to touch my ear to make sure that they didn't fall off. Any other pods, my ear starts to feel an annoying dull pain after some time - but not with these. They've been in my ear for over 3 hours without any discomfort.5) Speaking of falling off - they won't. The design is fantastic, so unless you're flicking them off on purpose - they will stay in the ear. I had worked out with them (push ups, stomach crunches, etc.), and never had to adjust them.6) I am not looking for noise cancellation features with pods, because when I go for walks, I want to be able to hear ambiance noise for awareness. While they not intended to block noise, they do a good job in transmitting strong and clear sound. I often times have to reduce the volume to be able to still hear ambiance noise when outdoors. Next to a busy street, I do need to increase the volume, but that's expected.I don't use the gestures much, so can't comment on their responsiveness/usefulness, but I feel like a fool for wasting money on knock off brands for a third of the price, to see how terrible terrible their quality is in comparison (sound quality, pairing dependability, comfort). Also, this is not a wireless case, so you will need to charge with a cable.Overall, this is a solid piece of engineering that I would strongly recommend over any knockoff or other competitors.

title: If You Don't Dig In-Ear Silicone, These are Awesome
body: I'm the first to be an Apple Griever where applicable (f*** that iphone notch!), but these pods are phenomenal and I could not be happier with them. I'd go even as far to agree they're "magical".Remember when you were a kid with your snaz ipod/mp3 player and those ol' wired headphones? You would think, "golly gosh, how cool would it be if these headphones had NO CORDS and just somehow PLAYED JAMS in my DOME?"Well the future of my ruddy-cheeked boyhood dreams is here, and there is something undeniably neat about the buds vanishing on the sides of your ears without a detectable trace from a head swivel.All this can be said about any truly wireless buds though. What I personally like about these is what many don't prefer: their airy non-sealed fit.I've already got big noise-cancelling cans to put over the ears for hardcore music seshes and studio/field work. I wanted an alternative for biking, walking, and for just sort of existing with the addition of quality background music.These don't seal off your ability to hear like airpod pros and almost every other earbud on the market, so you could listen to tunes and also talk to friends simultaneously without the need of "passive mode".I also plan on using these for phone calls quite often, and if you've tried doing such with silicon in-ear buds it's like trying to talk to someone with your fingers plugged into your head (like you're talking underwater lol)This design does take away from bass and punchy sound capabilities of in-ear models, but that said sound quality is still terrific on these. Mids and Highs are especially nice and clear and there's still a good amount of fullness noticeably better than the traditional wired apple earpods.And the freaking case is absolute fidget-toy evocative of eMacs and white Macbooks remembered fondly.All said I am late but absolutely on the airpod bandwagon, and if you don't like the ear-plunger style of earphones save yourself some money and get these!

title: I wanted to dislike these, but I can't.
body: I didn't buy these from Amazon, but I use the listing for price matching, and know that my item is authentic.With that said, I think that the AirPods were some of Apple's best engineering work last year, and Gen 2 is the best yet.They connect to my iPhone XR instantly. I don't always see the charge status notification, but there has never been a delay in getting them to work. In use, I notice almost no lag, and I have never encountered dropped audio. They play nicely with my Apple Watch and my Lenovo Windows laptop as well.If you like your music loud, you won't like these; they have virtually no noise-cancelling and cannot get very loud. That said, sound is clear and high-fidelity.Battery life is excellent, especially for a product so small. I have had no trouble getting the 24 hours combined usage Apple suggested between charges, but I tend not to use my AirPods more than 2 hours per day. Using the ubiquitous Apple 5W 'iPhone' power adapter and the included Lightning cable, I get from maybe 10% on all devices to 100% in about 90 minutes of charging.With my usage, I end up needing to charge maybe once every week or two.I skipped on the Wireless Charging case because AirPower never reached production and I have my Qi charger set up just to make sure I don't fall asleep without charging my phone. The case is great, nonetheless. If you're clumsy like me, consider getting a case like the one in my picture.Honestly, I don't really like Apple products. For a time, I thought that included this product. One of the things I dislike most about this one is the fact that the batteries aren't replaceable, for all intents and purposes. Like I mentioned, audio quality isn't great, but it's good, and these AirPods are really good for making calls and dictation, however, and I will likely keep using them for that.That said, the fact that I'm writing such a positive review about these should indicate my satisfaction with it. And I would buy them again.

title: Why did I wait so long to buy these?
body: Disclosure- I didn't buy my airpods from Amazon- I went old school and went the brick and mortar route. I use wireless headphone daily. I ride by bike most mornings and use them through the day for music, streaming, podcasts etc. I resisted buying these because of the high price and fear that i would lose one falling out of my ear or simply misplacing them. I've been a long time use of the LG series and have used them for almost a decade. The hang around your neck and plug in your ears the traditional way. I'm still a big fan of LG earphone because of their cost and quality of sound. BUT tugging on the earpieces connected by a thin wire eventually causes one or the other to stop working after about a year. Which isn't that big a deal because you can usually find a pair for under $40 or so. But I finally broke down when the new gen of Airpods included Siri and all I can say is WHY did i wait so long. I LOVE the siri feature (so convenient to skip over ads on podcasts :) or switch podcasts or make a call etc) and they simply don't fall out of my ear (even when riding my bike). If you don't use Siri maybe no reason to go Gen 2 over Gen 1 (but you really should play around with Siri - its a great way to send a text while otherwise occupied - check weather- launch apps etc. ) The airpods of course pair seamlessly, are comfortable and easy to store and keep track of. And they pair easily with other non apple products (my TV, peloton cycle etc). They have become my go to headphone of choice. Once caveat. They are not noise cancelling. So on a plane they are adequate for short distance flights but can't compare to true noise cancelling headphones. BUT still the best purchase i've made in awhile. No regrets. If you can afford them and you're an iphone/apple user go ahead and treat yourself. You'll love them PS I didn't buy the wireless charging case since it seemed somewhat needless expenditure. I haven't had any problem keeping the case or the airpods charged.

title: AMAZING IS NOT A GREAT ENOUGH WORD
body: If you are leaning towards getting these then DO IT! I was hesitant because of how expensive a pair of wireless headphones are. What made me cave was ordering a "highly reviewed" set of knock off ones that were just horrible and couldnt connect to each other and when they finally did one kept cutting out. So I decided to get the real ones. I was seeing great reviews so I bought them and then it offered me Apple Care+ which I did purchase. Thats when I was getting really nervous because I was seeing all the bad reviews of people getting used ones and scratched ones. I even seen a review where someone said they couldn't connect them and only one worked, which was what I was afraid of. Well I looked on my amazon order after it had shipped and it gave me a serial number. I checked it with apple and it was indeed an apple product. Still a little worried at that point. I got home that day and opened the amazon box and was very happy that it was in a sealed package and very brand new. I was very hopeful since I bought the Apple Care+ there was no way it wasn't legit. I opened the package and flipped the lid open and they connected to my phone within less than 5 seconds and I was on my way. I din't have to charge them at all and they lasted all night til I went to bed. They cancel outside noises out the louder they are. They are very comfortable. The sound quality is amazing. You hear certain songs a little differently(in a better way). You hear parts of the song that weren't all that clear before. Its hard to explain what I mean and you'd have to get them to understand. I was never one to really like Apple. But now I have an iPhone XR, an iPad and now Airpods and I am so happy that I caved in and bought them. If I could give 10 stars I would. GET THESE AIRPODS! And just make sure you are buying from Apple and not a third party. If they offer you an Apple Care+ then it is legit from Apple. Sorry for the long review but you guys have to know!!

title: Hear here
body: This hear contraptions are white in color, this hear are put on your sound receptacles for listing to sounds. You could here better sounds when the volume is up and here less when volume down. Get your grandkids to beep bop and boop them to you multicellular device, but be careful because I here it gives you blueteeth when you connect without wires, oh it doesn’t contain wires except to charge and recharge and recharge and recharge ever time you need to use the air bud...I just loved that movie, the dog played basketballs and helped the team win, such a feel good movie. Oh I think you don’t need them fancy wires to charge your charge or recharge and recharge your air buds...the dog wore 23, and jumped and booped the balls with its nose. It uses a technology that was created in the mid 80’s to charge, its called Quiff, or Chi, or Tai-chi, but it’s spelled Qi (good luck trying to say it), however that there is a fancy case and it comes at a premium, I think the average cost of rent in low income places around town. Anywho. I see the guys and gals or guygals all over town wearing and harring these things, and just think they are lovely. You can have them in any color you want as long as its in white, this seems to be a identifier with Sir Issac Newton’s company. You would think with all those colors in the rainbow they would make snazzy fun colors that people could identify with.
